reviews.review_text: Peaceful campground on the waterfront, with amazing views. Large van/tent sites that are well sheltered and separated by established bush. Nice clean facilities.
Great park, love being so close to the water, my fur baby has the best time.  A fire by the water, with a view, with my furbaby, what more could I ask for?

Had a lovely stay with the family but unfortunately we were not aware that the cabin had no toilet which was very difficult on my 80 year old mother, also maybe some ramps and disable toilets would be great for our next visit

Our caravan spot was big and flat with alot of privacy. That big you can fit a caravan and car and tent on it. Toilets and showers were very clean and showers were hot. Such a beautiful spot right on the inlet
Fantastic caravan park. Roomy sites ..our sites were very private and sheltered. Lovely water views. Sites are all on the waters edge. Amenities great. Will come back again.
Nice place close to wilsons promontory. We went on the promontory tour and it was best.  Worth going.  Watch out for feral cats who strike and kill wildlife birds without warning.
Top spot, will be back. Clean amenties, great views, big spacious sites. No compliants, all good

Smooth and easy online reservation, with very quick check in. We were 3 pairs (6 people total). Since only 1 car is allowed per site, we booked 2 premium powered sites and 1 premium sea view site. My partner and I set up our tent at the sea view powered site.

What I liked: all amenities are within 50 to 100m. The toilets are well maintained and have hot showers. The laundry area is right next to them. There is also a spacious, well equipped pantry with a toaster, kettle, microwave, pots and pans, fridge, and even dishwashing liquid. Beside it is a TV or media room where you can relax and watch movies with friends. There is also a small general store at the front office, so check opening hours.

My partner and I stayed at the premium sea view powered site, one of the best campsites I have been to. Waking up by the sea and watching the sunrise from a cozy bed was chef’s kiss. Each site is spacious and bordered by bushes, which adds privacy. We bought firewood and brought our own contained fire pit, which is not allowed during summer, so check restrictions. We also went night fishing.

Overall, a very relaxing stay and we will definitely be back!

Great location with just a short drive to Wilsons Prom. The park has some great large beachfront sites with shrubs giving privacy from neighbours. The staff are friendly and welcoming. The amenities are clean & functional, plus you can have a fire in a contained fire bucket. We stayed on site 111 and would definitely recommend.
What a treasure of a caravan park, lovely big sites in natural settings, very peaceful, beautiful views and well maintained. We absolutely loved our stay and will be back.

" Had a wonderful stay on a powered site, beautiful caravan park! Neat & tidy. We were very happy with the site we were allocated - large and not under any trees for our 22ft caravan, like we asked for. We were surrounded by nice bushland . It was very relaxing and our cat Lily had a ball too- going for walks around our site on her harness and lead. Definitely one of our favourite caravan parks and we will be back to stay 😊"
Great cabin, perfect location. Hosts were lovely. A great value accomodation in a wonderful part of the world. High lú recommended
We stayed in a tiny house. Fabulous views and location. Super comfy, warm and clean. Everything you need. Great deck and own bbq, little kitchenette. Fantastic little spot. Highly recommend.
Great location for visiting Wilson’s Prom. Amenities clean and well maintained. Powered sites good size. Will definitely be back.
Simply Lovely
Good
Very family friendly and a close walk to the beach and not a far walk to the Jetty where you can see the sting rays up close.

Disappointing first time stay over the weekend here. Camp sites are tiny and cramped. Toilets and showers are limited and in pretty rough shape. Only 1 camp kitchen which is miles away from a lot of the sites makes cooking/cleaning difficult. The only saving grace was the water park which the kids enjoyed all be it very crowded during the day. For $180 a night per site I really expected more for a Big 4 park. The pedestrian key system is outdated and single car only policy makes setting up / packing up very challenging if you're in multiple cars.

Night maintenance staff were friendly and helpful.

We stayed as a family of six with two cars (no pets) during school holidays. While it’s clearly designed as a kid-focused park, we found it overwhelming and not very enjoyable as parents.

The splash park had very limited seating (only a handful of chairs a gew benches would've been great!) and no toilets or change rooms - you have to leave the spalsh park facility. It’s also separate from the pool, which made things difficult with multiple kids wanting to be in different areas. Having to take children across a cold, wet lawn just to use the bathroom was far from ideal. The pool itself was quite small and extremely crowded, and with only one toilet per gender nearby, it wasn’t the most comfortable setup.

There are strict rules around food and drinks in the pool area, which are heavily enforced by staff, making it hard to relax while supervising kids - think no snacks or a drink. Facilities were clean but dated and could use an upgrade.

The one-car-per-site rule (due to fire policy) was frustrating, especially given there were empty sites available. With a larger family, two cars were necessary, and having to unload kids in the rain at the gate wasn’t a great experience.

The park layout is quite compact with limited space to walk or relax. There aren’t open grassy areas to unwind, and overall it felt crowded and overstimulating, with a lot of rules to navigate.

If you’re after a place purely for kids and don’t mind a busy, rule-heavy environment, it might suit you. For us, it just wasn’t the right fit.
Fantastic holiday park for a young family with plenty of activities within the grounds. A stones throw away from main city centre as well.
Facilities are fantastic and the kids had an amazing time. Plenty of activities for the kids in the caravan park.
This place is an absolute beauty . Definitely recommend to those with children and the prices are very reasonable
Bit dusty

Great location & friendly helpful staff!
I give 4 stars, only because as a single person in a tent it was not relaxing at all. People coming at going from 7am-10:30pm and it was so noisy. This park is perfect for families with kids or a motorhome/ caravan up the back on the concrete slab area. Lots of kids!
I went to the office to get some wine (which was nice) and told the ladies why and they sympathised and offered to move me to the foreshore park instead where it was quieter but I was so exhausted and all setup so didn’t have the capacity to move again. The Equinox festival was on that weekend which was a nice surprise I loved it…
I was there to have work done on my car and Ubers were never available so spent double the amount on taxis. Maybe the park could have an arrangement with a local service at a discount. The amenities were clean, I didn’t go near the pool as I knew it’d be busy with kids. I’ll be back! but on a foreshore site.
Wonderful friendly reception. Excellent park, well facilitated. Had a great nights stay

We had the best time! The location is amazing, the caravan park is clean, the staff are super friendly and there’s soooooo much to do!
We can’t wait to go back again!
Super clean Room..Reception Staff extremely helpful and friendly…
Great stay but water pressure wasnt up to scratch with during the busy weekend
The park was amazing so good for kids and families,

We left our stay at the Big 4 in Inverloch with quite mixed feelings.

The facilities are a real highlight, the air-filled jump pad, pump track, playground, aquatic park, and indoor pool were all excellent and clearly well maintained.

Unfortunately, the accommodation didn’t match that standard, particularly given the price. We stayed in a two-bedroom cabin that showed clear signs of age and wear, along with some persistent odours. Space was also an issue, the main bedroom couldn’t accommodate a porta cot, and the second bedroom only just managed to fit one, which made things quite cramped.

The most frustrating part of our stay was the handling of the common room. On the first night, it was effectively taken over by a group of older travellers, making it difficult for others to use. While not ideal, this can happen in shared spaces. However, on the second night, our group, along with other guests, were watching the football when staff asked us to leave so they could set up a “movie night.” We were then told the same group from the previous night had booked the area.

There was no indication that the “common” area could be reserved in this way, and it ended up being unavailable to other guests for both nights of our stay. Given this was the only shared space of its kind available, this felt poorly managed and unfair, especially for something advertised as a shared facility.

Overall, while the facilities are great, the accommodation and management of shared spaces left us disappointed and didn’t reflect the premium price point.

Great place, especially for kids. So many activities. Love the cafe at the corner and the bar/food trucks next door at night. Grass site feels a little bit crammed on a busy weekend but can’t have it all!
Amazing place to camp
Great place for a relaxing weekend friendly staff great amenities
Thankyou for being super flexible re my site. And letting me change it on the day I arrived. I had the most peaceful stay Thankyou. The view of the water is to die for!!!
Great place - clean, modern cabin overlooking the water - quiet & peaceful. Wonderful views. Close to the Prom and its beautiful walks & scenery. Been before - will come again.
Absolutely beautifully located park. Friendly and helpful staff.  The sites are very generous, perfect for a visit to the Prom!
Awesome location with great views. Mel is so helpful and friendly thanks for a great stay.
Great spot on the beach, walk to duck Point.

The park is in a great location. We were on a site in our caravan,  right on the water, with great views.
The park grounds are well maintained and the staff are very happy to assist.
One amenity block needs updating, but is acceptable.
On our visit it was very peaceful.
This park has a natural bush setting, with lots of grass sites for tents. Staff are helpful and the amenities are well maintained, making for a very relaxing stay. Will definitely visit again!
Nice and peaceful place with friends and family
Would have given 5 stars but our campsite was right next to the playground so we got a bit sick of screaming teenagers at night.

I have always found Wilsons Promontory Holiday Park to be a beautiful and well-maintained destination. My partner and I have visited multiple times, and it has always been a place we’ve enjoyed, often recommending it to family and friends.

However, our most recent experience was very disappointing. Due to an unexpected late-night emergency, we were unable to stay for our booking and had to return home. We contacted management to request a voucher or the possibility of using the unused night at a later date, but unfortunately, this was declined. While I understand policies are in place, a lack of flexibility in situations beyond a guest’s control reflects poorly on customer service, especially considering the high demand and cost associated with staying at the park.

Additionally, when we returned the following day to collect belongings we had left behind, we discovered that our table and chairs were missing. We were advised that someone may have taken them. This was quite concerning, as we expected a higher level of care and security for guests’ property, particularly in a well-regarded holiday park.

It is disappointing because this location has always been a favourite of ours. Unfortunately, due to this experience, we will reconsider returning in the future. I hope management can take this feedback into account and improve both their flexibility in genuine emergency situations and their handling of guest property.

This Big 4 was fantastic with its powered sites facing the water.Always something to look at with an amazing view.
Couldn’t fault.Facing east in the morning was sensational.
Very limited WiFi. No dumping station.

Great caravan park in a superb location. Our site was right on the water so gave us uninterrupted views.

Good amenities. Overall would recommend and would return
Fantastic all round.
We had a lovely 3 night stay in Seaview Cottage, which is a little dated but has everything you need. Beautiful water views from the cabin! Only a short drive to Wilsons Prom.

Fantastic stay at Big4 Wilsons Promontory. Our tent site was spacious, well maintained and surrounded by beautiful bushland. Great facilities, peaceful atmosphere, and the perfect base to explore the Prom — we’ll definitely be back.

Everything was breath taking amazing, site, location, views, except condition of the standard cabins. Nauseating rotten smell, the place feels like a dump and was a big turn off. Come on Big4 you can surely upgrade these tiny cabins with only a few hundred dollars. Literally a one day job. The kitchen was literally falling apart and must been kept since 50s. If upgraded you guys have great potentials and returning customers.
Stayed for the Easter weekend! We loved it so much we rebooked for next Easter. Staff were lovely and always up for a chat! Put on a movie night on eater Sunday! Seeya in 2027!

I would love to hear a response from Big4 on this feedback. We have been coming to Yanakie Campground now Wilsons Prom Holiday Park for close to ten years now and love  this spot to spend several days with our friends. However disappointedly we are not going to continue  from now. There are many great things about this place but we are disappointed with campsites having gone up $15 per night in two years. When you add the additional cost of children it becomes very pricey. For many of us we are not seeing the benefits of this increase really as many of us don't utilise the new camp kitchen, we dont have water access on site so have to BYO or collect. The toilet block at the rear is mainly what we are paying for and this definitely needs some TLC. I've noticed for a couple of years that the bushes near the rear block need trimming for good access. This is the same for site 47 where overgrown vegetation made access difficult.  Most of us camp but for those that can't,  a cabin was a great alternative but $380 per night for a solo person is absolutely ridiculous and has caused several friends not to come now.  Big4 has its place in the market for those wanting all the bells and whistles but not only are we not seeing those but certainly our group don't need that and feel we have been priced out of a place we loved. Sadly we say goodbye to this place and Big4 loses a large group booking of people who behaved well and respected the park and other campers.

We stayed on a powered site one row back from the water front. Site was close to toilets, camp kitchen, and play ground. The site had bushes around it so there was some privacy from neighbouring sites and it felt generously sized. The smaller toilet block has been revamped since we visited last year and was lovely. It would be great to see the bigger toilet block get a facelift too in future. The coloring competition and movie on the big screen were a hit with the kids, and they enjoyed seeing the Easter Bunny bring by some chocolate. Staff seemed friendly when we interacted with them and we enjoyed our stay. We will be back.

Amazing park! We had a fabulous time camping over the Easter break.
Jess and the team  were so helpful and kind.
We will definitely be back next year.
Great park. Awesome location. Good family atmosphere.
Always love coming down...well done on the park...

Nice place!! Clean and tidy
Staff polite couldn’t fault it!!

As for the room we didn’t stay this time as we were in our caravan . Couldn’t be more happier with the location of the park and also what they have to offer . Bathrooms and camp kitchens very clean and not to mention how lovely the staff are 10/10 guys see you next year or a sneaky trip through the year .

Stayed in a powered site with a caravan. Spacious sites. Loved that it was dog friendly, lots of dogs around and all very well behaved. Hardly any barking heard throughout the park, and very clean. Toddler loved the beach and the playground. Will come back with the family next year!

Had a wonderful time in a cabin and our kids and grandkids camping for three nights. Fantastic service the staff were extremely helpful when we needed to change
Our booking and then again when we asked to change sites so our grandchildren could be closer! Views were amazing!!!

Update 2026. Still amazing site, love it here, sites mostly screenes from each other by bush so lovely and private. Used avan this time and loved it.
Amazing caravan park, we had a powered site and our tent so can't say much about the huts except to say most seem to be new and look nice from the outside.

The camping sites are all nice, all with power and a small premium group also include water (those ones are more likely to have a view of the prom)
Most sites are secluded with trees etc
between them and their neighbours.

Lastly, the location is great if you are visiting the prom or the sth gippsland area, lots to see here. Including a nice walk along the beach and an banksia forest right next to the park.

Great location. Corner Inlet less than a minute walk.
Nearest park to Wilson’s Prom entrance

The park itself is worth 5 stars, but I must take one off for the lack of noise/behaviour control. Both nights we were here, noise continued well past the 10pm quiet time. The Saturday night was the worst, with a very large group loudly celebrating a birthday late into the night with no respect for other campers. Sleep before midnight was impossible. The group next to us was also loud and smoked strong marijuana which was unpleasant. Noise control from management would have been appreciated.

The rest of our experience was great. The bathrooms are nicely done and the sites very spacious. We didn’t feel crammed in like the sites at Tidal River and except for the noise, are glad we chose this location instead.

Great location for camping, peaceful, clean & perfect for family/friends catchup & relax.

Good to develop a small area for night/astrophotography.
Great location! Well looked after Big4 park and lots do and see for the kids as well. We had a blast and will be back in the near future

Amazing helpful staff
Went beyond normal service to help us out when we had left laundry at the Big4 Port Welshpool
Beautiful spot, nice good size plots ( we have a 23ft van)so very user friendly
Amazing views and sunrise was spectacular

Great location- literally a minute walk to the beach.
Staff are friendly & helpful.
We stayed in the 2 bedroom ensuite cabin, it was very basic, basic was all we needed.
It’s a good place for kids- the indoor pool was a hit with the kids, there is a basketball court, scooter track, huge air pillow- bouncing area & the pedal karts.

I would definitely return to this park for the following reasons:
The cabin was spotlessly clean, bed was comfy & warm.
The staff are very friendly & accommodating
The water slides & heated pool are a big hit with both small & big kids
The whole park is well maintained & clean
Just a short walk to the beach & the main town. The local pub has good food & service & plenty of cafes.
Will return with our caravan next time.

A wonderful holiday and our powered c/van site was changed for us at our request upon arrival. Despite being busy nothing was too much. Facilities clean, activities for all, good security and close to everything. Thanks.

Best drive through sites we have ever seen. Friendly helpful staff added to the positive experience. Easy day trip to Phillip Island so 2 items ticked off our bucket list. Highly recommend this park. 10/10
Very comfortable for our short last minute stay

We had an amazing stay at this Big4 accommodation with our kids! The place was beautifully maintained, giving us such a sense of comfort.
We stayed in the 3 bedroom coastal villa.
It was just a short stroll to the beach, however there was no beach access from our cabin which we felt was little disappointing.
The indoor water play was perfect for those wet weather days. Staff were lovely and helpful.
Had a great time 🙂

We booked an ensuite site for our camper and it was perfect! A great sized ensuite provided the extra convenience while camping with our 3 kids. The park was well maintained and has great facilities for the whole family. Great location too, very close to the beach and a short walk to town. Highly recommend a stay at this holiday park!

Wonderful park especially for kids. I stayed here with my 2 year old and she absolutely loved the indoor water park. They also have a great playground, and bouncing pillow.
The staff were welcoming and helpful, location is great, just moments to the beach, short drive into town (though you could walk into town if you wanted to strech your legs)
On the night we stayed there was live music and food vans at The Sand Bar, which is literally a 1 minute walk from the park.
The cabin we stayed in was immaculate, a good size and comfy beds.
Highly recommend, we'll be back for sure!
Beautiful location. Very friendly staff. Amenities could be a little more spacious.

Great park, especially for the kids! They loved the splash park, playground, pump track and the jumping pillow!
Clean and tidy (dump point was very average with people not disposing correctly).
Only down fall is that there is no disabled amenities and we travelled with friends who required the amenities.
Staff were fantastic.park is great for kids.everything clean and tidy.
One of the best parks we have stayed at. Our drive through site was great. Stayed 5 nights. Will go back for sure

The staff, at both the original booking, then at the book in, were very polite friendly and helpful.
We had to , at the last moment we had to cancel two cabins in our group, the caring staff handled the refund swirly.
The cabins were very spacious and well equipped.  The recreation areas were amazing for the 6 children in our group, they were outside and active the whole time.  And the location is just so handy to everything.  Cannot recommend his park enough.
We had a lovely time at Inverloch, the campground was well located to the beach and nice stroll into town. The facilities and entertainment at the park were great.
Great park, amenities excellent , service top class.

We loved this dog friendly caravan park. Great alternative stay since Wilson’s Prom National Park does not allow dogs. Caravan park is about 30 min drive away to the main area of Wilson’s prom national park. Bathrooms & community kitchen is very clean, which is no surprise as they are cleaned 2 times a day. The surroundings around the caravan park are also stunning with amazing views especially during sunset. Duck Point Loop walk available to do and water near by is great for kayaking and canoeing.

A very stunning setting. Large spacious sites. $100 a night water view site spoiled a little by sunrise watchers with drones at 0700 almost on our site.  Ablution blocks very dated and in need of a good clean. Roof thick with cobwebs and mould. A let down for a big 4 caravan park. Walls  stained. Nice modern small camp kitchen and older basic smaller one. |The park extremely busy and surly must be taking in enough money to update those amenities. Little attention to detail by staff. |Loved the setting but disappointed  with amenities especially with $100 a night site.   Lift your game guys.
Breathtaking views but we were placed in an absolutely terrible camp-site. Ammenities are nice and well kempt. Staying at tidal River campground is much nicer.
Great place very peaceful our site was large even ground shaded just amazing the general store was fine had verity staff friendly amenities clean a little out dated over all very happy.

A great campsite just outside the Wilson’s Promontory National Park.  Staff at reception were very friendly and helpful when we couldn’t start our van (thank you again!).  Amenities were clean and tidy.  WiFi in the kitchen was good.  Right on the beach and a short drive into the national park for hikes! A great stay!
We were only at your park for four nights would have loved to stay longer, a great part of vic really relaxing, all of the sites were very large with trees for privacy

We camped at Wilsons Prom caravan park in Yanakie for six nights in March 2026. We absolutely loved it! The sites are large and grassy. Our site was against the back area which provided protection against wind. Corner Inlet is a fascinating water way. The tides are huge. Flat shallow swimming when the tide is in. As a bird watcher, there was a plenty to see. Dozens of black swans, flocks of white faced heron, spoon bills, ducks, pacific gulls and a large flock of plovers.
Facilities are excellent including a great camp kitchen. Only twenty minutes into the Prom. Highly recommend this camp ground. Oh, and our dog loved it!!
Nice quiet location.

Took our van down for the weekend, I live 20 minutes away and never knew what a fantastic campground this is. The sites were spacious , the amenities were clean. According to my little ones the only thing missing was one of those giant jumping pillows. Will definitely be back.
Great accomodation and atmosphere highly recommend and will dega be back.
Nice place

BIG4 Wilsons Prom Holiday Park is one of the best organised family camping parks near Wilsons Promontory. Located right on the peaceful shores of Corner Inlet, the park offers a relaxing natural environment with easy access to the famous Wilsons Prom National Park, only about a 30–35 minute drive away.  ￼

For families who enjoy camping with comfort, this park is exceptionally well set up. The camping sites are spacious and well organised, making it easy to park caravans, pitch tents, or set up camper trailers. Many powered and premium sites are positioned close to the waterfront, giving campers beautiful ocean and sunset views. The open layout of the park allows kids to move around safely while parents relax nearby.  ￼

One of the standout features of this holiday park is its modern and very clean amenities. The toilet and shower blocks are regularly maintained and feel almost new compared with many camping parks. Visitors often highlight how clean and comfortable the bathrooms are, which makes a huge difference during family camping trips. The park has multiple amenities blocks with toilets, hot showers, and laundry facilities so guests rarely have to wait long.  ￼

The park also has excellent communal facilities that make camping easy for families. The new camp kitchen is spacious and well equipped with cooking stations, fridges, BBQs, and plenty of seating, creating a friendly space where campers can cook, eat, and share stories after a day of exploring. There is also a playground for kids, basketball area, BBQ spots, and even a small kiosk for essential supplies.  ￼

Another big advantage is the location. The park has direct access to the beach and a nearby boat ramp, making it ideal for fishing, kayaking, or simple seaside walks. The area is quiet, scenic, and full of wildlife, giving campers the feeling of being close to nature while still enjoying modern facilities.  ￼

Overall, BIG4 Wilsons Prom Holiday Park is a fantastic family-friendly camping destination. With its organised camping sites, excellent facilities, and some of the cleanest and newest toilets you will find in a caravan park, it offers a comfortable and memorable camping experience near one of Victoria’s most beautiful national parks.
What a great place to stay, clean and tidy and well maintained. We will be back.
We enjoyed the place. Beautiful location, friendly customer service and clean cabins.

Great park in a great location. Beautiful park of Victoria. And when it’s not windy it’s so lovely to look at the water.  But it’s starting to get a bit expensive ($149 a night in 2027 for 2 on a waterfront site) when there isn’t much in terms of activities or things like internet and more food/groceries on offer.
We love it and will probably keep coming for a few more years but if we keep getting hikes in costs it might just become too expensive.
Sensational park. Our site was enormous. The view was incredible. We will be back again and again!

Overpriced, cramped, and poor management—avoid this park.

I would never stay here again and strongly recommend others look elsewhere. Our experience was disappointing from the moment we arrived until we left.

Terrible Check-in Experience: We arrived for a late check-in and were given no specific instructions on where our keys would be left. While we were trying to figure it out, our kids entered the park using the boom gate code.

Boom Gate Issues: We quickly discovered that the boom gate code only allows one entry; once the kids used it, we couldn't get our car in. When we asked the staff for help, they were incredibly rude and dismissive rather than being helpful.

Small & Overpriced: For the premium price we paid, the "2-bedroom cabin" was tiny and cramped. The entire park felt congested, with very little breathing room between sites or cabins.

Save your money and your sanity—stay somewhere that actually values its guests.

It's a shame this place now seems to be overrun by adolescents on electric scooters. It used to be a very family friendly park, when I came here two years ago. But I did not feel safe trying to teach my children to ride a bike with teenagers and adults flying along the roads on escooters, ebikes and hoverboards. Management need to crack down and police this as it ruins the feeling of safety.

They are not allowed according to their rules, but I never saw staff do anything about it and many of them were riding in and out of the park, past the management office. They also continued after dusk, some with no lights. So you really had to listen carefully for them when walking at night.

Other than that, the park is well run. Clean, and well maintained. Cabins and camp sites are pretty packed in but that's  normal with these holiday parks

Other guests were generally friendly and well behaved. A few campers up late chatting and laughing but no loud music and it didn't keep me awake. Dogs are allowed and dog owners all seemed to have their dogs on leash and there was never excessive barking or any issues.

Wifi was good. Hot water was hit and miss in our cabin and even the water didn't work sometimes. Maybe something to do with the place being fully booked? But everything else worked well.

I really hope they can do something to address the escooters as they had established itself as a very family friendly park safe for young children.
Great park but very expensive toilets were messy and rubbish bins were over flowing.
Park is good however amenties are in poor condition especially the showers. The water pressure was terrible resulting in water trickling out of the showerhead

Cabins were great. Floors not clean and showers had a bit of mould around the bottom. Otherwise cabins were very clean and had plenty of supplies (dishwashing liquid, washing powder, toilet paper and washes).
Towels and pool towels provided.  Beds are comfortable.

Cabin was really noisy as it was right next to the children's activities. Not relaxing at all. We booked for 2 adults, so it was strange to be put right next to a basketball court and bike ramp.

Dog friendly cabin with no grassed area, so had to walk to grassy communal areas each time she needed the toilet (not ideal in the rain at night).

Office staff were really lovely!

No point having Big 4 membership at all!

Location of the park is pretty good!

Maps supplied for dog friendly beaches is out of date (2024) so check online for accurate information.

We had a wonderful week long stay,
The kids adored the staff member Grace & we loved that there was always a staff member around.

The concreted drive thru sites were so great. Plenty of room and we especially loved the speed bumps on the road keeping people from driving fast!! Well done.

Awesome place for families with the absolutley amazing indoor water activities and so many other indoor and outdoor activities.
Close walking distance for access to beach.
Staff were very friendly , welcoming and helpful.
Security access system was excellent making the complex feel safe
We just stayed 5 nights at a powered site with our 21 month old and will be back! We had a wonderful stay. Everything is there for the kids and it’s close to town and very close to the beach.

We had a wonderful stay at this caravan park and would highly recommend it to families. The park was very clean, well-maintained, and had excellent amenities for kids, including a great playground and a swimming pool that they really enjoyed.

The location is perfect — the beach is only about a 5-minute walk, which made it easy for us to enjoy relaxing walks and the beautiful views. There are also plenty of restaurants and shops nearby, just around a 15-minute walk, which was very convenient.

Overall, it’s a fantastic place for a family holiday. The facilities, cleanliness, and location made our stay comfortable and enjoyable. We would definitely love to come back again.

Great caravan park. We stayed in a cabin, that was good, clean. The only thing was the carpet was due for a clean. The park has a great indoor pool and a water park for kids, one of the best. Close to a good beach. Helpfull staff.

Took our caravan there for four nights first timers. Well run park, great amenities especially if you have dogs or kids. We had a nice grassy spot next our friends which was a bonus. Definitely a quality caravan park which reflects in the cost per night but we got 20% discount for four nights. Very close to the beach and walking distance to the town which has a great pub on the corner . Great place to stay..
Great place to stay and staff could not be more helpful
Amazing staff and facilities, I would highly recommend bringing families here. Lots to do and see.
We love it here! It’s our third time around and have rebooked for next year. We love it!

Stayed 4 days 3 nights, don’t think I’m coming back to this one.
The site is a bit tight compared to others we have been.
Kitchen is too small, sinks and fridge are tiny, the fridge wasn’t even working for the first 2 nights and only has 2 electric stoves
Shower is clean but inconvenient, only has 3 hooks in it, no bench or rack for you to place your clean clothes.
No family shower, no good for family who has small kids.
Hot water in the shower is unstable, had to keep step in and out of I don’t burn myself
Playground is good.
Location is good, right next to the beach.
Lovely quiet park on edge of the water. Glad we decided to stay here and not at Tidal River. Staff were great.
Excellent Big4
Great little caravan park, not flashy but a nice place to stay, right on the waters edge, plenty of wildlife, a nice bush setting.
Great lication. Friendly staff. Clean amenities.

We truly enjoyed our 3 nights and 4 days at BIG4 Wilsons Promontory Holiday Park. Staying in the beach front cabin was such a beautiful and memorable experience — waking up each morning to a stunning sunrise right in front of us was simply unforgettable. There’s something incredibly peaceful about opening the door and being greeted by the sight of the ocean, hearing the gentle waves, and feeling the fresh coastal breeze on your face. 🌅

The cabin itself was clean, comfortable, and very well-maintained. It had everything we needed for a relaxing stay, and you can really tell that the park takes pride in keeping the accommodation tidy and welcoming. The bed was comfortable, the space felt cozy yet airy, and it truly felt like a home away from home.

We also loved the calm and laid-back atmosphere of the park. It’s the perfect place to slow down, unwind, and reconnect with nature. Watching the changing colours of the sky at sunrise and sunset, listening to the sounds of the sea, and simply enjoying the quiet surroundings made our stay extra special.

Overall, we absolutely loved the cabin and the beautiful beach front experience. It was a refreshing and peaceful getaway that we will always remember, and we would happily stay here again with the whole family. 🙏❤️
Dryers don't work I put my clothes in and they came out completely wet and the code on the door is a nuisance to have to remember all the time

The park was clean and there was lots of space. The playground was good for the children. However we got a cabin and it was not in very good condition. It was clean, but it definitely needs repairs as there was water damage, damaged architraves and skirting, The beds were comfortable. The sheets were clean and soft. The shower was good.

Lovely caravan park with large private ‘bays’. We had powered site 22 without water which had its own access to the beach. Lots of wildlife. Amenities were clean. Foster is the nearest town and dump point. Unfortunately the weather wasn’t the best whilst we were there so didn’t go into the National Park. Definitely recommend

Didn't give the rooms a star rating as we stayed in our caravan.
I absolutely loved this park, it was just perfect. All staff were very friendly.
Just a little shattered that we had to leave a day early as my husband got a phone call about a job he had quoted and had to start it the following morning.
I was hoping I could get a credit for that night, but buisness is buisness.
We have re booked to return in March,
Cannot wait to get back to this little piece of paradise.
I would give this park 10 out of 10 for location, facilities, and friendly staff
Thank you so much for having us. The facilities were good, the service great and the location was sensational.

Beautiful location with nice views. Stayed in the beach view cabins. Cabin was spacious and rooms were tidy. The group bathrooms were nice and tidy as well. Ammenities were great. Just felt 1 or 2 more activities for the kids would have been great. They were activities but something like a mini golf or table tennis would have kept the older kids busy. Overall wonderful place for a staycation.
We had an amazing time. The campsites are beautifully kept and offer a little privacy.  All the facilities were clean
Unfortunately was very windy whilst we were there

Everything was great apart from people constantly walking through our site to get from their Seaview sites to the amenities block (we were in 96). Grow some trees or give them a dedicated pathway.
Everything else wS great.

Stayed here 3 nights for discounted rate. Facilities working but out dated.. small kitchen with induction stove and not compatible kitchen ware. No internet except kitchen area.. we tried to do laundry and washer and dryer didn't  do a job lost 10 buck with no refund
Stunning,  but crazy work 38km easterly winds no shelter!

As far as location if one has a boat that would be an unreal destination, we went to enjoy tidal river and the 60 plus return drive was a bit of a bother for us mature people. The environment the kitchen the staff and everything else was immaculate and very amicable. At 45 dollars a day it was more than we expected to pay , but quite within the average price of caravan parks around. tidal river caravan park was 30 plus for us on the age pension and one would save on petrol and car wear and tear. it is worth to mention that management had a terrific special to pay for two days and stay one extra day for free. Very recommendable place and I will enquire about getting a message outlining the special so we can make use of them and recommend the specials to friends and relatives.i will be back  Thank to staff and management
Beautiful location and some very secluded and spacious sites. Amenities block was clean but a little outdated.

No rooms, camping......  Facilities need an upgrade. Two tiny kitchen sinks both in a corner.  Requires water minimising shower heads for water conservation. Location is fabulous. Victoria appears to be having a rabbit plague!!  Our water pump on our caravan decided to die and there's no water at many powered sites, beware, had to use a very small and crowded camp kitchen.
Quiet, not over crowded, spacious sites, great location for Prom day dripping, peaceful.

Loved staying here for our anniversary.  Although the amenities were absolutely amazing, and the staff super wonderful,  next time we will try to stay closer to tidal river, as we spent all our time there for hikes, swimming etc. A wonderful getaway nonetheless.  Highly recommend ❤️ 💙 💜

An easy drive ~30min drive into Tidal River. Although, due to patchy phone reception (Im oncthe Telstra network) from Fish Creek to Tidal River, I would recommend downloading off-line maps of the area to assist in navigating. There was no phone internet inside the cabin and patchy phone reception, some phone service near the main office.

The room was very comfortable for the price. It's not *fancy*, a few chips in the bathroom tiles, and the bathroom floor overflow drain is open to the outside so I imagine it would be quite cold in winter. But it was tidy, clean, the bed was comfy and the provided kitchenware was spotless.

Additionally, as someone with sensitive skin and eczema,  I very much appreciated that the shampoo/body wash did not contain SLS - honestly, I can't remember another time I have actually been able to use complimetary toiletries without my skin hating me for it.
We will absolutely be back again. I am glad we found this gem.

We have stayed here 3 times now, and we take our 2 dogs who really enjoy it too.
Nice mix of beach front spots, which are more open, or other sites back amongst the trees and more shelter. The service was friendly and helpful. Clean toilets and showers, well managed park with lots of facilities, playground etc.
We will be back!
Great 😊 lovely customer service, big tent site, quiet park. Thanks for a memorable trip!

Old ammenities, poor location if you want to spend time in the Prom and when we arrived after dark, the office was closed and our camp site had been swapped out to someone else without us being given information on a new site allocation. Disappointing.

What a great caravan park.The park itself  blends in beautiful with the surroundings around it. Kitchen and amenities were always nice and clean. Lot’s of other attractions around the park.  My wife and I enjoyed the Duck Point Track walk at the park. Gate way to Wilson’s Prom. What more can you ask for. Staff was very helpful and friendly.
Perfect camping spot for our family in our caravan, only downside was it didn’t have a family room to bath our baby and toddler.

Great location with picturesque views.

Quiet spot with camp sites or cabins to suit all travellers.  Park is very well kept and amenities are clean.  Perfect location for either R & R, or for exploring, fishing  or walking.

We just spent two lovely nights at Wilsons Prom Big 4 Caravan Park. The customer service provided at the front desk was fantastic and very helpful. We had a lovely site with surrounding shrubs giving us plenty of privacy and at night it was very peaceful with no noise. We would highly recommend this park.
We camped here on two occasions and loved it. The sites and amenities are clean and well maintained, its a quiet place and I would recommend it to anyone travelling to Wilsons Prom
Great location, big sites, wonderful staff and facilities
Was a relaxing base to explore the Prom & the bush campsites are spacious.

Our seaview site was private, spacious with great sea view. The amenities clean and well maintained. Location convenient to Wilson’s Prom.
Reception was welcoming.
Excellent Seaview site magic conditions. Very clean park.

We booked a beach view site back in August for mid Feb but only the week before.they called us and said they moved us to another spot which was behind the cabins! No explanations just refunded the difference of fees! Very rude very abrupt. The doors on the toilet blocks just slams shut and wakes everyone up. It only takes a little adjustment to fix that. This has been the case for two years which we have been coming here ! The campers just stay up till 2 pm and there is no monitoring. Amenities are so outdated and the maintenance is very average. Not coming back here again. The 2 stars is only for the view.

They gave away our spot that we booked 5 months before our arrival to someone else just because they booked for 30 days or so, very unhappy☹️ We  tried to make this an annual event but not sure any more.

Great for young families and great location so close to the water and short walk to town. The park has a good pool ( needs to be a little warmer) and great kids indoor water park. The pump track for scooters and bikes was excellent. Overall with some more upgrades to modernise facilities this will be a great destination. The sites are quite tight in some areas be sure to check your van size first. The newer drive through artificial grass powered sites were excellent.

We recently stayed at BIG4 Inverloch Holiday Park and unfortunately were quite disappointed with our experience.

Firstly, the staff were great  friendly and helpful throughout our stay  so no complaints there.

However, the two-bedroom cabin we booked was extremely small and felt very overpriced for what it was, especially for a family. The park itself also felt very cramped and overcrowded, which made it hard to relax and enjoy our time away.

When we tried to use the pool, there was a large group (around 15 people) playing games and kids screaming loudly. There didn’t seem to be much consideration for other guests, and it was unbearable to stay, so we left. The splash park was good and the kids enjoyed that.

We also tried the food truck on site and unfortunately found the food very basic and bland also the vending machine was not working so we couldn't get the kids a treat at the time.

Later that evening, a large group set up chairs right outside our cabin. With the thin cabin walls, we could hear everything, which made getting the kids to bed very difficult. Thankfully, they left around 10pm.

Overall, we regret booking here and won’t be staying again. It’s a shame because the staff were lovely, but the overcrowding, noise, and value for money really let the experience down.
My 1st visit, next time I would like to be at the foreshore sites.

Great big 4! Good location with a short walk to the beach and 5 min drive into main town. Lots of activities to keep the kids entertained Inc, jumping pillow, mini golf, pump track and indoor swimming pool and splash pad. Staff were very friendly.

The only negative feedback I have is that the floor in our cabin could have been mopped better, having bare feet our feet came up black. The cabin otherwise was pretty clean.
Caravan site, amenities close and well looked after.

Perfect location.
The room was very clean and had everything that we needed.
Comfy bed and nice, warm doona.
Definitely will return.
Powered tent site was perfect, big enough for everything, water park and pump track kept the kids busy all day, loved it here will be back soon

Super family friendly, majority of the non concrete sites were full of trees and beautiful. I wouldn’t want to camp on the concrete RV sites only as they were just slabs of concrete on top of each other. The rest of the park was beautiful
Great location and facilities were excellent. Just be prepared for a lot of kids around,which in turn was very loud at times.
Loved our accommodation and everything we could do in the park. Had a ball
We had a fabulous stay for 3 nights! Grassed site with ensuite. Lovely site, very clean and very welcoming. Easy walking to town. See you again next year! Thank you

We stayed in a tent and the stand and facilities were well maintained. My only complaint is the water play area, there is just too much water spraying everywhere, I feel more research is needed to make the experience better for all ages.
Great park well located|The staff were very welcoming and helpful at both check in and check out|Heaps of activities to utilize

Lovely friendly staff. Very clean amenities. So many fantastic activities for adults and kids. Hot spa was lovely.
Only lost one star for noise….. someone left a dog in their van barking while they went and socialised, had to ask people to not play basketball at 9pm because it was so noisy and a rooster crowing from 3am.

It was a beautiful park with lots of enjoyable activities. I loved the heated pool. The camp kitchens were very well equipped and maintained
The town also had an interesting history.

We had a great trip away as a family of three, lots to do within 15 minutes of the park. The park was excellent for kids, plenty to do! Whilst the park is older, it’s been well maintained and clean. It also looked spectacular with all of the beautiful autumn foliage around, and the backdrop was very scenic. The only downfall was that the shower in the ensuite for the camp site flooded a little, it was hard to keep the bathroom floor clean and dry. But a mop was provided, so we were able to clean up. Park owners were lovely and we had everything needed. Thanks again, we will be back! ☺️

We enjoyed a great few days with our grandkids. Lots of recreational pursuits available for the kids and a snug cabin for inside activities when the rain came. We also found plenty of options for day trips out and about in beautiful South Gippsland.

Best getaway place for family with kids. Friendly host. Facility is very clean, safe and totally kids friendly. They have everything within the park including heated pool, tennis, mini golf, playground, barbecue and a lot more.

Owners were lovely and the property was immaculate. The kids had a great time and being able to choose DVD's and games, along with borrowing swim goggles was a fantastic feature. We highly recommend staying here and we will be back.

We would like to thank Jenny & Marshal for turning what could have been a holiday from hell , ( when our Ute blew the clutch ) into a very enjoyable extended holiday. They went above and beyond to make our stay with two grand children an enjoyable experience. The park has everything for a great family holiday, and the service is top class.

Staff at reception were very welcoming and friendly. Close to Wilson's Promontory and beach areas if you want to hike or hit the beaches. Nice amenities but a bit of a wait when there are crowds for long weekends. They were kept clean and the floor mats for the showers were nice. Kitchen facility was great, large eating area and good space and set up for cooking.

Our first couple of nights it was very quiet and peaceful. Our last night was start of Easter long and school holidays and ended having people right beside us who played loud music, argued and quite unpleasant but can't fault the park for that.

Would stay again if in the area.

We had the best week at Toora. We camped in our camper and our friends were in cabins. |The kids could explore the park safely and us parents could relax. |Perfect size caravan park, the amenities were clean and everyone so friendly.
Great park. Very homely. Welcoming and a lot of care taken in supplying plenty of activities for the kids. Indoor heated pool and spa were also a huge hit.
Great park quite a lot of cabins indoor pool great alot of stuff to do in the area

We loved this park.  Quiet, secluded feel and sheltered from wind.  The amenities were good and location excellent - walking distance to the shops, lots to do and see around the district.  Only criticism was that one shower in the ladies block had a dodgy rose that sprayed out the top and over the divider.  It wet my clothes hanging the other side.  |Very pretty place!

Great caravan and camping park. Facilities were clean and good - and the owners are lovely people. Close to the shops and cafes (an easy walk) and less than 750 metres from the Toora railway station trailhead for the Great Southern Rail Trail.

Excellent caravan park. Close to Southern Rail Trail. Heated indoor pool and spa. Clean amenities. Great camp kitchen and games room.
Easy walk to restaurants and shops.

Completely overpriced because of a long weekend and 3 night minimum. $92 per night. Just $ gouging for a one day public holiday. Seriously in need of a maintenance man and a good cleaner. Everything is either broken or in need of repair and a good clean.
2 Hills hoists hardly useable because of broken, stretched and twisted wire lines and peg baskets full of tree leaves. Sardine City cramped sites into the deal. In all honesty, cannot recommend this park

This is our second March long weekend in a row at this hidden gem of a park. The camp spots are well maintained, our ensuite was clean and you can tell the owners take pride in their park. So much to do for the kids - indoor pool, mini golf, games and space to walk & have adventures. It's a short walk to the town (which unfortunately isn't busy), local playground and the southern rail trail.

Toora is a great home base to travel around and explore the Southern Gippsland region without needing to worry about a huge number of people getting in your way - check out this great little independent park without making a huge hole in your wallet, it's worth it.

second time stay. |Very comfortable bed, the sheets and Doona felt so fresh and great pillows.|Spacious fridge and freezer for our needs for our 5 days|Very clean. |Owners and staff very helpful and friendly. |The pool is a great size for swimming. |So many activities for children. |We will be back again definitely. |Thank you for having us.  Xxx

Beautiful campsite very quiet and very well maintained. Kitchen, bathroom, swimming pool, spa, tennis, mini golf all impeccable and free (by paying for the night). I highly recommend and congratulate the managers ! 😊😊

Toora Tourist Park is a great spot for adults and children with so many facities for the little ones to keep themselves occupied.  Great outdoor facitilies set in very well cared for grounds. ||Plenty of spots for camping, campervans,  and several with ensuite facilities.|  |We stayed in a two bedroom cabin which catered to all our requirements and a lovely verandah and table and chairs.  Beds were comfortable with wardrobes and bathroom with room to move, seperate bathroom and toilet.  All your basic needs are supplied in kitchen, lounge, dining area.  TV and DVD came in handy.||There are games and DVDS in the main ofgice at no charge also icecreams and small collections of soft drinks.||Jenny and Marshall are very welcoming and accommodating.||Great spot not far from Wilsons Prom,  Port Welshpool and there other spots to explore. There is an IGA store very close by.||I recommend a drive up to see the wind turbines the view is magnificent especially on a clear day.||We will definately be back.
We have been there last month , Big4 park is well maintained and beautiful location. Staffs are polite and very welcoming. We enjoyed our stayed there and looking forward to visiting again.
I absolutely loved staying at Big 4, we had a lovley secluded camp site right on the water front. Had a family friendly laid back atmosphere. And only 5 minutes from the Prom entrance, perfect 👌

Netter Campingplatz. Standplätze mit Wasseranschluss sind begrenzt und kosten extra. Strom ist überall vorhanden. Abwasser und Klo-Entsorgung gibts hier nicht. Ansonsten alles da, Toiletten, Duschen, Grill- und Kochplätze, Laundry, Spielplatz.
Mir war es für Februar, also Sommer, zu windig und zu kalt (lange Hosen, Socken, warmer Pullover/Fliessjacke). Würde den Campingground jetzt nicht nochmal anfahren.
Nice place to stay, has everything that you need. Park Service's were helpfull. Shame we could not have stayed longer. Had to move on.
Great camping spot right on the water, great sunset and sunrise

I would love to say that this caravan park was horrible so I can save it for myself, but I'd be lying. BIG4 Yanakie was perfect. The site we were given was perfection. It was right on the water. I would book again in a second if I could guarantee the same site. We were very spoilt.
好棒的露營地
Mel and Rocky were both super helpful and really made us feel welcome during our stay!
Loved it.
Peaceful and perfect. Neat and tidy. Large site.
Beautiful location for the heat thisJanuary week.Calm waters,good  amenities,close to Prom hikes.Very happy with the peace and space for our van.
Good service:-) Beautiful place. Definitely we're coming back.
Amazing place. Safe for kids. Good facilities. Friendly staff.
We came to the caravan park the service was excellent and what a great spot to stay and enjoy beach front sites.  We had some ice creams and enjoyed the area.

Our stay at big 4 Wilsons Prom Holiday Park was such a beautiful experience. We booked a camping site with an amazing sea view, and waking up to the sunrise over the water was something I’ll never forget. The calm morning light, the fresh air, and the peaceful surroundings made us feel so recharged.
Went there again (03 rd time) sea view cabin this time . As always the stay was great , loved each second of it . Location & views are excellent .
We always have a great time at this caravan park.

It is a beautiful camp sight, lots of tress and natural beauty. But we had a tent and near the water so very very windy, so much so we ended up cutting our trip short by 2 nights. If you have a tent ask to be put in a sheltered spot. Also, I do not know the criteria for a Big 4 but this was certainly not one. No jumping pillow, pool etc....very very small camp kitchen. Close to the Prom which is good

We had a wonderful experience staying at Toora Tourist Park. The location is excellent with shops close by, and the overall atmosphere is peaceful and family friendly. The pool, spa, and kids activities were great, and the pricing was very competitive for the holiday season. We felt lucky to even get a spot. Some of the games are a little outdated, but it didn’t affect our enjoyment. Overall, a fantastic place and we would happily stay again.

A brilliant little caravan park! This park ticked all the boxes with lots of things to keep the kids busy, great amenities and close to local attractions. The team were so friendly and lovely. We'll definitely be back :)

A great camp site with so much to do for the kids; two playgrounds, pool, free mini golf, big jumping pillow, soccer, etc. Facilities are clean and the two owners very friendly and attentive. Would love to come back of our route allows.

Nice caravan Park. Great play areas for the kids. Trampoline, mini golf, basketball and tennis court, heated pool, playground
Has BBQ amenities, communal kitchen with fridges and microwave etc

Stayed in a van the sites are not your top notch sites like some places. But I liked it here. It dose have a lovely indoor heated pool and spa. Nice games room and all the facilities you need for your stay. If I'm passing again I would stay here
Always a great time spent at Toora tourist park. Management and staff are so friendly and accommodating! A great little spot to visit!!
Beautiful environment. Close to beaches and the Prom. Quiet park and lovely, clean amenities.

A perfect location for a holiday with small children. We stayed in a cabin which had everything we needed. The kids loved the facilities and everything was so easy - there’s even a well-stocked supermarket within walking distance. We’re looking forward to our next stay!
The wifi didn’t work, the tv had no reception on any channel, the kettle was filthy,  1 of the stove elements didn’t work and the other took almost 10 mins to fry an egg.

Gippsland’s hidden gem

great caravan park has everything you need. It’s a short walk into town, and Foster is only a 10-minute drive away for anything you can’t find in Toora. One of the cleanest parks, toilets, and showers we’ve ever seen in a caravan park, and the heated indoor pool was a bonus! Drive-through sites are perfect for those like me who hate to back in a van. The staff in the office were very helpful, telling us what’s around to see and do. Definitely worth a visit.

We had a really lovely couple of days at this park. Lots of options for the 8 and 10 year old. Service was great and cabin spacious. Whilst not much happening in the actual town of Toora, we were close to tourist attractions such as Agnes Falls and Long Jetty.

Awesome tourist park. Central location to national parks, wilsons prom and Port welshpool. Facilities in our cabin were clean, well maintained and well equipped. Plenty of space and for the children to play (pool, trampolines, cloud, tennis/basketball courts, rec room) and adults to relax and make dinner or plan the days adventures. Staff were lovely and down to earth. I highly recommend this place.

Stayed here for 2 nights in a powered site.
Travelled all over Australia and this is definitely in the top 3 stays. Owners are very friendly and easy to communicate with. The park itself is kept tidy and clean. Modern amenities. Huge kitchen and dining hall. The parks activities are amazing (even have a heated swimming pool and spa to top it off) with all the gear ready for fun without having to hire anything.

Can't go wrong with a week or two here to check out the surrounds. Beautiful waterfall just up the road.

Cheets Toora Tourist Park we will be regulars!
Great place to stay, plenty to see and do! Kids and adults catered for! Nice people running the park! Thank you for a memorable 4 days! 😀😀

We stayed on a powered site for a few days, the park was very tidy and there was loads to do for children, indoor heated pool and jacuzzi was fantastic and amenities were clean all within close proximityof Toora's main street, only criticism is that some of the caravan sites could be a bit more level. Overall we would stay there again.

We had purchased caravan,the site we were on had power but no water so watch that when you book but it was a short stroll to the camp kitchen and toilet block.  It was extremely windy when we were thete and were very thankful we were at the back of the park sheltered from the wind.
Nice and close to wilsons prom about 20-30min.  No pool at this park but the beach is right there to play in.
Very little to no mobile phone reception or internet coverage, there is WiFi but only at 1 camp kitchen
Great spot with generous sized sites. Our site was pretty well sheltered from the crazy winds at the time. Great location and easy day trips to the NP and attractions nearby.

We were staying in a caravan for one week and yes the location is beautiful however we didn’t realise the powered sites didn’t have water and no dump point!!! How strange for a Big 4 to not have these facilities. Also, they ran out of gas during our stay so not only no water but their amenities blocks produced cold showers. So we couldn’t have a hot shower in our caravan or the amenities block!! Our stay was so disappointing and we have been to quite a lot of Big 4s now.
We used the powered camp site.  Great location.  Dog friendly, on the waters edge.  Friendly staff and shady sites.

Very nice but very windy.
Although lots of shelter.
Many ant nests.
Great park ,great sites drinking water on nearly every site ,staff are very friendly and we had a coffee van every day aswell, 2 great camp kitchens and everything kept really clean

Loved the friendliness of the staff and everyone camped around.
Very enjoyable time.

Such a beautiful location, facilities are well kept and staff are lovely. Overall amazing experience and addition of activities such as an outdoor movie night with a cold rock truck was such a nice holiday add on.
This place could not get any more beautiful! Sites an awesome, facility’s are great and the people are the best. 10/10

We were here for a week on a powered site, the Park had a great vibe , the kids had an absolute blast, in a very handy location to head down to Tidal River etc, the amenities were always clean and the park very well maintained,  will definitely be heading back there again.
The park is well maintained - everything worked, is very kids friendly and is right next to the lake for paddle boarding/kayaking. We had a great time!

Extremely disappointing customer service. 6 days out from my boyfriend and my anniversary and we were given a call today telling us we had been double booked and they were unable to fulfil our fully paid for, booked in advance, 2 night stay. They took zero accountability for the issue that caused this result, saying it was “out of [their] control”. They offered us the option to pay full price for a 1 night stay in a different camp site as compensation. This ‘full price’ offer is double the price that the original campsite is for 1 nights stay. Also, this offer was for 1 night, not for 2 (as we had booked). Both my boyfriend and I are students, making this offers lack of affordability as well as inability to accommodate for two nights completely unacceptable and frankly not at all good enough. I provided all this information to the employee on call asking if there was any way to avoid this incredible inconvenience. She had zero empathy for the position that they had put us in. Completely unreliable and untrustworthy service. We are now left with no accommodation in the area as they could only refund us and not provide any compensation. I wouldn’t recommend booking for any specific dates that you were looking to celebrate.

Relaxing holiday once again . , loved the food vans
It was a real treat not having to cook for 2 nights
Fur babies love the beach as well , dog friendly is a plus .
Great park in the perfect spot Thank you 🙂

Our first time caravanning as a family and we can’t fault the place. Low key park but with everything we needed. Great for day trips around gippsland - must visit squeaky beach and Walkerville South. Thank you to the staff for the excellent customer service.

We had a powered camp site no water.
Site was huge and protected from the wind and elements. Sea front site windy as ever.

We were grateful to be on a site set back from beach which was sun filled and protected. Toilets close by could benefit from some soft closure on doors as they shut close very load often.

Visitors need to work on their hygiene with sanitary disposal but that not bad on the park for cleanliness. Promptly with sms reporting of problems and events.

Duck point track is a  beautiful calm dog friendly track and opens to a lovely beach with swans and tippy to sit in. Only 1km walk 10 mins. We packed a picnic and seat drinking and eating delicious cheese board.

Coffee truck and pizza wheelie good pizza was available for a night off or date night. Love that. Thank you.

They provide me with an unpowered campsite after I have paid for a powered campsite.

No one to talk to and management is terrible

All spots are mess
I stayed on a site. Great service and it is amazing what a difference a renovated bathroom makes. If only more places would do the same.

Paid $30 more for a powered site than other places so far. Disappointed to find no cooking utensils eg. Fry pan, saucepan, tongs etc.
Spoilt our plans for the night.
Also shower at about 10pm that night, almost cold!
Kitchen and amenities were clean and nice.
Great place thanks

My husband and I have been Camping at the BIG4 Ingenia every year for the past five years. What started with us just staying there in our tent in that first year became a big friends and family pilgrimage at the same time every year in January. It ended up being six families with a good mix of adults and kids with some staying on the caravan sites and some staying in the cabins. For the first few years, we absolutely loved it. The park offered amazing swimming facilities for the kids, great food trucks, Nice cabins and pretty well maintained caravan sites and we were all really excited to go every year. Unfortunately, the third year in the prices started to climb substantially and we also noticed in that particular year some of the amenities dropped in quality to the point where some of the members of our party complained to reception about the cleanliness of some of the amenities. We were also a bit unhappy about the date restrictions on some of their caravan/tent sites that resulted in one family of our big party not returning after that. The other members of our group decided to stay on another few more years as we had taken the time over the years to reserve our spots to be close to one another but every year the price kept climbing and we started to question if it was worth it after three years in a row of price hikes, especially as a lot of us had stayed at many other caravan parks around Victoria that offered the same experiences at a lower price. This year thankfully the price of the sites hasn’t skyrocketed again but we just found out that our friends that has stayed in the cabins got a really massive hit. Also the caravan sites that we have stayed in for many years has deteriorated to a point where we just can’t justify staying there any more. We are older and wiser and like I said have had many other experiences in caravan parks around Victoria that makes it very difficult to justify the price that Ingenia Inverloch continues to push without any evidence of higher quality overall in their park to justify this. To be honest, we feel like it’s daylight robbery. Finally we decided to stay elsewhere with our group next year and just to make matters worse when we cancelled our long time booking, their response was really disappointing and management couldn’t care less that they had lost long time clients and many groups of families. This park really needs to take a look at themselves and start providing some justification for many years of ridiculous price hikes. Very disappointing to say the least.
Great place to stay and visit the surrounding areas

We stayed on a grass site. The sites are smaller than other parks, felt a bit sqashed in.
There is also no disability toilets, the existing toilets are very narrow and tight to get around. I have a walker to get around and this was stressful. The location, staff and all the splash pool, heated pool and areas for children are fantastic. We want to come back with our grandchildren.
Our Family had a terrific time and enjoyed all the amenities, rooms were great and service was excellent.
Fantastic stay great staff very helpful will certainly be back and bring with us another family 👍👏👌✅✅✅
First time staying and very impressed with the amount of children activities! Will definitely come back another time soon!

Good deluxe cabin.  Fully equipped, had everything we needed .   Only issue was parking as very tight where we were with our  4 door Ute and another big 4 wheel drive vehicle parked right behind ours.  Easy access to Andersons inlet across road and close proximity to town centre.  Friendly staff and good amenities for young families.  Will be back again 👍

The unit we had was very well appointed, with all amenities required. The staff assistance was great (friendly an professional), and the location of the site was very handy to the town centre. We really enjoyed our time there. Thoroughly recommended.

Really great holiday park! Our 3-year-old loved the pool and splashpark, jumping pillow and playground. We didn't use the pump track but saw it was very popular. Comfortable cabin and great location - so close to the corner store, sandbar and kid-friendly beach.
Staff are very helpful ,lovely big park good facilities

Poor handling of a simple late check-out request. We are a young family with two children (2 and 5 years old) and requested an 11am check-out. On arrival, we were told to ask in the morning and that it would be manageable. The next day, one staff member suggested 10:30am, but another then refused and insisted on a 10am check-out. This lack of consistency was frustrating, especially when the park was very quiet with only a few families and many empty cabins. With a 3pm check-in, there was ample time to prepare rooms.
On the positive side, the amenities are excellent, the location is great, and it’s a fantastic park for kids. Overall, a good stay, but flexibility and clearer communication need improvement.

Hi, one night in D16 cabin. Two older adult men , one lady and two small Jack Russell’s. We were expecting a small courtyard? Dogs could easily push open wire door and escape. No enclosure anywhere. Car was damaged trying to reverse into small car park  space  on left hand side , told to park there! Difficult 3 point Turn . It’s a small car! One key set for three adults!!  Ridiculous storage in main bedroom? Plastic bowls and tumblers
Great van site and location as always. Amenities in much need of a makeover. They were very run down and not very clean this time round which was somewhat disappointing

Loved the park and the location but our
caravan site was really small and hard to reverse caravan and fit car.

We were also set up near children’s playground, toilet block, camp kitchen, coffee van and village green which was EXTREMELY noisy ant times and lack of privacy. Children riding bikes on road and hitting our car.

Can I suggest a door stop on the men’s toilet door so the same does not slam shut day and night. Plus children riding bikes should also be required to obey the 5klm speed zone

We completely understand it was the busiest time of the year and all in all we still loved our stay.

Thank you very much for hosting us.

Loved our second stay at this park! Jess and the crew are super friendly and helpful!
Stunning views and gorgeous big sites, well maintained and a great spot for accessing the Prom.
We’ll definitely be back!

The location is nice, no doubt. But be aware - infrastructure and hygiene are absolutely terrible. There are only 6 (!) male toilets in 2 blocks for all the sites and all the cabins without toilets. One out after cleaning the mess was unacceptable. About half the campers are
without any knowledge how to use a camp toilet or shower. There is no dumb point for caravans so these guys are using the toilets to empty their tanks. Our friends stayed in a cabin - price value is below zero. Pure rip off. Don’t know how BIG4 accepts these standards. Immediately rise cleaning frequencies and/or install a third block. Dump point should be coming immediately. We will not come back.
Powersite with amazing environment, clean toilets and well maintained sites.
Great location and much quieter than being on the Prom over Christmas and New Year. Lovely park, very accommodating hosts. We were really happy

Staff friendly. Park grounds great. The facilities need upgrading at the back of the park. But playground kitchen area is modern and new. Good spot to camp with dog and for days spent in national park as dog sitters available 20mins away.

The BEST sites ever!
Coastal view cabins or sites are awesome. Enormous sites for caravans or tents, I never seen any sites as big as those here.
Clean bathrooms and you can see the cleaning times shown in the wall.
Also great location that you can visit nearby Wilson Prom national park and Big Drift.
Clean costal waters that you can see Banjo Sharks, swans, birds, pipis and crabs.
Many many walking tacks you can choose.
A great place for family, so many activities can entertain adults and kids.
Maybe strong wind at night.

A really wonderful camping experience. There’s power and water, hot showers available, and it’s only about a 10-minute drive to the national park. Most importantly, the campsite is clean, tidy, and very well maintained by everyone.
We’ll definitely come back again with the kids.

Fabulous, they let me in at the Inn no booking, at 4.55pm on Christmas Eve, so no need to look for a stable. Beautiful camp ground, lots of space between sites, sheltered from the horrendous winds, great place to stay, so close to Wilson's Promontory.  Thank you !

Need to cut back trees and shrubs to make it easier to manoeuvre through the park, particularly through the back of the park.there were larger spots available for large vans but we feel like we were in the tightest spots
Beautiful area, nice and clean services, friendly staff.
Well maintained, with excellent shade and sheltered site. Great location. Will be back.
Powered camping site fabulous spot and so quiet. Will definitely come again.

Beautiful place, great service, beautiful views and walks. Watch out for high tide time if you walk along the beach. No dump place for Grey and black water in the caravan park. Use Tidal River dump when you visit the park. It is in the overnight hikers parking

We loved our premium camp site with sea view, the ground and border bushes are well looked after and so pleasant. Amenity was excellent and kitchen was always clean and had all the equipment. We could shelter from wind and rain in the nice dining area. We’d love to come again.
We love the pool and water park!

AMAZING PLACES for KIDs
has eveything clean fun excellent service
Perfect location, great on-site facilities rooms was decent and bed was comfy. Only problem was the loud bathroom exhaust and no towels for use in a cabin.

We have just had our 3rd year in a row stay at big4 inverloch! We love this park, so many things to do for families and activities and extra food trucks and services they get in! Facilities are great, close to town if we need anything, take away options and everything close, so close to the beach, the lolly bag on arrival was so perfect to help keep the kids happy in the car while we reversed into our site which can be a stressful time! Overall fantastic stay and can’t wait for next year!
Perfect location, friendly staff, clean facilities, endless activities of fun! Can’t wait to book another stay here!

Great location, not enough amenities and are in need of an upgrade. The block near us was shut twice that we know of due to blockages.I was yelled at from a distance while I was on my EBIke (Stationary)by a very rude staff member and told that EBIkes are prohibited inside the caravan park. Little did he know that the battery was not on nor did I use the battery while I was riding inside the caravan park. The speed limit is 10 klm per hour which he and the rest of the staff give no regard. We witnessed several near misses with the electric vehicles and small children. There is a lot of activities for the children but will not be back anytime in the near future.We have booked into the nearby RACV Club looks great and a LOT cheaper

Stayed with grandchildren on an ensuite caravan site
Wide internal road made parking van easy
Ensuite shower / toilet made camping easy with young children
Facilities generally were great
I was extremely disappointed the game room had no less than 8 claw machines . These are highly attractive to children and use of them is surely akin to gambling

Some of the staff were inconsistent with the requirement of helmets.  This is especially an issue when dealing with kids as they need consistency with rules as it can often be triggering.   And when spoken to staff about this, her response was that some staff are young and just don’t know…this is not a reason for inconsistency as they are staff and should enforce it if it is a rule
