Caravan park is good, but our powered site which was over $200 was mostly uneven dirt.  Had to arrange our tent so we could find a flat surface.

Spot on for familys, the full Big 4 experience. Went camping with another family at the park, facilities for the kids are amazing (has everything you could think of), including entertainment. Staff are very friendly too. Beach is 50m from the park entrance and there is a general store on the corner. Safe and family friendly, kids rode their bikes everywhere and joined in cricket and splash park play. We wanted to extend our stay after the first night but unfortunately fully booked. Can imagine coming back many times.

Extremely disappointed that we were not informed that the park is dog friendly after they changed their policy post our booking. It cost us a lot of money to have our dog minded locally. A meek apology doesn’t really cut it.

We had a lovely time at Inverloch. The kids had the best time and lived the splash pool and jumping pillow. Lovely staff and great patrols at night keeping the park quiet after 10:00. Nice and safe for the kids and everyone very respectful when driving in and out of the park.

Great experience, love the park.
Would be great to have a larger amenities block down the back.

We stayed at one of the safari tents which was clean and comfortable. Safari tents are not great with the wind (which can't be helped) but apart from that? it was a lovely stay. Kids can roam around comfortably unsupervised.

The park is the best we have ever been to! So much for kids to do and it’s all inclusive. We stayed in an EcoTent and it was amazing but noisy with the wind, not the parks fault, we chose a really windy time to be here.

The indoor splash park and pool were a big hit and all weather! We highly recommend the park.

We called for a last minute booking and we were so well accommodated. The staff are so friendly and welcoming.

We have rebooked for next year so we don’t miss out!

The facilities were very good and very family friendly. So many activities for all ages.
The cabin was old, cramped, one bedroom smelt musty, the bunk bed  mattresses were very thin and uncomfortable and the cost was far too expensive for what we had. It was January, peak season so we expected to pay a lot but also expected better accommodation.
The service was prompt and the staff were helpful and friendly.
The location was excellent.
Lovely small town. Quiet caravan park with putt putt and jumping pillow. In door pool and spa. Amenities very clean
Absolutely beautiful peaceful and relaxing 😎 this is our second time there and will be back again. Plenty for kids to do also 10/10
A lovely park with clean amenities and some great communal areas. The local town is full off knick knacks and treasures.

Nice clean small caravan park with lots of facilities for families
There are many cabins available but I am in my caravan so I don’t know what they are like
Great park ensuite sites very clean and roomy will stay here again
A lovely little park with great amenities. The kids loved the pool and games room. Would stay again in a heartbeat

I chose to stay here as the park has pet friendly cabins so we were able to bring our small dog.We stayed in a pet friendly cabin which was perfect for our needs.The staff were friendly and very accommodating when I needed to change original booking dates.The park itself was clean and tidy and the indoor pool is great.We will definitely stay again.Toora is a lovely little place

We had a powered site with ensuite. Recommend for the extra storage space. Nice flat and shady site, but close to the road and those trucks going past at night are loud. Our family made great use of the pool, but it needs linger hours so mums can relax in the spa after kids go to bed. Overall enjoyable stay.
Great place.to stay
Beautiful clean place, amazing amenities and staff (I think owners) are very lovely. Will definitely be visiting again.

Stayed at the tourist park for 5 days with hubby and the kids. Everyone had an awesome time. We had unlimited access to a pool and spa, basketball and tennis court, mini golf, trampoline, swings, playground, table tennis, jumping pillow,  big screen TV, huge DVD library, board games etc. The kids never ran out of fun things to do! Also, the tourist park is located next to the town Main Street and public swimming pool. Started the year with fun times in the heat (and thank goodness there was air conditioning in our cabin that reached all the rooms). We’d highly recommend a stay at this caravan park!
10 out of 10

In a quiet town, which doesn't have much other than a cafe, pub, pharmacy and small supermarket. Have to drive to Yarram for your bigger town. Close to good fishing at Port Welshpool and Port Franklin. But overall, nice and quiet and relaxing camping location. Has an indoor pool and spa, games room, tennis court, mini golf inflatable pillow, trampoline. So plenty for the kids.

My family and two other families booked in for 2 nights there were 15 of us in total and we all had a fantastic time,we didn't need to leave the park,kids went swimming for hours on end plus tennis,basketball,trampoline and jumping pillow,if you have children this is a must!!,highly recommend
Small park with everything you would need. Situated in a picturesque town. Nice greenery inside the park

Lovely little park in a beautiful location not far from the Prom gates.
Sites are a very decent size.
Staff were amazing and very friendly.
The communal spaces such as the camp kitchen and amenities were always kept clean. We thoroughly enjoyed our time there.

Current facilities do not adequately support all families. Additional equipment such as more toilets and fridge space would be helpful. Although there is another area available, it is too far away to be practical

The Cabin we got is pretty new and it's right next to the water 🤩 the view is so so beautiful. We enjoyed our stay all day everyday!
During our stay we noticed that lots of campsite are beautifully maintained and located right next to the water as well. I think we will try that later 😊

Great beach front site for large van. Plenty of space. The serenity of the location was spoilt by drones flying over us for a lot of our stay, unsupervised children bouncing basketball after 9.30pm & from 6am. I am a woman and was disturbed to find a male in the womens toilet block at 9pm, using the toilet, cleaning his teeth & about to have a shower. When I entered he asked if I was in the wrong place, I corrected him & waited outside for him to finish. I was uncomfortable with this encounter.

Beautiful camp site right on the water. The bathroom, playground and kitchen facilities were well maintained. Unfortunately the front reception was never staffed and the rangers were unreasonable regarding car parking.
Didn't stay yet right on the water. I'm staying next time here. I had a look.

Group of 6.

Unfortunately we didn’t enjoy it.
Location and park are nice, however we won’t be coming back.

Toilets very dirty, only 2 bbq in the park. Dirty kitchen. Really small in general.
Simply perfect!! We stayed on a beachfront spot and it was one of the best campsites we stayed in our 3 month trip in Australia.
Schöner, sauberer Campingplatz, perfekt für einen Ausflug in den Wilson Prom Nationalpark.

We had a wonderful stay at Wilson’s Prom holiday park, the sites were spacious and very secluded with spectacular views of the Prom. I would definitely recommend this park to families wanting a quiet break and plan on returning .
Cabin was new and spotlessly clean. Staff were great

The new tiny houses are great, beds comfortable and actually bigger then anticipated.but with no bathroom, no running water and no fires allowed (despite being advertised as part of the deal) we felt they were over priced. We also had to request water bottles as they were not provided.

The main bathrooms in the park were very very tired.

We did have a wonderful time though and will definitely return, as the location is fabulous.

Love the place.

Need path ligting as flood lights spoil can the camp experience..

We would love to come back
Lovely park, friendly staff and great powered sites. Would recommend.

Thank you for an enjoyable stay at Eagle Point.
We had the most wonderful view from the Kingfisher cabin and hope to return soon.

What a pleasure to stay in this park. Right on the lakes edge with a lovely coffee chop there. Easy to get into Paynesville to look around, which I did. Enjoyed going over to Raymond Island for a look. Beautiful Autumn days for looking at the lakes which were as smooth as glass. I'd visit again, probably not in high season when I'm guessing would be heaving with holiday makers. Very nice owners/managers of the park and helpful too.
Carvavan park was good but hot water is not enough hot water is disappointing
Eagle Point caravan park is the perfect place to relax. It's nice and quiet and the scenery is spectacular. The park amenities were clean and well looked after. We enjoyed our stay.
Big view
Very clean and comfortable. Beautiful spot! 👍🏻
Great new managers who are putting in a big effort

We loved our stay.  Its such a great spot & beautiful location.  The security lady was lovely too ensuring the park guest were obeying the rules.  Which made it a restful stay too.
We only had a non- powered site caravan site.  Which is self sufficient so I can’t comment on your rooms or bathrooms.
What an awesome spot. Less than 5 minutes to walk to the water. Facilities are clean and looked after. Will definitely stay again

Stunning premium site with a magnificent view. Staff Daniel and Kerrie very friendly and knowledgeable about fishing and the area.
Will definitely stay again
Had everything we needed. Clean, tidy, great staff.

Very lovely and friendly service
I beautiful spot near the beach
Quite place with wildlife

Great waterside caravan park. Our original booking had to be changed and we were then put on a brilliant premium powered site. Close to amenities, camp kitchen and the Eagle Point foreshore hub.  Fantastic location only 5 minutes from Paynesville and 15 minutes to Bairnsdale. Enjoyed the stay would recommend.

The park is in a fabulous location next to Lake King.
All facilities are clean and the staff are helpful.
We stayed there overnight on a cycling trip and the options for safe scenic cycling routes nearby are numerous.

We stayed in our caravan on a PREMIUM unpowered site which was a lake itself in the morning after light rain overnight.
When we arrived no one in the office.
A "Self guided" site tour had us walking around for some time to find the site.
For a Big4 park it was a great disappointment.
Would not recommend.
Beautiful location great facilities friendly staff. Thank you, had a great stay.
Good site in excellent location. Clean, efficient and easy.

We recently stayed at Eagle Point Caravan Park and had the most amazing time. From the moment we arrived, the team made us feel incredibly welcome. A special thank you to Kirsten and Kerry — they were absolutely amazing. They made us feel right at home, ensured we had a good laugh, and genuinely cared that we enjoyed our stay.

We booked four premium powered sites where my siblings (my two brothers and my sister), our mum, and all of our kids stayed together. It worked out perfectly and the view from our sites was fantastic. It was such a great setup for a family getaway and there was plenty of space for everyone.

The amenities were always very clean, the grounds were super tidy, and the camp kitchen was spotless and well maintained. I honestly couldn't fault the park at all.

With young kids, it was the perfect place to stay. They had an absolute ball and there are so many activities to do around the area and close by. It made for the perfect long weekend getaway.

The food trucks were also a great bonus and something we all really enjoyed.

Thank you for having us — we thoroughly enjoyed our stay and honestly didn’t want to go home. We will definitely be back!

Tranquil spot. Although the park was pretty full, there's was a lovely atmosphere. People were quiet and kids were safe to ride bikes all around. My only complaint is the irritating talk radio station that played in the toilet block all day and night. Not sure why they had too play anything, but why not calming music.
Lots of facilities, walking distance to wonderful historic country town (that has all the basic services), and easy drive to some great sights
Great little park for a relaxing weekend a short walk to pub (great meals),IGA and chemist

Stayed here for two nights, in a caravan the staff was very friendly and  the location was walking distance to the town which has a food works for the essentials. Only negative is the caravan sites are very small! There is a dump point on site and good bbq and camp kitchen facilities. Would I come back here again and stay...... For sure!

Flat powered site. Clean bathrooms with bathmats available. Lots of hot water and great water pressure. Great camp kitchen and games room and other facilities.  Would be great for families or adult travellers. Courteous and helpful staff.
Welcoming arrival.  Lovely clean cabin with all one needs. Pet friendly!!! 💗🐕
A great place for a vacation. Conveniently located near town centre, shops and restaurants. There is an indoor heated pool and spa.

I'm rating this tourist park on people I have spoken to. Have not stayed there but my Doctor is just across the road, people say it's a very nice clean and friendly tourist park, and it certainly looks like it is, and it's only 12 KMs to small beautiful town Foster, but Toora has a Foodworks supermarket and a hotel plus nice clean park that you can take your dog for a nice quiet walk, and I think they have a heated swimming pool at Toora which is very handy for people with medical problems. They did have small chemist there but sadly it has closed, Foster is close with chemist. Tourist Park looks beautiful  plenty of shaded trees, people I spoke to where all happy with stay there.

Stayed here over the Anzac long weekend.
The caravan sites are well kept. Bathroom amenities clean and functional. There are playgrounds, a jumping pillow, tennis court with basketball ring for the kids.
Dogs are welcomed on leads and there is a paddock at the rear of the park where you can't also walk them.  It's a short walk into town where there are pubs, a cafe, pharmacy, grocery store, and other shops.
Closer to water falls, Welshpool pier, the trail trail walk and lots more.
What a great caravan park good clean amenities good size and tidy sites also very close to the great Southern Rail Trail.

A fantastic place to stay. We stayed over the Easter long weekend and it was buzzing! Despite the amount of people the ammenities were always very clean and tidy. Plenty of space in powered sites even with people close by and great facilities of mini golf, jumping pillow, playgrounds at our back door and lovely scenic views from up the hill.

Great spot to go and see multiple locations around Gippsland as well. Was certainly a great trip and we can’t wait to come back next year! Thanks Toora Tourist Park for your great hospitality and service

A wonderful place to stay, especially if you have young kids. Great location close to Foster, Fish Creek and the Prom. And the town of Toora itself is a short walk away and charming.

Cabins are the most spacious I’ve experienced in Victoria, clean and modern. Friendly and helpful staff; they even posted my charger which I left behind for free.
The only room for improvement is the wifi but that could be the connection for the whole area…

Highly recommended and will return. Thanks for a lovely stay.

I was here over easter weekend, and the atmosphere was amazing! There was a lot to keep my little squirrle brain occupied, and having that kitchen there was an absolute GODSEND! The people here were lovely, and it is very kid friendly.
Friendlly staff who helped with power for my Tesla
Great family park, good amenities. Recommend for a short stay
Extremely clean park.
We are regulars the last 5 years and every time we come we notice several improvements. They take feedback on board and service is always impeccable. Staff are caring and attentive.
The staff here are amazing. Always smiling and very helpful. I left something in our cabin and they successfully passed it onto to our friends who were still at the holiday park.
Lovely summer holiday spot.  Thank you

Loved our stay here, great location, great campsite, tight fit getting in with the van when all the sites were full. Toilet blocks getting close to needing a freshening up.
Splash zone and pool were enjoyed by the kids.
Crazy busy during January.  .  Staff not able to keep up with cleanliness in toilets.  Probably not enough toilets to people .  Great location.  Pricey for caravan site even for peak in my opinion

We stayed at powered site .
Kids loved splash pool and swimminng pool.
Staff and service was great .
20 min walk to town centre .
Overall great

Great things for kids do. Jumping pillow really bouncy, hire bikes good fun but pricey. Pool was terrific. Toilet block needs a lot of renovations ie dripping taps, faulty shower heads. Poor internet. Staff extremely friendly and helpful. Code is really long to remember for gate.

Suggestions:
1/ Please clean the washing machines and dryers
2/ There needs to be a filtered drinking fountain for everyone to use near the outdoor playground  the water in the rooms was not nice drinking water

The only good thing about this place is that kids can walk around freely and ride their bikes
Felt safe as every one else is their for the same thing plus there are cameras everywhere 👍👍
It’s great to go with another family
Three to four days is more then
Enough time …otherwise it’s the same thing every day ….. pools water park bounce pillow and outdoor playground

Great family destination! Has all the entertainment for the kids.
Great service from all staff.
Great park. Clean and tidy facilities. A winner for the kids with a pool, splash play area, pump track, jumping pillow, etc, Dog friendly. And the staff are all lovely. We will definitely come back!

Very busy. Sites are super tight and small. Challenging 4 vans to get in and out. You are literally on top of each other it’s that busy. Indoor water park. Small indoor pool - hard on the eyes. Very chlorinated. The games room is awful. Full of cheap claw games that parents are harassed $ for. We stayed  3 years ago and it was a really cool space - not now. Table tennis $10 per day hire. Small playground. Jumping pillow. Small Skate park. Lots of very small kids scooting around in bikes : scooters, so be careful driving here. Good security re gates and entry / exit. 3 years ago a security guard was on duty patrolling till midnight. Not now. 2am noisy neighbours and no one cares. It’s a family park. Rules are rules. 10pm quiet. Facilities are very small 4 the size of the park. Good big camp kitchen. Great location to the beach and walk to town. Would not come back. Expensive ++++ in high season. I’ve stayed at much better Big 4’s with the kids. Wouldn’t return.
Packing was an issue, only one carpark for a 3 bedroom apartment, we had 2 families with 5 children to unpack cars was really difficult with only one car allowed

We had a fantastic family holiday with you. We stayed in 3 separate cabins next year we hope to get 3 closer together
Everything was great but the only comment I would make is that several of the children and some of the adults got cuts on their feet in the indoor pool, the surface was a bit rough
We are looking forward to holidaying there again next January

We had an amazing stay at Inverloch. The staff at reception were very friendly and knowledgeable on the area. The maintenance team were wonderful helping us out when wind threatened our awning. The food and coffee vans were great. Excellent quality and variety. Can’t wait for our next visit

Felt like we were squished in like sardines…
Worse experience ever!
We were all just finished setting up our camping site when we just sat down to have a caravan pull up next to us… all the amendies were all plugged in right where out tent entrance and cooking area was already set up… their hot water service was pumping out heat right where my kids were sitting, they flooded our cooking area when they didnt plug in their pipe to drain their grey water out..
then when my 4 children age between 11/2 through to 10 couldnt aleep as people wouldnt keep their voices down till 11:30/midnight.
We stayed only one night.. out of 4 nights we had booked for….
No other room available for us to move our set up either…

On a positive note…. Splash park, pool, go carts all extra activities were great and the cleanliness of the facilities were amazing too..

But unfortunately we will not be staying there ever again.

Awesome caravan park, in a fantastic location, staff very friendly and helpful. However unfortunately this year, after 12 straight years, we found our lovely, lush grass sites (as advertised) a combination of attempted replantation of rolled out turf (dead) and crushed rock. With the rising cost of sites over the years, we expect better. Ensuite door clip is broken, which meant door did not close. This caused flooding during showers. Fixing these improvements, would 100% give a 5 star.
Nice and quiet , friendly staff , clean facilities

We stayed in one of the tiny houses for 5 nights and it was absolutely superb. The view was just stunning. The facilities were great and whilst there was no running water during our stay, the staff supplied us with bottled water and did our dishes for us. We used our own camping gear for cooking but made use of the little bbq that was supplied exclusively to the cabin as well. The heating worked perfectly and the cabin was very comfortable. I had had surgery prior to coming and was still recovering and the staff were fabulous in making accommodations for us by allowing us to use the disabled facilities which were really well equipped for me. It was a superb getaway for us.

Absolutely amazing place to stay. The views were awesome. I had a wonderful spot one site back from the beach. Big shout out to Josh for helping me with my van. He was very patient and friendly. Same goes for Jess in the office. Thank you both so much.
Caravan camping. Lovely outlook surrounded by nature.

I was really impressed with this place! My friend and I stayed here for one night. The staff were very friendly and kind to us! We stayed in Cabin 1, a small cozy house that had everything we needed and was very comfortable! 👍

The only thing to note is that the bathroom is shared outside, since there isn’t one inside the cabin. It wasn’t a big issue for us! but since the weather was quite cold — especially at night it did feel a bit chilly even with the heater on. Still, it gave us a nice camping vibe and felt like a lovely getaway experience! 🌿

What I loved the most was the stunning view — a wide open sea that looked absolutely beautiful, especially at sunrise around 6 am 🌅 Overall, it’s a wonderful place to stay — perfect for families, friends, or couples looking for a peaceful retreat outside the city. Thank you to the staff for taking good care of us and giving us such a great experience! I’ll definitely recommend this place to my friends! 💕

One of the best camping sites I have been to. Clean toilets, hot shower, kitchen providing all we need, well care and beautiful structure.
The lock device on doors that was a bit annoying to press the code all the time.
Great location and the cabin was clean and comfortable. We will visit again.
Beautiful cabin with great views.

Lovely spot for families. Close to all the walks offered on the Promontory, but more comfortable than the camping at Tidal River.  Our car broke down & we needed to it & our van to be towed , and the staff were very helpful .  Thank you .

Quiet beach literally 20 metres from the van, beautiful view.
Sites have vegetation in between which is rare and offers privacy with a sense of peacefulness, much appreciated.
Clean amenities.
Nearby general store is well stocked.

Staff were extremely helpful and caring when we had a medical emergency whilst staying at the park. Special thanks to Josh and to the office staff. This is a lovely park, with a variety of caravan/ camping sites and many private bush type sites some with beach access. The camp community area is excellent.
Nature getaway.
Great experience and nice people! Lovely to have so many rabbit around!

We stayed in the 3 bedroom cottage #29. We had an absolute stunning view and plenty of room to make it home for our short stay.
We will definitely be back. Thanks for having us

A hidden gem which should be on everyone's list to go and stay at. The water front sites were spectacular and so big and roomy. The rest of the park is so relaxing with so many trees and walking areas, it was lovely. We loved it as did friends and we will go back anytime. Do yourself a favour and go there :)
Beautiful location on the corner inlet, amenities are very well maintained and the staff are very friendly

We had a caravan site which was fantastic.  30 second walk to the beach,  quiet and private.  Staff are amazing and the whole place is beautifully manicured. All facilities are very clean and for our first time caravanning,  it was a fabulous experience! Highly recommend it to anyone.
A beautiful spot on the shore of corner inlet. The facilities are clean and the grounds are pristine. A nice place to stay while visiting the Prom
Fabulous location and good amenities. Kid and dog friendly. Friendly and helpful staff. Will definitely stay again.
Great friendly homely caravan park environment surrounded by the beautiful greenery and sounds of nature 🐝🐸🙌

Very poor experience compared to other years. new management have ruined the park and with their new rules targeting minorities. Ethnic people were discriminated against by management  and one particular aboriginal boy was targeted by other campers. These bullies had seemed to be acquainted with the security as there poor actions and behaviour went unscaved. Me and my family of 25 that have been coming here for numerous years will not be returning after this personal attack on a member of my family
thanks

I had a really disappointing experience at this caravan park. From the moment we arrived, the atmosphere felt unwelcoming and uncomfortable. Staff were rude, dismissive, and treated certain guests noticeably differently. It honestly felt like people from minority backgrounds were being judged and singled out, which is completely unacceptable in this day and age.

Caravan parks are supposed to be relaxed, friendly places where everyone can enjoy their stay. Instead, this place left us feeling unwelcome and uneasy. Policies were enforced selectively, and when concerns were raised, staff brushed them off rather than addressing the issue.

I would strongly recommend management take a serious look at how guests are treated and ensure everyone is welcomed with respect and fairness. Until then, I wouldn’t feel comfortable recommending this park to others. 👎

It was a great location, unfortunately John slipped in the showers, there are black mats on the floor which must have had water under it, causing him to slip over when he stood on it,  bruised all down the back of his leg, a oc/ health and safety issue!!  We had to send my son-in-law up to tow the caravan home early by 2 days as John couldn’t walk/sit because of the pain it caused! Need something done about this issue, there are a lot of old people that travel these days and you don’t want t anymore slipping on the mats..  have stayed there many many time, love it, but would think twice now 😔
Nice caravan park.
We were only meant to stay a few days. But asked to stay a few more. We stayed almost a week. Very clean. Well-maintained. Prices are very affordable. Very quiet. Our dogs loved it as much as we did.
Lovely location with Lake views. Weather wasn’t the best but was still very relaxing. Love the hub and cafe. Hope to return soon.

It was very disappointing to be squeezed in between two tall caravans on a neglected site with numerous dirty permanent campers. On top of that, we received grumpy service from the manager. Never again we will stay there

Beautiful surroundings with huge flocks of birds at dawn and dusk - but beware of booking Cabin 2 "Rosella". Ostensibly sleeps 5 but its a poor design for any more than a couple and its very tired. A very slow and dated benchtop double hotplate and no frypan make cooking for a family very difficult. Replacement with a modern small footprint induction hotplate would be a good investment. The lino floor is also heavily marked so it looks grimey even after being swept. A few updates would make a lot of difference.
Nice little caravan park on the water. Cafe located just behind the park right in the water. Clean amenities and nice grassy sites.

We had a wonderful stay. The kitchen was very well equipped, everything was spotlessly clean, and we were surrounded by greenery and a pier. Communication was smooth and courteous. The atmosphere felt like 1995, and the music in the bathrooms brought us lots of joy and memories.
May goodness guide you and may blessings follow you every step of the way. May your life flourish beautifully.

Beautiful spot on the water front.  The park has Just become a big 4 classic.  We had a premium site close to the waterfront area.  The location is a five however the amenities block is like a 2 definitely needs a complete renovation as they are very old.  The caretaker does lots of grounds maintenance however the cleaning in the showers needs a rethink as they don't seem to clean the tiles above waist height.  There was a lot of long dark hair and toothpaste on the tiled wall in the shower l used for the whole 10 nights we were there.  Lots of rabbits running around at night.  We had 2 lots of school kids on camp and they were very close by and at times noisy. Lots of great places to go for a walk -along the waterfront or at the nature reserve  next door. Ideal spot if you have e a boat as the boat ramp is very close by.
Great location, clean facilities and friendly staff.
Booked a large powered van site and loved the location close to the water. Nice quiet park with clean amenities. I would recommend a visit.
We stayed two days, it was a perfect location.  The staff were friendly, we will definitely be back. Loved it 👍

The Caravan park has become very expensive to stay at.   We've been here about three or four times with a family catch up. It was reasonably priced..  New Managers took over and the Prices went up dramatically when we booked last year, with the promise of improvements to the park coming.
Hardly anything has improved at all.  Our site was mostly just sand.  A bit of play equipment has been placed near us but not yet fully installed.  The toilet block near us looks very tired.  Half the lights are not working.  Shower roses leaking.  The shower block up the top is slightly better but still pretty old.  We WILL NOT BE BOOKEING AGAIN NEXT YEAR.  We will be going to another park where there are better facilities for a comparable cost.  Disappointing.
Great view and very helpful staff

This is an off chance discovery, whilst we had in possession a rented campervan, and tried to find a powered site for the night…
First night was okay even though they got the message wrong on what we wanted in terms of water views… Second light we moved across to a powered site that had water views and it was just stunningly beautiful… We fell in love with this location, and plan to come back as soon as we can…
Serene, peaceful, campers all around, but everyone doing their own thing, being quiet, and respecting everyone else.
A great experience and highly recommended!
Great location , not far from the Prom.  Park is very clean and spacious with big sites and privacy.  We had a waterfront powered site .  Great views .  Toilets were very clean .
Nice little spot to stay. Next time will will book water view!

I camped here with my family last weekend as it is one of my parents favourite spots due to being pet friendly & on the beach! We had an ocean view site which is only a few steps from the water! The view is beautiful. It’s nice to see a campground that is kept clean and free from rubbish! I love that you can have a camp fire here, not too many spots allow this anymore! Upon leaving I noted a few suggestions I had via email and I not only received a nice email reply but also a phone call. I wasn’t expecting either but truly thank the team for taking the time to speak with me!
The facilities here are good, we had a windy weekend but there’s plenty of space to sit inside the shared kitchen to escape the weather if needed.
Nice campground that had heaps of room between each site. It would be perfect for kids as a good playground and calm waterfront.
Great well maintained Park. Staff were friendly and amenities looked new and modern. Large grassed sites and would stay here again. Close for day trips exploring the Prom.

As a first time visitor I was really impressed. The camp ground is well spaced out with very sizeable sites which are screened from one another by the natural bush setting. Lots of bird life and sunrise coming up over the water add to the experience.
The staff were very friendly and knowledgeable of the area and its attractions. My family and I will definitely be back.
Excellent
Great place for camping.
Lovely space. Great customer care.
We took our caravan. Staff very helpful. Views beautiful. Site very generous size. The weather was average but plenty to do in the area. Thanks for having us. See you again 😊😊
Thank you so much for a wonderful stay. The rooms were clean and comfortable and with the ocean on our doorstep we had such a magical stay! We cannot wait to come back next year!

Pros:||Beautiful location with wonderful views from the spacious prime water front sites (water and power).||Cons:||If you don't mind the relentless sounds of bouncing basketballs then this just might be the perfect place to visit.  Generally, quite otherwise.||Would a 5 rating if it wasn't for the basketballs relentless sounds.  Shame really, such an easy issue to avoid.

We love our stay in Inverloch.
It's the second year we've been, the facilities at times are a let down. The pool could do with some good cleaning and the toilet blocks at times were quite miserable.
We love the large drive through sites and being able to walk into town and the inlet. Disappointing that the price was so high this time around and I think it could be looked at.
Great location, activities for the kids and short walk to beach and shops.

Staff were friendly
Nice clean park
Plenty for kids to do
Beach is a short walk

Great Holiday Park, my family and  I stayed for 4 nights in a luxury cabin and it was fantastic! The cabin was extremely clean upon arrival and the dishwasher, clothes washer and dryer were a surprise but very appreciated! Well equipped kitchen and the beds were quite comfy (even the kids bunk beds were good). The park itself is close to town and a short walk to the beach which was handy. The splash park (albeit a bit noisy) and pool was a great way to wind down after a day of sightseeing. Overall the facilities were very good and the grounds are kept in top condition, my only issue was the staff on checkout seemed a bit rushed and brushed off my complaint regarding the faulty wristbands that we were given (perhaps i got someone having a bad morning).  Apart from that, i recommend this park for a family stay as there is lots to do along the coast and the park is nice to come 'home' to... Well done Big4 Ingenia Inverloch.
We had a wonderful stay. The only thing was it was a little pricey for a one night Unpowered site.

We’ve been staying at Ingenia Inverloch Caravan Park for the past 11 years, and it’s truly our favourite time of year. We book three sites with our friends and family and are hoping to extend to a fourth — it’s become a special tradition for us. Even more so now as our mini Groodle could join us!
The park is always well managed and beautifully looked after, which is why we keep coming back year after year. The only suggestion we’d make is that a new amenities block would be a fantastic addition, as the current ones are starting to feel a little old and tired.
Overall, we absolutely love staying here and highly recommend it.

Staying in the campervan, facilities are always kept as clean as can be (over 200 sites and they are ALL FULL) pool and water playground keep the little ones happy, and a very easy and quick walk to the beach

Very difficult with children scooting up and down concrete strips in they electric scooters.and flying toys and balls into the side of car and van. Not shade was a issue for us as I have lupus and can't be in direct sun .

First time at the holiday park.
Good facilities, rooms are tiny but what do you need as you there just to have a sleep.
Kitchen is equiped if you need to cook or it’s a special area to cook that available for everyone and almost empty even at the busiest time.
Slightly overpriced during school holidays and rooms would need a softer pillows.
Would definitely come back for longer period of time as it much to do in the area and explore many beautiful beaches.
And the water pressure in the shower is the best !!!
We had an amazing stay! The service, cleanliness, and location were all excellent. We really enjoyed our time and will definitely come back again.

Great location walking distance to town, bluff walk and inlet
Amenity’s could do with an update just small for the amount of people using the park
First few nights were so noisy and could not sleep.

No sound control at all. Music blasting at night and no staff around to say anything.  Literally could not sleep. A few people complained and staff didn’t do anything. Staff has no consideration for families.  Amenities look family friend, but service is far from.  Has someone who has kids. Definitely won’t be coming back here.

We stayed on the powered site. The on site activities for children were superb! The heated pool and splash park were awesome 👌  The only issue we had was at meal times. During peak season there were anywhere between 4 to 6 families preparing meals and not enough indoor seating. Please consider splitting kitchen facilities in another location on site just like the showers. Also a fridge with a freezer would be super handy. We had a great stay and would definitely recommend it to others!
Great holiday for family with kids. All inclusive, indoor pool and indoor water park (perfect for all weather). Pay for the peddle bikes but they're a great hit with the kids!

We were in our caravan, the ensuite was great (rooms rating). The park is definitely very family oriented & travellers our age really miss out on the facilities due to the masses of young families. Who wants to get in the pool with 100 young kids. Great park for families, not for relaxing adults wanting some peace & quiet. I was so over the lack of parental guidance each morning we woke to balls being bounced past our van at 7am. If you have a young family this is the park for you!

Lovely caravan park with good amenities including a well-stocked and tidy Camp Kitchen and beautiful views. It’s very picturesque! The only negative, which meant cutting our trip 5 days short due to feeling unsafe in a camper trailer, was the WIND!! Make sure you’re prepared as the location means strong cross winds from Bass Strait and Corner Inlet, which we were not explained when booking via phone and choosing a site.
Friendly receptionist, easy access very clean park.
Great for access to the Prom. Cabin was well equipped but in need of some maintenance. Super comfortable bed and quiet at night.
We stayed in a Premium Powered site. It was such a fantastic location and the site facilities were the best I have come across. Thoroughly recommended.

Had a great time and our tiny home was amazing. Fantastic view and in warmer weather it would be great to sit on the balcony. I just wish they had closer toilets but other than that, perfection and will be back!

The best trip! We love this campsite and the sea view cottage was perfect for a long weekend with Friends.
Can’t wait to book agaib

Stayed for two nights to explore the national park. Paid for the premium seaview site. Do it because its worth it. Front row seats to watch sunrise - recommend you also do it was beautiful coming over the water. Crystal clear water with a nice little walk to duck point. One amenities block was new the other older. The older one had better space to hang your stuff in the shower but water was very slow to get hot. They only want you to use the shower for three mins as there is no town water here. Great newer camp kitchen with two air fryers! Bunnies everywhere especially at night. Lots of potholes on the roads. You need to drive into the national park. Would recommend!
We had wonderful time Big 4 Yanakie. Rooms are clean and as shown in pictures. Will definitely come back. Just a small thing, kitchen could have more utensils. Next time I will carry my own.
The location is ideal, it’s right on the beach with perfect views depending on your site, mine was a premium site worth every dollar.

Brilliant place to stay we had a standard cabin No 4 it was about 100 Metres to beach across car park, it didn’t have toilet or shower however the facilities are not far away.  Loved our stay ended up extending an extra night.

This place is truly a piece of heaven. This was our 2nd visit with family and we can’t wait to visit again! The property and its LOCATION (right at the doorstep of the national park) are superb and the lovely service staff make it exceptional. Thanks for treating your guests so well and please keep up the great work 👌🏽
Great spot, lovely people in charge

A beautiful place to stay, we camped, and it was so peaceful and surrounded by nature….
The sites are huge, and private, facilities great, and staff very helpful and pleasant to deal with, we have booked our next escape here in 3 months…..😍

Great location, close to Wilson Prom entrance. Friendly staff, easy check in.
Convenient room for short stays, has all the basic utensils needed. No dramas.

Only downsides were from Cabin 8 location, mobile reception/data was very poor from 1 bar (Super slow) to 0 bar (No reception, this was the case 95% of the time).
Cabin 8 floor was a bit sticky & the carpet in the room were a bit dull & dirty when we checked in.
Loved the pod. Clean, modern and warm.  Right on the water's edge with magnificent sunrises and sunsets. The Park facilities were great.

Fabulous holiday park with amazing surrounds. The sites are really big which makes  such a difference to your caravanning experience. Facilities were clean and well looked after. Beach and the prom on your doorstep. Staff friendly, chatty and helpful.  Will definitely be returning.
Beautiful ensuited sea view cabin. Plenty of room. Helpful staff. Very clean and tidy. Would stay again.

The site is set to a gorgeous lake with amazing views. Also a short walk to a nature reserve which has numerous walking tracks. Approx 15 mins from Bairnsdale and a shorter drive to Paynesville. The sight has a mixture of onside cabins. Also flat  powered and unpowered sites. Kid and wheelchair friendly. Very pleasant friendly staff and the site itself is very friendly. The showers, toilets and games room are very tired and in need of a little TLC. The toilet block at the bottom section felt unclean, mold heavy stains on ceiling and floors pealing. The cost was just under $80 a night for a powered site. These seems a little  expensive.  I mostly stay at Gday sites and have only used Big4 sites twice. I am not sure what Big4 stands for but it's clearly not stars. I did however have a pleasant stay and the location is perfectly placed to visit different locations.

We stayed here over the Australia Day long weekend, Friday to Monday, and booked a premium campsite expecting a quieter, better-located spot. Unfortunately, the site we were allocated didn’t feel “premium” at all.

Our campsite was directly next to a toilet block (with large lights on all night), a camp kitchen, and a communal fire pit. This meant constant noise late into the night and early mornings; people cooking until around 11pm, washing dishes from 7am, and groups hanging around talking loudly. On one night, a group of adult women kept returning to the bathroom area from 11pm till 1.30am, laughing and yelling right outside our tent for extended periods, making sleep impossible.

What was especially frustrating was seeing other premium sites in much better locations (away from amenities and noise) sitting empty until Saturday, while we arrived Friday, stayed longer, paid more, and were given a worse site. It felt unfair and poorly managed.

The lack of sleep and constant noise meant we couldn’t relax at all; which was the main reason we booked the trip. We even discussed leaving early because of how exhausting the restless nights became.

Overall, the park itself may suit some people, but I wouldn’t recommend booking a premium site unless you can guarantee it’s not next to high-traffic areas. Sadly, we probably wouldn’t return.
Very clean have all facilities needed.

Lovely place to visit and stay we have been here twice and will definitely be back,the amenities are the best and the park is kept pristine and the sites are generous , and everyone is so friendly,staff are always out and about checking everything is running smoothly the view over the lakes is amazing so thankyou again for an amazing visit
Very clean campsite. The staff there are top shelf!!
Kerry is truly a gem. We wish to return back someday.

Excellent views, daily cleaned ammenities, great kids playground, friendly and very helpful staff.
Nice walking to the cafe to get a coffee each morning.
The perfect first caravan park experience for our kids.
We had a great time at the park, the weather was a wild but still enjoyable. The amenities were very clean, and the grounds well maintained.
Great Cafe with great views. Good service.

Great campground with large sites and clean amenities. Great public playground and picnic space is all but an extension of the camp ground. Well set up camp kitchen. We had a great stay and hope to come back when we are next in Victoria.

Lovely welcome from the friendly, helpful receptionist. Good sized pitch with some tree cover. Well maintained washrooms with clean showers and plenty of hot water.
After enjoying the beautifully clear sea water at Narooma and Mallacoota the murky, weedy water in Jones Bay was very unattractive, and the smell of rotting weed was very unpleasant and reminded us of the sulphur smell of Rotorua in New Zealand. Despite that we enjoyed a short coast walk towards Paynesville and another circular walk around the adjacent conservation area.

Vanessa and Danny's absolute favourite place to be. If there were more stars to give Paul and Kirsten would receive them. My happy place 😊
So welcoming, professional and caring. Quick to answer any questions or help in any way they can. The park is clean, well maintained and family including dogs friendly. They just ask that you keep your doggy on a lead and pickup the doodoo when puppy has finished.
Not too much to ask really.😊
The lake is there for all to use.
The park has toilets and showers, very clean at all times.
Fire pit to gather around meeting others on their own journey.
Kitchen area BBQs, games room for the kids.
Quiet time is 10pm
Do yourself a favour and check out this awesome opportunity to get amongst nature with rabbits running around and birds in the trees , fishing, walking, riding, endless options. Only 10 mins from Bairnsdale and 5 mins from Paynesville.
Love love love it 🥰

We stayed at Eagle Point Caravan Park over Christmas and New Years and I must say that was our BEST EXPERIENCE we have had so far.The management team Kirsten and Paul and all staff Kerrie,Tony and Daniel were all so amazing and friendly.Loved the communal fires,  all the chats and the amazing friends we have made.The ambience and having the lake to enjoy made the experience all the more enjoyable.
We can't wait to be back for Easter.
I would highly recommend families to visit the Park .
Thank you for making our stay
enjoyable and memorable.

Had a great stay over the New Years period. Site was great, we had an unpowered premium spot. Park is well maintained and the staff go out of their way to help in anyway they can. Definitely rebooking and coming back again. Epic job Eagle Point.

Excellent camping spot. Love the premium unpowered site. It has great ocean view and big open space on the other side of the road. Plenty of birds. Swans swimming on the ocean. Toilets and childrene bath available. Kitchen is very small though and tends to be very busy in morning and lunch. Bon fire and wood for rent and for sale. Can be very windy though. There is a small coffee shop nearby but the nearest grocery is 7 mins away by car.

Hi...We had a wonderful 10 days at Eagle Point Caravan Park. Staff are very friendly and helpful. Facilities are kept clean and fresh. We had our own Caravan so, did not see the rooms hence, no comment.
Many Thanks to the Staff...keep up the good work.

Great camping options.
Plenty to occupy the kids.
Again a fantastic stay, Christmas. and New year, pool and spa have hand rails so any age can use them .amenities excellent. Maintenance of park spot on. Lovely greeting on arrival. Highly recommend.

Great park overall. Some facilities are wearing out as time goes by ( visited there for many years), but overall still a very good stay for a family. Lots of things to do in the park: amazing pool + spa, tennis, basketball, wallyball, table tennis, mini golf, pillow. Also can climb the hill with lots of cows to see a sunset/rise. Short drive to Wilsons prom - about 30 mins. You can use this park a base to visit Wilsons prom daily, as Wilsons prom accomodation is hard to get or overpriced. Very cute little town with no real attractions,but a few cute spots. A fe wlocal attractions also short drive away. The park is aging a bit - for example some TVs are missing, Internet is very very slow, also 4G is absolutly slow as well, so don't plan to work in there, just relax. The staff is absoilutly welcoming and warm.

A bit narrow powered grass sites.
Generally clean on public area. Kitchen has plenty tables and dishwashing area. Indoor pool is small but enough for relaxing and spa area is hot as well. Have multiple outdoor playgrounds including tennis court, mini golf, jumping pillow and general kids playing stuff.
Booked two sites with friends and got mix up for dates. No easy way to explain who’s fault it is but ended unhappily. Hope they can have a clearer booking/management system.
Location is fine, has one general store and cafe within walking distance. Most sightseeing places are more than 1 hour drive.

We booked a powered site with an ensuite. Very impressive park. All amenities clean, plenty to do for the kids, games room, mini golf, jumping pillow, trampoline, basketball & tennis courts and indoor heated pool & spa. The community kitchen is fully equipped beyond expectations. Very happy we stayed here. Only thing that could have been done further was the en-suite being replenished with toilet paper, but not a deal breaker. Highly recommend.

Travelling in a Motorhome.
What a great park for families and adults.
Clean, great facilities, awesome indoor pool and spa.
So much entertainment in one place. Hill walk had an amazing view and sunset.
Great park close  convenient for Wilson’s Prom

Great location we were lucky to find a cabin at busy times of Melbourne cup day.  Great location and not far from Prom. Very close to Toora wind farm and Agnes Falls.

Cabin was very basic and needs a bit of love and cleanup.
What great little park .kids had a ball . If anyone thinking of going  inverloch big 4 . Go here first  so much better value and when they say the pool is heated it is .not like inverloch
Well rendered.  Will be back.
Could have enjoyed it more if the weather was better.

Really enjoyed our weeks stay.  The cabin was clean and had everything you need.  The indoor pool and spa were a highlight.  Great place to base yourself and look around the area.  Great place for families.  Lots of activities to keep the kids entertained.
Will be coming back, for sure.
Well laid out. Pity about the pool being available only till 1700hrs.

Stayed in our caravan, and must say - will definitely be back.
Excellent service, the manager couldn't do enough for us. Extremely well kept and clean.
One of the best we have stayed in.
Very clean grounds well maintained.  Ingrown pool awsome.
Enjoyed our 2 day stay here while visiting the sites in the area. Clean, tidy and economical.

Lovely spot to stay to explore the Prom if you are travelling with pets.
Different grades of caravan site - Waterfront (power and water), Premium (power and water), powered (power only), and unpowered. The park is not on town water supply, hence difference in sites to most van parks. Beautiful large sites with vegetation screening you from the neighbours.
No town sewerage either, only septic so no dump point. 2 amenities blocks, one old and one newer.
2 camp kitchen areas, kids swings and basketball half court.
Loved our stay and hope to come back

Great few days at Yankie BIG4 caravan park
It was very clean and well presented close to the Prom
Very enjoyable

Great accommodation, clean, tidy.
It was wonderful staying.

Jess helped us book a cabin for that night and included an offer that was available getting 3 nights for a price of 2 nights. And since we would arrive by midnight, she also arranged everything for us that we would need when we arrive. We loved the amenities and services provided by the staff. They keep the place neat and tidy. The cabin was neat and the lookout was brilliant. Everyone kept it nice and quiet. No loud noises. The place is full of rabbits at night. We are already planning our next visit.
Beautiful spot clean amenities and a tidy park..and friendly staff..
Quiet,great amenities close to everything including fishing

Beautiful spot on the water even in inclement weather!
Everyone is very helpful and friendly!

We stayed in the beachside cabin in the off-peak season. It rained a lot and it was very cold,  but the location and the cabin itself made the weekend fantastic. We were able to go for walks in between the rainfalls in the national park next door and,  return to the cabin to watch nature do its thing from the comfort of the spacious and modern cabin.
Set right on the inlet . It’s a lovely position

Recommend to anyone wanting to get away …
This is absolutely a beautiful spot and staff are friendly and helpful.
A must stay
Thanks again
Julie V
Lovely 4 day family holiday at Big 4 Wilson’s prom. 5 kids from 5-12 yrs. They loved the beach and all the nature surrounds. Cabins very comfortable and very clean.
I really enjoyed my stay Wilson Prom holiday park , loved the whole experience of being so close to nature and the wild life woke up to the sound birds and the sea .
Great location! Big sites for caravans and amazing views
Can n tidy...

This was the second time we have camped at this site, the camp sites are great. They are surrounded by bushes, which gives you some privacy, the possum place area was a fantastic new addition, it was not there last time. Only issues that I found with the park were, the price had tripled since last time would also suggest to owners to have a summer and winter price structure to encourage more visitors during colder months. Second issue is the limit of people allowed per site we had 6 adults and 1 child which meant we had to book 2 sites, suggestion here would be have the booking system allow for more people per site by limiting each site to 6 adults and not count children eg: 4 adults and 4 kids should be able to book 1 site

Wir waren Ende Februar mit unserem SUV mit Dachzelt für eine Nacht hier. Es war unsere erste Campingerfahrung in Australien und dieser Holiday Park hat uns einen wunderbaren Start geliefert. Das Personal hat uns herzlich empfangen. Wir erhielt Camp-Site Nummer 124 mit viel Platz, ruhig gelegen und Sichtschutz zum Nachbarn. Die Sanitäranlagen und Camp-Küche waren gepflegt und sauber. Wir hatten einen schönen Spaziergang am Ufer. Ich kann diesen Campingplatz nur weiterempfehlen.
Communication was excellent, including weather warnings. Communal bins emptied regularly and staff lovely.

Lovely park.
Fantastic facilities.
Great for big mother homes.
Thankyou.
Excellent stay! Fabulous friendly staff!
Had a great time glamping
We love Inverloch Big 4; pump track, jumping pillow and pool facilities make sure the kids have plenty to do. Grounds are always beautifully kept and the staff kind and helpful.

Excellent family-friendly park in a great beach location. We went mainky for the indoor waterpark but it was too loud for our little ones. However everything else was brilliant. Superior cabins are huge, new and super clean.

We enjoyed our stay at one of the ensuite sites to the back of the park. Indoor splash park was a huge hit with the kids and swimming pall with a shallow paddling part which was perfect for our little kids. So much to do here with the jumping pillow, playground pump track basketball court and mini golf. We definitely will be back.

The caravan sites are well laid out and spacious and the facilities are clean and well maintained.
The kids facilities with indoor pool and waterpark were very popular, as were the cycle and scooter area.
The park is close enough to Inverloch to walk into town if you fancy
Wonderful family-friendly spot.
Clean, great for kids and adults alike. Staff always helpful and friendly.
Great park and great sites

Great place for the family.
Thanks for allowing us to have the same cabin as last year.
Great service and very clean.
We will definitely be back again.
Cheers

Fantastic!
We have never not met nicer people anywhere in the world!
Had a beautiful weekend away here. Great services available and comfy spot
Our first family holiday and absolutely loved it! The entire park, all of the staff and even other visitors were amazing. We are so grateful for our time here, we will be back! Thank you!
Nice place to stay, perfect location, 7 min from Paynesville. Uncomplicated check-in and check-out. Cozy.
Beautiful part of the World
Nice!
Well maintained facilities, beautiful location, fantastic team running the park, with the lake, a national park and a cafe next door.
Great views from the Kingfisher cabin and our hosts when out of their way to make our stay comfortable. Friendly welcoming hosts … 100% recommend
Nice park in a pretty spot. Clean amenities.  Dogs enjoyed swimming in the lake. And a cafe right at your door 😊 And if lucky you will see seals in the lake. We really enjoyed our stay.

Lovely & quiet, amazing bird life, wind protection.
Toilets , showers kept amazingly clean & fresh.
Great location and was delighted.
Will be back- thanks accepting us and our dog for the long weekend
Mick & Jenny.
Spent the weekend on a powered site and had the best time. Great family park with all amenities. Clean also. The owner is super friendly.
Bedroom was extremely tight fit difficult to get out bed at night,dining table felt very unsteady

We enjoyed our stay at the caravan park. We were close to the facilities and staff were friendly and helpful. It was only a short drive to other locations like Paynesville & Bairnsdale, and a bit further to Metung and Lakes Entrance.
Lovely park in a terrific location, lake and 50 acres of bushland reserve. Great for bird watching & nature walks. Nice owners keen to make you welcome. Will be back :)

Pricing is up there...
Great location...

Camped with my two boys.
Awesome time, great location, lake and park a hop skip jump away, friendly people, camp kitchen was great, fishing was good few big bream in the river and lake, seen seals in lake,  kids had a awesome time, definitely be back for weekends and holidays.
Ice relaxing place

A little gem of a park for camping. Spotless amenities. Absolutely love the little herb garden beside camp kitchen. What a fab idea and so practical!
Self check in online but quick and helpful assistance via phone when I had a couple of questions.
Can’t wait to go go back.
Relaxing park, love it.

We booked a standard caravan site and got upgraded to a premium site with water views as the park was quiet. Its a great caravan park next to Lake King. Great views and walks a Cafe on the waters edge, good clean facilities, highly recommended.

Rocky and the team at Yanakie BIG4 Wilson’s Prom were fantastic! Loved our three night stay here on a site! The amenities were very clean, we used the small camp kitchen and found it great and of course the kids loved the playgroup and basketball area. The view was phenomenal and already booked another stay as we loved it so much! Thanks Rocky and the team for being awesome! 🏕️
Fantastic place to stay. Located just outside national park so perfectly located to access Wilson’s Prom as well as surrounding areas

What a slice of heaven!
Stayed on a powered site and lucky enough to have fires this time of the year.
So peaceful - with a beautiful back drop
We’ll definitely be back!
Great campground with amazing views and large, grassy sites! Have been before and will be back. Staff is super friendly and helpful.
Great caravan park in a beautiful location. Loved being able sit around a fire during winter. Very friendly and attentive receptionist.

Absolutely stunning location. Our cabin had spectacular views of the beach and was very clean and comfortable. The team at Big 4 Wilson's Prom are so friendly and welcoming. Highly recommend staying here. Wilson's Prom is an amazingly beautiful part of Victoria. Will definitely be back.

What an absolute delight this place is. Everything feels clean and orderly and Jess who was at the office could not have been any more welcoming and helpful. The website is accurate so be aware that not all accommodation has its own bathroom, but the amenities buildings are well maintained. All in all, we’re thrilled to be here.
Stunning views, peaceful setting and great location for accessing the Prom!
LOVE this camp ground. / park. One of our favourite.  Will return again as soon as we can

Stayed here 3 nights in the Winter and park was very quiet, good facilities around (kids playground and bball hoop), about 35 mins from Tidal River so closest you can get without camping there. Nice walking trail next to the park that lends to the beach. We stayed at the Seaview Cabin and overall was decent, had everything you need. A little outdated but does the trick for a small family. No WIFI or much phone signal
Great stay - wonderful location
Very scenic location and amenities that are regularly cleaned. Would recommend.

We had a powered site right on the water.  What a view!!  Such a serene place to stay.  Amenities were a little tired but very clean and the staff on the front desk were so friendly and informative.  Would love to come and stay again.
Thanks for supporting us with a perfect site, loved your campground and will be back.
An absolutely amazing stay at Wilson’s Prom Caravan Park. Location is perfection & all amenities so well kept.

great kitchen and available services.
really nice bathrooms.
stunning location.
really great value

Staff and facilities were great. The rooms need some deep cleaning. Moldy silicon in shower and toilet, window trims moldy, behind beds dusty and mold & one of the rooms smelt awful. Would stay again just would request a cleaner room.

We found our site to be generous, fitting our 9m motorhome fine. The toilets down our end were dated and the cubicles small, but all working and cleaned daily. The roads inside the park are wide enough for two cars to pass. Short walk to the beach, fantastic location and town. Great place to base yourself for the region

absolutely appalling|Arrived on Monday only to find that the key to the bathroom doesn't work.  tried calling but left on hold so had to walk up to get the issue fixed.|Tuesday woken by loud staff members on the walkie talkies.  By late that morning they decide to do grass laying on both sides of my caravan.  by late afternoon I'm surrounded by tractors and pallets and hoses. Some guy just pops his head in the door and asks when I'm leaving as they are laying grass.|I reply to him I'm checking out Wednesday.  the reply is we'll work around you.|I'm sorry I'm paying to stay here in some measure of peace and quiet and you think its fine to do heavy maintenance around me.|Go to the office to find that the guy who popped his head in was the park manager.|Appalling management skills.  How to turn someone off ever coming again.|I would have been ok if I had known his was going to happen, maybe shift to another site but no.|Ended up coming home early as it was going to be the remainder of the day, I was going to have to put up with this.|Never been before and can safely say will never visit again.|Thanks for ruining my first holiday after the loss of my partner 11 weeks ago.

Powered site, lots of grass areas and beautiful trees attracting bird life.
Love that it's pet friendly.
Great location.
Staff amazing, so friendly and helpful.
Definitely recommend.

The surprising highlight for me was the bed. I suffer from insomnia but we were here for 3 nights and I slept really really well hahaha. The pool and water park were great too. The kids loved it and want to go back.

A great park, with lots of activities for the kids. Very close proximity to the beach and a shop near by for coffee. We had an awesome time here staying on a site with our van, will be back in the future!
Excellent ensuite site. Camp kitchen luxurious as an alternative to outside cooking in rain and cold.
We stayed on a drive-thru van site
Amazing accommodation and location with pool and water play at the park but most of all amazing, friendly, supportive staff that helped our stay with our 3month old alot easier! Thank you!
I would definitely come again for a time next time.

This park is well located and has great facilities for adults and especially the little ones.  We brought our caravan and dog, and stayed on a grassy flat site that was of a decent size.  The staff, especially Janelle, was fantastic, she was friendly and very helpful.  We were showered with advice, lollies and vouchers for ice-cream and peddle karts.  The only minor issue was the amenities block.  Whilst the showers and toilets were clean and cleaned daily, the building needed a spruce up.  There were cobwebs inside and the fly-screen was almost black with dirt. To the point where another guest had written a request to clean the screen on the cleaners check list.  But the cleaner removed the checklist and replaced it with a new one and the fly-screen remained dirty.  Small point to a otherwise great stay. Great facilities for adults and kiddies alike so much so that we are coming back with our grandson soon. Highly recommend this park.
Great kid and pet friendly park. Absolutely will be back

Lovely park for the family. Really enjoyed the water park and pool, plenty to do with kids. Our family spent alot of time in the communal hall space. The only downfall for us were the two ovens in the communal kitchen barely worked so we had to take our dinner out and use our rooms oven and bring back. Thanks for the great stay

The view was very beautiful and all rooms are great with complete equipment. The WiFi is okay. Very clean with lots of things to do, especially with the kids! 🤩👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 #view #rooms #equipment #wifi #cleanliness #activities #kids

The Cabin is lovely
And great position near my family
In caravans and tent
We come every year to inverloch
Im from cairns and come down for a time away and christmas
The only thing i would recomend is more than one t towel for 5 nights and some beach towels
But other than that the cabin was clean and tidy
Great place to stay for a family get away.
We were thre in our caravan, great location and great park, we will be going back for a longer stay

位置在威爾遜岬園區外東北角的連鎖飯店，位置獨立安靜，可安排獨棟木屋住宿或車宿，也提供簡易活動設施，以及烤肉、洗衣、烹調的場所，很方便，
離沙灘海岸很近，也可就近戲水與散步，很推薦。
Beautiful location and park.

First time coming to this lovely Park.
Wonderful service, amazing views and great large sites!
Amazing!!

Stayed at the beachside cabin.
Saw an amazing sunrise over the ocean from the room.
Saw the Milky Way for the first time with my naked eye!
Captured the Milky Way Core on my camera!
Photos uploaded are not edited straight from the camera.

What a great spot, we had two days enjoying this area in a waterfront site.  The sunrise was just magic.  Hubby enjoyed kayaking and fishing.  We went discovering the area and saw beautiful beaches, Emus, wombats and kangaroos. Highly recommend staying here.

We thoroughly enjoyed our stay here on lovely large grassy caravan site. Manager was very welcoming.  We spent 4 day's here, with most of the days at the Wilsons Prom National Park which is very close.
Nice clean amenities, c'park is right on the waterfront overlooking Wilsons Prom.
As said by another reviewer,  we did the walk on the walking trail to Duck Point from the c'park late in the afternoon which is only a lovely 2klm return walk and captured a beautiful sunset rom the Bach.
Such a special place. Lovely large private sites amongst trees and bushes, right on the water. Older amenities but clean and functional, lovely staff

Stunning camping spot, stayed on a powered site in our tent, short drive into the national park of the prom but with camp kitchen, playground and campfire facilities. Next time will go for a premium spot closer to the playground but still loved our stay. Not too busy when we went (very chilly when the sun went down, it is may after all) and no issues with other campers all very friendly and considerate. Toilet/shower block a little run down but kept impeccably clean and they are renovating the other toilet/shower block currently. Highly recommend.
We had a very pleasant stay, the park is nice amenities clean,staff are friendly and helpful.
Beautiful place to take your Campervan.   Very relaxing, peaceful, clean amenities, friendly people, pet friendly.  Highly recommend.  Will definitely be back.

Ahhhh… the Serenity!!!  Park is located right on the bay. Beautiful location and well set out. Amenities were clean and tidy. If you want to relax this is the place. Watch the sunrise and moon rise from the caravan park.
Excellent location on the water. Very peaceful. Cabin was cozy abd warm. Great cooking facilities  including an airfryer.
Camp site superb. Stay again for sure. Great people to deal with
Great location , staff were great, very easy to access.

My cabin was a bit old and sad inside don't get me wrong it was a great cabin spotlessly clean and had everything I needed its location was ideal looking over the lakes when I visit again I will ask if I can get that same cabin

Stayed for 4 nights in this beautiful park. Premium sites are large and well appointed with views overlooking Lake King. Very easy walk to lakeside cafe with our dog for morning coffee.  Managers of park are very obliging and park is well maintained with additional improvements currently being undertaken. Very beautiful setting adjacent to nature reserve and parkland. Definitely a park we will be coming back to. Thankyou especially to Kirsten.
Very awesome

Great place to stay on our way to or from Melbourne. If the coffee shop on the foreshore improved it would be brilliant!!
👍

Love Eagle Point Caravan park, nice walking area and so peaceful, staff at Eagle Point so friendly and welcoming, this was our second stay and love it even more, clean, quite, and relaxing, and surrounded by easy reach townships, will be coming back again

We had a terrific time. Such a clean and well presented Caravan park. Our first trip in our van and for our dogs too.
We will be back soon to explore some more.
A lovely caravan Park would stay again for sure 😊
Quite often have coffee in the new place over looking Eagle Point Bay
These cabins were clean with linen ect beautiful views and very roomy and comfortable in a nice quiet environment we will be staying their again

Great spot straight across from king lake, we had a superior site which was great location, camp kitchen and bbq great. Loved the herbs and lemon tree. Staff lovely and accomodating. Showers need upgrade but were always clean. Have to pick the right one but definitely nice and hot…. Think they can take some time to heat up..

Great place to stay if you like fishing I'm sure it's lovely in summer. Lorikeet was extremely clean you could smell the bleach when entering. But it was very clean warm beds warm but it's so old and in need of an update definately. We bought an oven dish from op shop as we couldn't bake otherwise. ( left it there)
Welcoming staff. Clean and tidy. Great locality.

Great location as always staff keep the site immaculate, had a great relaxing time.

One suggestion for the large powered sites would be level concrete pads would be willing to pay the extra for a dry no mud site for van and annex.

Keep up the great work.
Originally put in a site surrounded by 3 cabins. Spoke to manager who changed our site to an open site with a view. Very responsive to our request.

We stayed on a powered camping site. Lovely grassed area, fantastically clean toilet amenities. The caravan parks location is great, right on the foreshore there’s a park for the kids, cafe, walking tracks and the fauna park. Such lovely helpful staff. Will be back very soon
It was a wonderful family weekend, staff were friendly, had plenty of activities and clean facilities. Pool and water park was the family favourites.. l

Last minute booking that was available for 2 adults and a small dog in campervan, easy booking via phone as website wasn’t working (showed that it was available, but when you actually went to Click book it said unavailable). Arrived after hours, I received a text message with the plot. We were allocated to however the map at the front gate is out of date!!! so we spent a good hour trying to figure out where we were meant to stay for the night, pulled up in a spot that was listed as the same number we had on the Esplanade only to be met by disgruntled campers, we ended up walking back to the main park and found the after hours person- turned out that the after hours phone was switched to silent. Unfortunately that meant we missed out on using the indoor pool by time we had dinner first, which was such a shame for the $69 we paid. Perfect for families with kids riding around on bikes and scooters, just mind the dog poo in the road at night if walking to the bathrooms!! Toilet block and shower area (good water pressure and hot water- no payment) is a little outdated but clean enough, splash park, indoor pool and Camp kitchen looked good but we didn’t get to use.
Friendly staff, clean toilets, hot showers and well maintained sites, what more could a camper ask for.

We Stayed on a grassed powered site and had a very nice stay. The park is very family orientated and there were many family gatherings happening over the weekend.
Amenities were clean and comfortable.
The staff are very friendly and knowledgeable.
Thank you all.
Deb and Dale
Stayed twice here now. Really enjoy it. Great park.

Clean comfortable cabin.
Like the kids activities.
Appreciated the heater being on at arrival.
Liked the toiletries.
Staff welcoming and friendly.
So close to the beach.

Awesome location.
I stayed in one of the dog friendly cabins.
The garbage bin was an attraction for my dog 🤣 It would be better to have a foot operated one. But wasn't a huge issue.
Kitchen basic. Would love a steamer pot n grater.
But all in all loved my stay there with my fur kid.
Definitely worth going back ♥️

We've just returned home from our very first trip in our 'new to us' caravan with our 2 year old and kelpie. Stayed Friday-Sunday in mid November.

Each occasion I called the park prior to book and then change dates, the reception staff were nothing but helpful and friendly.

The park itself was much busier than we had expected for just a regular weekend and there were kids everywhere! You can see why though as there was so much for them to do plus being so close to Melbourne, it's a great little get away. Might not quite have the room but would be a great idea to try and fit in a walk/bike path next to the main road through the park to help keep the kids safer as did get an bit nuts watching them dart in and out amongst cars towing vans. Our son loved the slash park, pool, jumping pillow and playground and know he will love the pump track too when he's a bit more confident on his bike and scooter to keep up with the big kids.

No complaints from us and we will be back as loved how close it was to the beach (2min walk), a great little corner store with coffees and fish and chips (1min walk) Pizza, burgers, live music and ice cream (also 1min walk)
Awesome caravan park, so much for kids to do with the jumping pillow, pool and pump track.

We had a lovely week in Inverloch at Ingenia. The kids loved the pool and splash park and jumping pillow and had a ball riding around. The weather was against us and our site was grass which ended up being very muddy, completely out of anyone’s control for sure but made it difficult to get out & about. We went into reception a couple of times and had long waits, again, out of the staff’s control but when another person jumped in front of me in the line, no one had noticed that I had been waiting the longest. The doors on the splash park wouldn’t read our wrist bands and had to have help from the staff to get in. The dump point was one of the worst I’ve seen in 6 months of travel - Ingenia - it’s time to upgrade to a dump-ezy and move it to a better location rather than in the main thoroughfare of the park. Tried to use the bbqs but they weren’t hot enough and I had to transfer my plate of uncooked chicken back to our van in high winds.
Thanks for the opportunity to give feedback, looks like there’s some upgrades going on, hopefully there’s some funds going to the functionality of the park rather than packing in more and more units.

We stayed in the pet friendly cottage with a big back yard for the dogs.
Easy friendly check in, great park for kids
Pool, waterpark, mini golf, jumping pillow, skate park, games room, play ground.
It's basically on the beach.
Our only issue was not being able to get a taxi or uber into the racv resort for dinner. Make sure you book early to avoid disappointment.
All good great kitchen in room so we cooked and stayed in for a drink 🍷. Barbeque outside as well.
Had a wonderful stay in a lovely cabin. All amenities were spotless. The only minor complaint would be the 1 ply toilet paper!  9.99 out of 10 xx
Excellent service and would highly recommend
Great place for the kids. The water playground is brilliant. Pool is heated. Staff were really helpful, if you hire the buggies be prepared for a leg workout.
Fantastic park, just needs alot bigger change room in pool/ splash park.

Great size pitches for caravan. Site is clean and tidy.
Fantastic and friendly welcome on arrival.
Quiet and peaceful place to stay, all away from the huddle and bustle of everyday life.
Will return for another stay.
Highly recommended.
