We stayed in a beachside 5 berth holiday cabin. The cabins are newish & front onto Corner Inlet. Perfect for a couple but I am not sure that I would want to be in there with 5 people albeit possible to sleep 5.|Cabin is generally well appointed however could be improved with a few additional basics (salt & pepper, oil, additional garbage bags, tea towels & toilet rolls etc). A pod coffee machine would be a great addition.

Beachside cabins are newish with a fantastic view of Corner Inlet. We were a couple & the cabin was comfortable for 2 but would be squeezy for 5-6. Bedrooms are tight as is the bathroom. A few extra basics - salt & pepper, oil, additional tea towels, garbage bags & toilet rolls would be an improvement. A pod coffee machine would be a bonus.

Managers were friendly & helpful with advice on tours and walks / attractions etc
Great site and plenty of space, easy check in

Lovely location. Perfect for us, 2 adults and a small child, for 2 nights.

Good facilities. A perfect way to explore the region.
We stayed in our caravan right on the waters edge and our family stayed in a cabin also facing the water. The views were spectacular. The cabin was clean and spacious.
Great cabin & Awesome views!
Had a lovely powered camp site on the water. We will be back. 😁
Absolutely beautiful place to stay. We had. A cabin right on the water and it was amazing sitting out for a morning coffees definitely be back.

The service and location is fantastic, and the campsites are great. I only gave it four stars because it is pretty pricey for a small campsite and I did find it difficult hearing the grass cutter 2 days in a row during the day for a couple of hrs, which was disturbing when I wanted to just relax. Otherwise, the location was great.

We stayed in our camper van. They need better paths so you can get around without gathering swathes of wet grass on footwear. The bathrooms are poor.  There's not enough places to put your personal stuff while showering.  The nextdoor shower splashes under the partition so you get to share your neighbor's shower.   They need more washing machines and dryers.
Beautiful location, staff friendly. Wildlife fabulous.

Stayed in a premium beachfront site and it was stunning. Only complaint is that there is no dump site, but that's minor.
Loved it and we will be back.

Absolutely beautiful location. Breathtaking sunrises. Highly recommend a waterfront site. All the sites are huge and very private. There’s a very pretty loop walk (approximately 2.5km) from the caravan park to Duck Point.
Lovely staff. Very accommodating. A great central location to visit Wilson’s Promontory, the lovely town of Fish Creek (where Alison Lester’s gallery and bookstore is!) and the quaint little town of Foster.
Lovely place to stay nice and clean and staff are very friendly
Such a lovely peaceful park, lots of wildlife and bush walks
Clean tidy and spacious caravan park

Had a powered site and stayed for four nights.
Absolutely lovely park and facilities were so clean.
Great check in system and the park was clean and had good amenities. Highly recommend.
Stunning lake view. Most of their facilities are clean apart from the bathrooms area during the peak times. Love that they have food trucks coming in at night and coffee truck in the morning.
Great spot, large site with views of the water. Very peaceful. The dogs enjoyed a walk along the lake in the morning

Self check in does not allow you to ask questions. No keys needed for amenities which does not let you feel secure, anyone can use the amenities.
Amenities need an urgent update, hot water or lack of it wasn’t the best. Park is very outdated & needs to be looked at. Dump point needs to be looked at pressure from hose was extremely inefficient. Lack of a shop needs to be considered.
Camp kitchen needs an update. New hot plates wouldn’t go astray
Staff that we encountered were extremely lovely.
great park facilities old but clean
Really enjoyed our stay at Eagle Point friendly atmosphere and our site was roomy and had great views

Nous avons adoré passer 1 nuit dans ce camping. L’accueil est chaleureux, les sanitaires et cuisines sont fonctionnels et bien entretenus. Il y a tout ce qu’il faut, en plus le lac est juste à côté avec de jolis aménagements publics (tables, BBQ, jeux pour enfants). Le prix est d’ailleurs moins élevé que dans les campings des environs. Chers touristes et locaux, c’est une bonne adresse !
Un détail : il y a une radio dans les sanitaires, c’est peut-être simple mais c’est appréciable !

Eagle Point caravan park!!
The serenity!!! Love this place… stayed on caravan site with views of the beautiful lake.
Kirstan and Paul are great managers. Friendly. Couldn’t be as more helpful and attentive. Pet friendly, Great choices for water sport, bike riding and walking tracks. Boating and fishing.Great ammenities in and outside park. Will be back!!!

An absolute beauty this caravan park. Fastidiously maintained, wonderful atmosphere and a perfect location. We stayed there in our van with our dog and we had a very pleasant stay. Would recommend this fantastic park to all.

What a wonderful part of the world.  We have never been to Eagle Point before, and it did not disappoint.  The lovely quiet park is right on the water.  There was plenty of parking.  The cabin was very clean and the beds super comfortable.  The best part for me was there is a cafe on the beach within 50 meters with a beautiful set up and great coffee.  White the boys were out fishing I enjoyed the sunshine on the deck and admired the beautiful facilities.

We stayed for one night. Amazing place!

Clean toilets and showers, a spacious and welcoming kitchen, and a coffee shop nearby in the morning ☕️

What more could you ask for?

Spent 1 night on a powered site which was not enough. A quiet, clean and well maintained park away from the crowds at Paynesville. Has well located unpowered sites with lake views which would be our preference when we return.
Fantastic service and nice to know that the managers patrol the park regularly.  Amenities were a little outdated but clean and tidy.  Showers were excellent.  Would come again.

A great site, right on a reserve as well as the lake.  Camp sites good, but a pity about the privately owned cabins that are mostly in much need of some tlc.  Good camp kitchen, lovely views and great walks and drives.

I have now stayed twice at Eagle Point CP and am a very happy camper! First time I stayed with a friend for 4 nights. Second time for Fri & Sat night so I can get up and go to the Paynesville Market and the Howitt Park Market (Bairnsdale) as I am a stall holder there. It saved me from getting up far too early and drive over an hour to get there. This is a perfect arrangement! Thanks!
Friendly and helpful staff

BIG4 Wilsons Prom is the best! The park has huge grass sites, great facilities - including a coffee caravan that rocked up over the weekend - and the most beautiful views over Corner Inlet. The staff were super helpful and it’s just minutes drive from the entrance of the National Park! Perfection.

This was such an amazing location for a caravan park!

It had a really lovely up to date camp kitchen and a playground right near by for the kids to explore.

The staff were also fantastic with any little question I had in the lead up to our trip (relating to organizing a group of family members).

We enjoyed our stay so much that we've booked again for the same time next year.

We loved the camp site. Only pity is they had a street light right next to our site and a very bright flood light for the bathroom..which really was an eye sore. It would be great if the lights are switched off around 9-10 pm/ have path lights instead

Very disappointed over the ever increasing price increases over the past 7 years and management system.

They wanted to charge me a cancellation fee on two sites as less than 30 days notice. Yet we had not received confirmation of sites or an invoice with just a couple of weeks weeks until our stay! We always rebooked when leaving the previous Easter, so there was no excuse for lack of communications.

$70 per night for a powered site, without water, is not value for money. Power trips out in the barely equipped Camp Kitchen when too many appliances are plugged in.

Always queues for showers, unless you chose to shower in the middle of the day. You are now paying extra for the Big4 name, pricing is not representative of (lack) of facilities.

We will be taking our loyalty elsewhere. ... View

Such a beautiful location
Sites are a very generous size
Even though it was a busy Easter weekend the park felt great
Will will be back again next year
Staff also very nice

Closest accomodation I could find to the Wilson’s prom attractions outside of tidal river. The cabin we stayed in was homely and tidy. Shared facilities relatively well maintained. No complaints, would definitely stay here again

Lovely spot. Sites were nice and spacious
Fires were allowed.

It was a wonderful time while we camped in Big 4. The view was great closer to the beach. However, as the holiday park seemed busy at this time of the year, the kitchen was not in a great state at times.
But overall it was a good stay.
Great site and location.
Too many flies.

We were very disappointed with the "Tiny Home".  We were sold the booking over the phone when we rang to make enquiries about availability of a cabin. We were told the only option left was the Tiny Home.  Cabin vs Tiny Home made us believe there would be an ensuite, and a microwave etc but NO!  Yes the website explains this, but we were sold the "home" over the phone.  It was just a basic cabin on wheels! We had a lovely stay,
the view was very nice but the name of the accommodation should be changed.

Great sea view cabin and so close to the entry gate at Wilson’s Prom. It was lovely to have bedding (and spare blankets), and towels provided, and is perfect for up to 5 people. The staff were always friendly, helpful, and easy going. Coffee, tea, sugar and milk are complimentary, we forgot to bring down salt and pepper, and a box of tissues.
The staff are friendly and very helpful.  The park is right next to the beach, which has a boat ramp. So you can bring your boat there.

Myself and my son had a lovely stay over the okays. We camped out on a powered site for 4 nights. The staff were very nice and the park was very clean despite the rain and being fully booked. Great location with plenty of space. Thanks for having us!

What a great camping spot. Lovely private & large spots suitable for big & small rigs.
Right on the water, great for SUP or just taking long walks.
The amenities were clean, and plenty of hot water.
Wahine ( my dog) and I had a restful and peaceful stay.
We would love to come back.
A tip for you, get up early and experience the uninterrupted view of the sunrise.
Coffee van was a great surprise on Sunday morning too!

I was on a camping site with ensuite and unfortunately due to the awful weather, our site and tent became a mud pit. We attempted to change sites but due to the amount of mud and water, we had to abandon our stay and forfeit our remaining 2 nights. Although this was a very negative experience for my family, the staff here were nothing but fantastic, particularly Kerri-Ann. They were all understanding, friendly, patient and attempted to offer alternatives - in the end refunding the 2 nights and giving us a bottle of wine. They easily could have just said bad luck. From a very negative experience, the staff turn this into a positive and we will definitely be back. The facilities were also amazing and well maintained, certainly a great stay.

Nice place, plenty for kids to do but is on the pricier side for a caravan park.
Unfortunately we encountered terrible service - rude grounds keeper and unhelpful management which tainted our stay.

Well what a hidden gem, my wife and i plus other family members (they had a caravan on an ensuite site) stayed here for 2 nights, visiting the region from the Mornington Peninsula. We stayed in a 2 bedroom cabin that was extremely comfy annd very neat, tidy with a great layout. The staff at Big 4 Inverloch were fantastic, nothing was to hard for them. The pool is salt water and warm, indoors too and there is an awesome indoor water park for the kids to run amok in... The outdoor BBQ area is great, outside a very well stocked and spacious camp kitchen, the loaction is also very close to a great beach.
Overall a fantastic place to stay in when you next visit Inverloch, we will definetly be coming back again.

Kids loved it.
Thank you to Tanya for great friendly service.
Had a great time. Great  location.Very pleased with the cabin. The facilities at the park were fantastic. Would recommend to everyone.

We had a great stay overall. The kids loved the activities and family friendly options available. The grounds were well maintained, and the reception staff were friendly and welcoming.

The pool area could use a little extra attention with cleaning, there was some build-up in the corners and leaves etc in the bottom of the pool for days. It would also be helpful to have some in-room information about local takeaways and nearby attractions.

Otherwise, a lovely place for families and we would happily stay again.

Excellent value of money - booked for 4-days/3-nights with my family, the place is a 2-min walk to the beach which is awesome - i've enjoyed a walk with coffee. the place was perfect with our toddlers especially during overcast weather they've got to enjoy indoor amenities- so much fun with the heated splash pools, inflatable bounce, trike, etc. We went to variety  of beaches nearby. It exceeded my expectation as its close to everything perfect for travelling families.

Absolutely beautiful place to stay.
Kate at reception was an absolute legend, so helpful.
Another fantastic family weekend at Ingenia Inverloch
Loved our stay there on the lake.  Our TV Didn't work at all but we survived and next time there won't be a problem.

I needed a place to stay overnight that was pet friendly as I was travelling with my dog. The cabin was perfect for my needs, as well as for my dog, with the large, well-fenced courtyard for her to run around safely.
The cabin was so clean and fresh, so I immediately felt relaxed. Overall it was a really great place to stay.
Fabulous family park.  So much for the kids to do. Staff were so accommodating and friendly.  Highly recommend.
Really great parks for kids and family’s and close to town and beach ect

Nice and secure for the kids, pity all the games in the games room need payment. Rooms are neat and tidy, showers are tiny so don’t book if you like to shave your legs cause you’ll end up tangled in the shower curtain on the floor. Great location, lovely town. Kind of wish they had night staff at the park to shut the hooligans up.
Mattress were very uncomfortable dishwasher didnt work other then that amazing service was fast and on to things when needed would cone back
the facilities are absolutely amazing such a fantastic family friendly spot
Brand new cabin. We love the place

Great park and facilities, when allocating a site for a 22 foot van access needs more consideration.
Would recommend this park to others.
wonderful place❤️
Such a great spot lovely team of people campsites were decent size and danced apart nicely and definitely group friendly will go again

We booked a beach front cabin and it was absolutely gorgeous. So comfortable, spacious, immaculately clean and beautifully furnished. The whole park has a really lovely atmosphere. Will definitely return!

This is a great caravan park. The guests are awesome, extremely kid friendly. There are no loud, selfish or obnoxious guests. We went in April and it was delightful. The camp kitchen, basketball hoop and toilets are the standout features. The toilets in particular are lush considering we're camping in a park. Will do it again. Everything was perfect.

We parked our caravan and enjoyed a spacious well kept site.  The new facilities are great and well maintained.   Everything is at your fingertips to make your holiday very comfortable and enjoyable 👌
Cabin was spacious and modern with a water front view.

What a beautiful place, it grew on us the whole weekend long and by the time we had to leave I wanted to stay forever! The wind can blow here, the trick is to get a sheltered spot deeper in the park where there is grass on the ground. In site 5 we had a caravan and tent and it was so private we were hardly aware of both the wind and other people. It felt like camping deep in the bush! Good ablutions with lots and lots of hot water. Only one car allowed in but the overflow parking is right next to the park and easily accessible. The General Store up the road has everything, even cough syrup and as a base to explore the Prom it is hands down better than that crowded campground at Tidal River... One of our favourites now. Thanks!!!

Despite the cold weather at this time of year, I enjoyed my stay at the Park. I loved how clean the site was, and I loved walking along the beach, taking a stroll whenever I could. The people respected each other and made friendly conversation which elevated the experience. And I especially loved spotting the wild animals at the Park.

The camp site was amazing. I loved the recreational areas like the kitchen, the playground and the ice creams we often bought while staying. The Park had awesome views, which created a quiet and serene time for us. Alongside the constant upholding of cleanliness to the site and amenities, we'd love to come back again.
The best sheltered caravan site in a beautiful setting!

I was very concerned that one night people near us played extremely loud music till 4!!!! in the morning along with yelling out, shouting etc. I wanted to call reception but didnt have an emergency number - the number i needed was on the door of reception!!!??? There was no way i was leaving my van as i was feeling very unsafe. The phone number needs to be on the information page that is given to people staying there!! In an emergency going to reception is not always an option. I was also disappointed in that there was rubbish in the hedges and bushes, not a lot but for such a supposedlly pristine and special area of our country, it is disappointing to see. We cleaned up what we saw.
One of the most beautiful spots we have ever camped. Big beautiful sites, well maintained, natural bush, stunning views. Great facilities. Highly recommend.
Very good park in a great location. A variety of sites matching most needs, facilities pretty good - very much a classic caravan

We paid for a premium Seaview site, gorgeous views but if the wind blows you will feel it. For the price we paid which was not cheap the site was large but very basic.
Facilities were tired but clean.
The park is a long way from places to eat out but close to Yanakie General Store which has a little supermarket and basic stuff you need.
Beach at park is very tidal with lots of seaweed and really not a swimming beach if tide is out.
It’s a rustic park, but its biggest bonus is that its close to many beautiful places to visit like Wilson’s Prom.

Our site was cosy and close to the water, so we could hear it at night and take a short walk to explore the shore. Such a beautiful setting. Loads of birdlife and trees, I’m glad we didn’t get in to the national park campground. It was only 5 minutes to the NP entrance and 40 to Tidal River. There was an issue with a power cut one night and a text was sent out immediately, at 3am and emergency generators started up to provide minimal light in the amenities. By early morning it was on again, but out of the park’s control. We’d stay here again without hesitation and recommend it highly. The amenities are old but clean. Laundry and lines were excellent.

Booked a cabin for two nights as a base for us to explore the park from. Was disappointed on arrival as initially they couldn't find the reservation, then thought we had a caravan, before finally sorting out the cabin that had been booked. The staff were friendly but it didn't give a good first impression. The cabins are basic but serviceable although if you want to do much other than microwaving food then you are a bit stuck. The location was the best part of it, but this was the most expensive place that we stayed across ten days, and at the same time the most disappointing. I am sure that the only reason these prices can be charged is due to the actual location.
Great customer service. We had a last minute change of plans but the management team was very supportive and accomodating. Clean facilities, well labeled sites, good vibes. Much recommended

We stayed in a tent for one night while travelling. What a superb spot for a caravan park. The sunrise was absolutely spectacular!! The conservation area next door was a treat. Our whole experience was seamless and so peaceful. Would love to explore a lot more. Amenities were top notch.

We have just spent 2 nights at the park and thoroughly enjoyed our stay.
We stayed in a cabin that looks over the lake,  such a great spot.
Thank you Kirsten and Paul for welcoming us to the park.
The grounds and the facilities are so well looked after.
We will certainly be back 🙂🙂
Great little find, quiet location and cafe on site
Great caravan park near the water. Staff very friendly. Amenities good.

Park in a good location, not far from the lake. Fairly inpersonable  with contactless check in. Very basic set-up. Needs modernising. Hard to find a rubbish bin. Noisiest flock of cockatoos in the tree branches above, i've ever heard. Lots of permanent on-site set-ups. Severe road dips driving in to the park.
Nice quiet location to park your caravan and explore the near by attractions like lakes entrance, paynsville, bairnsdale and all the other small towns
We were extremely happy with our stay, in such a beautiful location. Kristen was fantastic and very kind and friendly  and it was great value for money. We will definitely be back again soon

Stayed in a cabin for $200 a night..was greeted by lots of small cocroaches scurrying back into the cracks they came from was the worst accommodation I've ever experienced..very old and outdated holes in the walls where something once hung a clothes hook hanging on one screw ..check out the photos someone else put up it tells the story!!

Our first time at eagle point caravan park over the Australia Day long weekend. We thought the price was very fair in comparison to other parks and the location is fantastic, that’s where the positives end. The toilet and shower facilities were filthy, not sure how often they were cleaned but they aren’t cleaned very thoroughly. Out of our 3 nights we only had a warm shower once, the 2nd night we had to have cold showers, when we questioned it we were told to just run the water for 5 minutes (it didn’t work) and on the 3rd we found the water wouldn’t regulate at warm, it was either hot or cold. There is no shop like advertised on the website, fortunately there was a small cafe down on the foreshore. Our campsite was a dust bowl with no grass, situated inbetween permanent sites, leaving little room for our car to be parked and we felt boxed in. Whilst this park might suit those stopping by for the night or those with vans, it’s not kid friendly, there is no park or pool on site and the facilities are not clean.
Our suggestion would be to improve cleaning of the facilities and more regularly, grow grass on all sites and consider investing in kid friendly activities on site.
It was raining hard the night we arrived, so we had to share a kitchen, which was a great experience. Staff is amazing, amenities were clean, park is specious. Loved it!

I am usual someone who will give a place a red hot go. I booked a premium site via Hipcamp. First time using the app and to be honest I was disappointed and found somewhere else to stay. I booed a premium unpowered site. The picture on Hipcamp showed green grass, trees and next to the water. The site its self was not close to the water, not a tree in sight and a dust bowl!

Edit after business reply:
As new management, it’s fantastic (not) to see that all you took from my review is the comment about price. No care for the cleanliness and camper experience. I’ve seen a lot more “happy campers” in previous years.

Original review:
Very disappointed this year. My family and friends have been coming here over the Christmas/NewYear break since 2009. There has been a change of management and it’s gone downhill.
For a powered site, this year it’s $62 a night and it’s going up to $112 a night next year. For that price, I expect them to knock down and provide new toilet/shower facilities and replace the water park/pool that they knocked down years ago.

New management on first meeting, came across as rude and unwelcoming.

Toilet/shower facilities are dirty and toilet paper/soap aren’t stocked.

Camp sites have no grass and are practically dirt and dust.

Not enough bins located around the caravan park.

No kiosk where you can buy milk, bread, ice or the newspaper like it was for the past 15 years.

I’m not sure how much longer we’ll be coming back, but steer clear and go to one of the other caravan parks.

Nice camp ground the powered and non powered sites are fairly big, enough for your tent and car, there is a reserved space for cars if you can't fit it in.
The facilities are a little dated, partially renovated, but in general good order, dog friendly park, there were several dogs around but still peaceful minimal disturbance.
Right on the waters edge, just outside the park there's a small cafe with a limited assortment of food options, mainly cakes and treats to have with your coffee, does have some simple pastry items, it looks out over the water (grass beach very little sand) and a small pier (hit and miss fishing spot).
All in all a great little holiday park my only issue was our site was very dusty basically dirt with very little grass, although other sites were lush and green.
One warning the bugs at night can be extreme so bring insect deterring lights (red,orange,yellow) white lights will have you swarmed with beetles and bugs on some nights. see pics. On the upside not too many mozzies.

Disappointed no internet even though you look on there site says internet wrong . We had to leave on the second day even though we paid for 3 nights went and told them & guess what no refund no internet.

Eagle Point Caravan Park, located near the Gippsland Lakes, offers a variety of outdoor activities such as fishing, swimming, and water skiing. The park features beautiful walking tracks and is situated close to the heritage-listed Silt Jetties, making it a popular spot for nature enthusiasts. Facilities include family-friendly swimming areas and opportunities for kayaking and windsurfing.
Campground is from my point of view not to recommend... sorry! Sites sometimes grassy sometimes muddy, amenities :no comment! Fees not too expensive.
Had a powered site with ensuite. Park well laid out and very tidy. Plenty for kids to do.

We stayed in our caravan at an excellent drive through site. The surfaces were perfect for the weather we encountered. Facilities were very good and the staff friendly and helpful. This is an excellent park and our stay was enjoyable.

Booked the Eco Tent for hubby's 40th and we had a lovely relaxed time, with plenty to keep our 5 year old busy in site. Appreciated the bottle of wine left in the room also.

Only suggestion is to increase amount of hooks to hang towels on!

This was our first time staying at this park. We definitely will stay again! Very friendly staff. Whenever you pass any of the staff you're greeted with a smile. The park itself was absolutely amazing!! The grand children loved the indoor splash park, pool, jumping pillow and the go carts. Very well kept and clean.
Thanks for our wonderful stay!
The most amazing place to getaway with the whole family!!! Everyone was catered for from grandkids to great grandma. Facilities and staff were top notch. Nothing was a problem.
Very nice stay great location easy to get to Philip Island for MotoGP
Fantastic stay in a caravan drive through staff and location as well as site fab
Very nice place and staff beach just a walk away can’t wait to bring my kids here
I mean, do you need anything else? Plenty of space, clean amenities and access to the beach.
We had a great time during our stay! The amenities and services were great!
Very friendly staff. Very clean park with lots of amenities and great location for exploring the area.

Stayed in own van. Slightly tight fit, ( we have large van. Great facilities. Fantastic for kids.heaps of activities for them. Extremely clean facilities and grounds.
Highly recommend
Lovely Park with awesome facilities and lovely helpful and friendly staff 👏🏻☺️
Fabulous family holiday - 4 families - we rented two cabins and two caravan sites with ensuites

Great park and facilities for kids, however not been able to park 2 cars at a cabin when have 2 young families staying is very annoying. Every time you are heading in and out of the park you have to car shuffle and always leave one car parked on the road. Doing this with babies and toddlers is a pain. Reluctant to stay again because of this stupid rule.
Amazing location. Had a wonderful time during our two night stay. It's just 30 mins away from Tidal River. The amenities were really good and The kitchen was clean and convenient.
Camped on a beautiful site overlooking the bay. Stunning sunrise.
Return campers to this fabulous location and park.
Like the park but we found that we needed to drive out of it to visit places or do some shopping.
非常好的露营地，设施干净、工作人员热情、风景优美。

Really helpful staff.
The park is clean really good campsite with a view of the sunrise. We would stay there agin in a heart beat. All guests were nice and helpful it was amazing.

A very well run park with very clean amenities.
Would recommend.
Great location and access to Wilson Promontory National Park and Welshpool for departures and return, of the full day cruise, which was outstanding.

Very peaceful place to stay. Mostly the caravan park was nicely maintained and the sites were large and neat. The amenities were very old and basic, it felt a bit like school camp! They were clean though, which is all that matters. We will definitely return as it is in such a lovely spot.

We thoroughly enjoyed our stay at Wilsons Prom Holiday Park. The natural beauty of our surroundings made for a peaceful and relaxing getaway. The campsites were maintained, spacious, and offered a great balance of privacy and convenience. The facilities were clean, and the staff were friendly and helpful, ensuring a comfortable and enjoyable experience. I highly recommend booking your stay here and look forward to returning for another great stay!
Perfect position for discovering Wilson’s Prom. Parked in amongst the bushes, so your spot is private. Definitely would come again if in the area.

Had a great couple of days here with our pet. Just outside the national park so great location. Got site 27 which was right at the end and very secluded. Almost like our own little beach next to us. Just make sure you are self contained as water isn’t readily available and the amenities are far from this site, that said, we were happy with our far away one and the amenities were quite good when we did head down to them.

Convenient location near entrance to Wilson's Prom National Park, many of the caravan sites are private leafy alcoves and
Clean amenities. Friendly helpful reception staff.. It's all good.
Alex V

I would not recommend this place! Our first issue was the lack of a cancellation policy anywhere on the website or in our confirmation email. When I tried to cancel a week before, they said we were too close to the dates to cancel. Even when I tried to explain that there was no cancellation policy anywhere on the website, they refused! So we had to go and we had a terrible time!

We stayed in a cabin and the walls were paper thin, you could hear everything outside. And the campsites are packed on top of each other so there was a massive family literally right behind our bedroom wall. We could hear them all night!

There is also no WiFi or cell phone service, and no oven anywhere on the premise. This was not communicated to us prior to our arrival. They had a couple of air fryers in the community kitchen but no baking sheets so that did us no good. You have to bring your own baking sheets, not oven pans!

We ended up leaving a day early because we were getting absolutely zero sleep with all the noise and there was no water. When we complained to the front desk, they truly could not care less. Terrible customer service! Especially for the price, this was awful!

Loved our waterfront site in our caravan. The views are spectacular. Tge sun rises over the Peninsula and water in front of you. Bathrooms are a little old but they were very clean. Staff were friendly and helpful.  It does get very windy at times so keep an eye on the weather forecast. The bird life is beautiful.  Local general store is well stocked.  I'd be happy to come again. Just remember that there is no dump point.

Very friendly staff. Really clean and lovely Campsite. We had a powered site with our compervan for 56$ a night.
Definetly recommended. :D
Lovely new cabins a little fine-tuning was needed. We had to fix the toilet seat and the kitchen tap was not properly installed. Also we feel it lacked platters, cheese knife and champagne glasses.
We have had a great stay again will do it again for sure
Cabin was clean but the only thing I can complain about would be that we booked a two bedroom cabin and got a one bed cabin but had a great break away from home  👍👍👍👍

일출이 너무 아름다운 곳이에요.
조용하고, 키친은 깔끔하고 늘 정리정돈이 잘 되어있어요. 화장실과 욕실 시설은 좀 오래되긴했지만 따듯한물도 잘 나오고, 청소를 잘해놔서 사용하는데 지장없습니다.

Lovely space, big lots. Had 10 people over 2 lots and had more than enough room.
Was quiet and not far from Wilson’s prom.

Great stay again…. Only feedback, would be good if the bathroom:shower facility doors can be improved, to stop banging. Very annoying at night. Apply a gas strut of some sort.

Mobile phone range extremely poor, with the increase site pricing, should consider to improve, WiFi?
Clean facilities and the best kitchen area I have stayed at for a long time well done very helpful staff

Over Labor Day weekend, the office staff were consistently friendly and eager to assist us whenever needed. It's truly our go-to spot to relax and unwind. Having a coffee van at the park for the weekend was a great addition.
Very clean amenities very helpful and friendly staff

Great location close to the Promotory
Very friendly staff
Everything about our stay at the Prom was wonderful

Well....
I think we turned up on the windiest day of the year. But we were only 15 metres away from the ocean.
The sunrise the next morning was breathtaking,  great position, facilities and friendliness.
Very nice caravan sites and very well kept amenities
Nice beach view,have toilets and showers, clean tidy camp site, nice size block for setting up tent, great fishing around the bay,friendly staff.  Would come again in the summer time.
beautiful spot on a clear sunny day! Nice & tidy, great facilities. The owners are lovely. Cheers definitely be back
Beautiful  outlook ! Dimmies were nice too

So glad we stayed here|Had the best spot in the park overlooking the water|Facilities being upgraded shortly but very clean|Laundry prices cheapest we've come across|And the Cafe views and coffee terrific |The customer service was excellent,| nothing was too much trouble

So we thought we would come back here for the long weekend and what a disappointment it is now. Pay for what is called premium, premium (big sites, non powered and a dirt pit😔). The water front, oh wait water front it's now replaced with a car park then the lake. Was the upgraded office really worth it, I'll leave that up to you. We won't be back or recommending.
Lovely Caravan Park on the water- lake King. Amenities clean and accessible. Easy stroll to a cafe and great kids park. Dog friendly.

Direkt am Lake gelegen, saubere gepflegte Einrichtungen. Freundliches Personal.
Einkaufsmöglichkeiten u.a. in der Nähe.
The Hub has great coffee, tea cakes with friendly staff. The view is lovely over the lake. Worth a visit.
thank you for Supporting the Scouts as we set up our new Eagle point site, the new foreshore building and playground are great.
Nice coffee
Awesome caravan park, used to stay here with family quite a lot until we left the park, it was an experience to visit again :)
Great park for kids. Well maintained
Great family friendly stay!
We had a great stay. Water park and pool were excellent. Other amenities were standard Big 4 and good. Games room an expensive, underwhelming visit but overall fantastic.
Amazing stay with our family of 7. Something for everyone, from toddlers to teenagers. Cabin was perfect, new and clean. Can not fault.
What a wonderful place to stay at Ingenia Inverloch ....so much for the kids to do , great amenities,  friendly helpful staff . We can't wait to return
Was a great cabin just disappointed that there was no early check in or late check out for the $310 for one night 3 pm check in and 10 am check out is a lot money
We thought it was ok for the price you pay the kitchen  could of been cleaner
Wonderful reception from ladies at check in so friendly  polite & helpful.

We had an amazing week in the lv poolside cabins. Defiantly will be booking this accommodation again! Staff were amazing especially the maintenance crew and pool supervisors! Areas were always clean and the food trucks were tasty!!!!
People at reception made us welcome and adjusted dates when I couldn’t travel 3 months ago due to illness. Very helpful.

NOT happy at all! i had a cabin there for nearly 10 years and it was my whole childhood and i absolutely adored the place, until igenia took over and gave us 30 days notice to pretty much get out and demolish our cabin or move it else where we spent so much money on renovating, and all the fees all year round. they wanted to get rid of us and many other cabin owners for ‘campsite land’. many cabins like ours who had so many memories with our families and money lost because of igenia. igenia had no sympathy what so ever.

Booked a cabin with porta cots but the rooms couldn’t fit porta cots at all, asked to be near the kids area upon booking 6 weeks prior and got stuck as far away as possible, fridge had mould growing in it. We have stayed at other big 4’s but they weren’t this bad.

I'm not usually one to complain but wasn't happy at all with the stay. To start with cabin was smaller than what I saw in pictures, on top of that the room wasn't at all that clean..  it even had a prerogative msg under the bunk bed slat that my 6 year old saw..
I had a 6 year old n 15 month old so maybe wasn't ideal for our family.. I thought being holidays there wouldve been activities for indoor being that we were stuck in the room while it was raining.. Ended the trip early and couldn't wait to get home.. i don't think ill be back..

We recently stayed for 2 nights is budget cabin and it was great. All you need and kitchen has everything to prepare meals. Lots to do for the kids. I had some trouble over the phone making some changes to a booking but this was resolved when I called back the next day and spoke to a different person.
The only thing we felt could be improved is the pool temperature and the splash park was even colder.

Wow what can I say! Fantastic place well equipped
Beautiful well appointed cabins and facilities and clean too!  These guys where fully booked and under the pump but always very happy and ready to help, the splash park and pool where a huge hit with my kids, will definitely be back here and soon.

Big 4 Wilsons Promontory Holiday Park is an excellent choice for travelers looking for a comfortable and convenient base near Wilsons Promontory National Park. Located just a short drive from the park entrance, this holiday park offers easy access to the stunning landscapes, wildlife, and breathtaking beaches that make Wilsons Prom such a popular destination.

The park itself is well-maintained, with a range of accommodation options, including powered and unpowered campsites, cabins, and safari-style tents. Facilities are clean and modern, with well-equipped camp kitchens, BBQ areas, and laundry services, making it ideal for families and groups. The playground and recreational spaces ensure that kids are entertained, while the peaceful surroundings allow for a relaxing stay.

One of the best aspects of this holiday park is its proximity to Wilsons Prom’s incredible beaches, such as Squeaky Beach and Norman Beach, where visitors can enjoy crystal-clear waters, white sand, and scenic coastal walks. Wildlife lovers will also appreciate the chance to spot wombats, kangaroos, and emus within the park.

Overall, Big 4 Wilsons Promontory Holiday Park is a fantastic option for those seeking both comfort and adventure, offering a great balance of nature, relaxation, and convenience.

Great location.
No water available in the kitchen on our last day. Didn’t see any staff communication or helping guests around this issue. Disappointed about not allowing a second car on premises. I understand the safety of it all if everyone had a second car but realistically not many guests would require a second car. On our weekend there were only an extra 10 cars parked outside.
A group of us stayed in our caravans for 3 nights, great location, park is being upgraded as a work in progress under new owners and management but clean and tidy and no complaints from us

Very accommodating staff with excellent customer service. The cabin was clean, tidy and reasonably well appointed. Our only gripe is that one of the bedrooms was a little tight to get in/out of bed. Otherwise, great.

We had a wonderful time at the park and within an easy drive to all attractions at Wilson’s Pro. Could not fault anything with our 4 day stay. The Yanakie Caravan Park was a great choice for our stay. Thankyou😀
Absolutely wonderful experience.
Down to earth accommodation. Great staff

Magnificent location!!! So much better than National park, lots of space, greenery, view was amazing!!!! Facilities really good!! Lovely kitchens and play equipment for kids.
We had a great time!
Our site view was amazing. Thank you so much.
Good caravan park, a bit far from Tidal River, but also much quieter & relaxed.

Helpful staff went the extra mile to return items inadvertently left behind. Lovely, clean accommodation with exquisite view, just metres from the water. Loved it. Will be back. Can not recommend highly enough.
Very noisy campers nearby partied until podafter midnight ignoring park rules. Amenities not up to standard expected and barely adequate for a full park. Layout of sites very good.
Great camping spot! Quiet, large site, close to walks, perfect! Reasonable price!

Die Anlage ist sehr schön. Wir hatten genug Platz und die Privatsphäre ist gegeben. Die Sanitäranlagen waren sauber einen Minuspunkt gibt es und deshalb auch nur 3 Sterne. Die Anlage ist schon etwas älter, in den Duschen ist keine Ablage. Für ältere Herrschaften kann dass eine Sportliche Herrausforderung sein. Die Waschmaschinen nur auf kaltes Wasser fanden wir auch nicht so optimal. Ansonsten ist das gesamte Gebiet für Wanderfreunde bestimmt ein Traum, den Campingplatz können wir weiterempfehlen.
Nice place when summer

It didn’t occur to me that a powered site wouldn't have water. No, I didn't read the specs before booking. Without water, it's a basic, though spacious site. Old,  tired amenities, narrow shelf and one hook in shower.
Large grassy area near camp kitchen.
What a beautiful place to kick back and relax - both before and after a bushwalk at Wilson's Prom. Tree therapy at its best.

My cabin was situated in a quiet place in the caravan park.  It was clean, comfortable and  had excellent heating and hot water.  The bed was comfortable too!   I enjoyed the privacy of the  verandah out the front even though it was rather chilly.
Loved our stay at Seaspray Caravan Park.  Friendly owners, lovely grassed flat sites, immaculate a modern amenities. Highly recommend!
Very modern facilities. Excellent service and great owners. Plenty of space available on each site. Short walk to take away or the surf club. Plus its right on the 90 mile beach.

Nicely appointed caravan park, good showers and toilets. Has shared cooking area with hot water and general lounge area.

About 100m walk to the beach

Offers after hours checking via a drop box

No Dedicated ev charging in Seaspray, caravan park considering installing ev charge points. Charge at Sale or Traralgon  before driving into seaspray. Also no fuel past Longford.

Plenty of on-site units for sale as of April 2026.

Stayed for 5 nights on a powered site over the Easter period.

The sites were generous and location was great with a very short walk to the beach.

The bathrooms were ALWAYS clean and one of the cleanest we’ve stayed at.

Thank you ☺️

We have been camping here regularly for over ten years now and have always thoroughly enjoyed our stay. This year was unfortunately different. While the facilities are still great and the sites are well maintained and comfortable, no longer being allowed a fire made a huge difference to comfortability. We understand restrictions however there was not a total ban, and according to the CFA guidelines fires were permitted as long as they complied with rules, but to be told two days before our trip we can’t have a fire under any circumstances meant we had two uncomfortably cold nights. Additionally not all who booked were informed, only one party out of the three.

Then to add to that, after so many years of using our same sites with our regular booking, to be told that someone else has booked them now without checking with us first felt really rude. We understand first in best dressed, but I guess loyalty doesn’t mean much. We’ve never had an issue in the past with knowing our site is secured in our name, and reconfirming with payment upon leaving. This time we were told sorry, too bad. Will be looking for a different spot for next year, which is a shame after over a decade of happy memories.

We are annual comers and the place always welcomes our big family!! They’re always so accomodating and helpful! The place is nice and clean, well maintained. The beach is walking distance and what an added bonus to hear the waves while you’re sleeping. The place is well secured so it feels safe for the kids.
Very well managed and set out. Sites are grassy and amenities very clean.
No fires anymore, no cooking on charcoal until May 1, what a shame on those cold seasprsy days and nights to not be allowed a fire.
Wonderful, clean caravan park in a gorgeous little coastal town. Loved the beach and had a great meal at the life saving club down the road. Will definitely be back!

Toller Park! Wir waren zwei Nächte dort. Es war nicht viel los und wir hatten unserer Ruhe :) Tracy und ihr Mann sind super nette Menschen und extrem hilfsbereit. Wir haben uns direkt wohl gefühlt. Der Strand ist wunderschön und menschenleer und der General Store auf jeden Fall einen kurzen Abstecher wert!
Toiletten und duschen waren super sauber!

Absolutely loved Seaspray Caravan Park!
The grass was so lush on our site and the site itself was huge!
We stayed 3 nights and were thrilled at how beautiful the beach is. The beach was flagged with surf life savers on duty.
Facilities are modern and clean and the park is right on the beach !
I loved the offer of a separate toilet for children with its own fob for secure entry.
The whole park is very clean and well managed start to finish.
The kids playground equipment looked new and well maintained and there was a great kitchen amenities / BBQ block.
Seaspray town has a lovely surfclub overlooking 90 mile beach and a cute general store - both venues had great afternoon live music  on Sunday.

This is the best caravan park we have ever stayed in. The lush green lawns are well maintained, amenities are in immaculate condition. I have never seen a park so clean and tidy. My only suggestion is there are no trees to offer shade from the sun or the strong winds coming straight from the ocean. We had a powered site close to everything. Highly recommend.

Lovely caravan park, friendly staff and facilities were best I've seen in a long time. Family friendly, kids can ride bikes, play games, play on playground, and of course it's close to the surf club for safe beach swimming.

We stay Australia weekend so while the camping ground was pack, we had no issue with noise.

We had a great time camping out at the powered site. The facilities are great, cleaned regularly and huge camp kitchen makes cooking very convenient. The indoor swimming and splash area, basket ball court and gaming rooms keeps the kids occupied and lets the big kids have a good time as well. Only disappointment was not able to use the EV chargers. One was not working and other one is next to a cabin which is reserved for the guest. So if you are not staying in the cabin, you can’t use the charger. I Pulger in my car as the spot was empty, only to be told by move my car. Overall great experience. Love to come back.

We loved our holiday! So did our kids suchh a fantastic park! This is the second time we have stayed there. Loved the pizza truck and comby drink vans it added a bit of extra fun for tge adults while the kids played

We were so disappointed in our stay here. We booked back in March for a stay with our extended family and were really looking forward to our week together. While we expected mud due to the rain, the ground maintenance has been seriously neglected making more issues than necessary. No lawns were mown, no holes were filled and no drains were cleaned which just resulted in more water pooling on our sites.
Also, there needs to be some enforcement of park rules. So many children riding bikes/carts were in traffic and were unsupervised. We nearly ran over one poor boy! It really was chaos up the main camping road with kids going everywhere.
Although the staff are lovely and the location is awesome, obviously the inflated price we paid to stay is clearly not being invested back into the park

Stayed 2 nights on an ensuite site. Ensuite was nice, clean and warm. Close to esplanade and town. Noisiest park we have ever stayed in. Kids riding bikes which would normally not be a problem except they had plastic bottles jammed in between the frame and wheel to sound like an electric bike. Noise was constant and deafening from 7am until after dark. Also hot water kept running out in camp kitchen when washing dishes. Worst camping experience ever.

Great site location and size, P12 is a powered Ensuite site within view of the park and easy reach of the splash park and pump track, great for families with kids. Check in was quick and easy especially given it was a busy long weekend check in day, staff are friendly and welcoming. Camp location steps to the beach and easy walking distance to town. Looking forward to coming back next time .

Very overpriced. The water park is tiny in comparison to photos and disconnected to the very small pool. The games room has nothing free the kids can play everything costs on top of very expensive park fees. The ensuites are tiny and so far away from where you position your van. No towels are provided either. There’s no shade covers on the park the entertainment at the park is completely reliant on weather whilst being located in an area that gets rain majority of autumn, winter spring. Honestly just save your money you can get an air bnb cheaper then a cabin here the entertainment is not worth it, this park design was poorly thought out for functionality

As cleaning business owner with extremely high standards I was so incredibly impressed how every inch of our cabin was beautifully cleaned, no dust, smelt amazing and fresh, the shower and sinks were all incredibly clean.

The activities for kids were also just beyond amazing the games room was a mini arcade. The water park was just so much fun ive never seen my son so happy and in his element. The staff were sooo helpful, and friendly. Professional yet super welcoming and made you feel at home and so comfortable. The park itself was so beautifully maintained. Everything looked like it was constantly stayed on top of. I even saw staff wiping down the jumping pad after it had rained for kids to use. The level of care that goes into this place exceeds far beyond 5 stars. If yo I are looking for a family getaway where you can relax and enjoy the park itself with all the activities then this is the place for you and your kids. We truly cant thank big4 imgenia inverloch enough for making this everything we wanted and more.

First and foremost, the cleanliness of the park was outstanding. The grounds were immaculately maintained, and the facilities were spotless. The amenities were modern and convenient, making my camping experience all the more enjoyable. From the moment I arrived, the team at Eagle Point Caravan Park went above and beyond to ensure my stay was pleasant. That said, the luxury safari tent itself was fantastic. We’ve stayed in the same style of tent at another site and loved it just as much here. The electric fireplace adds a cozy, upscale touch and really makes it feel like true glamping. Despite the very minor hiccups, the kids had a blast - especially in the pool - and we’d happily return for another stay 🥰

We spent the Australia Day long weekend at Wilsons Prom Holiday Park with friends. We had 2 powered campsites for our tents.
The location is stunning, with beautiful views and plenty of natural beauty, but unfortunately, our overall experience wasn’t great.

What we liked:
•	The campsites were spacious, so we had plenty of room to set up.
•	The park’s surroundings were absolutely gorgeous, with incredible views and a peaceful atmosphere.

What we didn’t like:
•	The flies were unbearable during the day, making it almost impossible to use the campsites. While flies are common in summer, it felt like the park could have done more to manage this issue.
•	The power inlets were really far from our site, which made setting up inconvenient.
•	There weren’t enough toilets, and one side of the campground didn’t have any at all, meaning a long walk for anyone staying there.
•	The communal kitchen was too small, with just four small fridges that were dirty.
•	There were only a few BBQs, and just one of them was working.
•	Each campsite was limited to one car, which was frustrating for group trips.
•	Although the website said firepits were available, we received an email two days before our stay saying fires weren’t allowed, even though there wasn’t a fire ban in place.

Overall, while the views and location are amazing, the poor facilities and lack of proper maintenance made our stay less enjoyable. We wouldn’t be rushing back anytime soon.
