**Be aware of excessive charges by making minor changes to your booking**

This caravan park may very well be a nice place to stay especially with kids, however you must be aware of unreasonable charges if you choose to change the dates of your booking, change from and standard site to an ensuite, change your cabin type, or reduce your one week stay by a day. In all of these cases you’ll be charged a $50 “administration” fee to make such a change as per the terms & conditions hidden in the fine print.

These charges are deemed fair & resonable by management based on their previous experience in working in hotels and restaurants. However in our many years of camping and in particular staying in excess of 80 caravan parks in the last 15 months have we never once been charged to make one of the changes listed above. Every caravan park has been fair & reasonable, have appreciated our business, until now.

Management imply that these charges are fair and that their customers are accepting of them? I don’t know anyone who would be happy to be charged $50 to change the date of their 3 day getaway, to reduce their 3 week booking by a day, or to swap sites or cabins to something more accomodating.

In our case, we provided 7 weeks notice for our stay in an off peak period to BIG4 Traralgon to reduce our 5 night stay to 4 nights and were soon reluctantly phoned by the reception staff to inform us that we would be charged a $50 administration fee and would therefore receive a refund of $30 instead of the full rate of $80. We were informed that this could be used as credit towards a future booking, thing is we are from Perth so that’s not a practical option for us.

Therefore we are being penalised over 60% of the single nights tariff that we no longer required, yet we’re still committed to the existing 4 nights (or 80% of our original booking) worth $300. So they would rather penalise you and create conflict and then still expect you to stay? Another way to look at it is, that we were penalised the same amount to take one day off our booking as someone cancelling their whole two week booking?? What part of that equation provides a fair & reasonable customer service experience?

Australian consumer law exists to prevent such unreasonable charges such as these examples, but management of this caravan park are completely oblivious to how their policy negatively impacts those customers who need to make minor changes to their bookings.

I write this review hoping that management will review their policies for the sake of future guests wanting to make minor changes to their bookings, as not everything works out as planned, providing 7 weeks notice of such a minor change should be accepted and not penalised as all our previous experiences have been.

May I suggest that management of the Traralgon BIG4 reads the terms and conditions of other BIG4 affiliated caravan parks? Some even offer a full refund up until 10am on the day of checkin….
Very friendly and helpful staff Great place for kids with great play facilities and activities. All facilities very clean and in excellant condition

amazing

Large ensuite site was big enough for a 26ft caravan or a small caravan and boat. This is where our experience becomes remarkable. I was recovering faster than expected and was discharged. We wanted to salvage our booking and we were able to, only loosing a night.

Well exceeding my expectations, upon check in we were told that the missed night would not be charged. I cannot thank the management at Whiters enough for their compassion and understanding.

Very enjoyable stay. Four burner stove, microwave and fridge/freezer. 5 minute drive to shops and restaurants. Two bedroom cabin that sleeps six. Indoor pool and lots of things for the kids. Staff very friendly. Tea and coffee making facilities.

Great stay! Location was great with lots of things to see around 10mins away. Beach walking distance & local shops were all 3-5 mins drive. Staff were accommodating and friendly. Great vibe. Lots of things to do on site. Never a dull moment. Bonus it a dog friendly :)

We ran into some mechanical problems with our jet ski and the stuff was extremely kind and let us use a variety of different tools that they had, and with their help we where able to get right back on the water.
Thanks a lot, we will for sure be coming back...

Stayed in a glamping tent, the set up was beautiful.
The beds and blankets were super comfortable & the kids absolutely loved the park.
Walking distance to beach & corner store that does  good fish & chips
Fabulous place for kids and groups!
Very bare. Only a few trees. Walking the dog not easy - can't go around the perimeter.
So close to the beach. So many things to do. We will definitely be back.
Nice park good facilities.

Service was incredible the staff are so friendly and offered extra amenities. The location is fantastic right next to the beach and everything else you need! Especially having a local coffee shop on the corner. The waterpark inside the big 4 and all the other activities were insane!! Kids had the time of their lives. The rooms were great the only complaint I could have is the beds were so beyond uncomfortable. The main double bed was hard as a rock and the double bed my daughter was on had springs digging into her back. Would 100% stay again just in a different room hoping the beds will be better 😊

Inverloch Big 4 was our best camping trip yet!  The location is amazing, and all the facilities on offer kept our boys entertained for the whole long weekend.
We are already booked to come back, and can’t wait!
Great park, clean and well maintained cabins and facilities. Friendly staff, would definitely recommend.

Had a great family stay in the 2 bedroom enquire cabin. It is small but has has all the facilities that you need including equipped kitchen. Amazing facilities for young children. Loved the indoor pools, bounce pillow and skate park.
Thank you for a great stay.
Great place! Very clean and perfect for kids

Great place for families, nice grass sites but they are packed in. If you dont have young kids that benefit from the facilities I'd suggest the Foreshore as its go lots of trees. Water park and pool is cool but was cold so only used it once in 10 days as kids are under 3 and froze but didn't bother older kids. Kitchen & toilets well maintained and clean. Wish Parks like this would ban smoking, especially if they are trying to attract kids. Over all very good and there is a walking track into town and close to the beach.

Brilliant park. Definitely the best we’ve stayed at yet in VIC. And we’ve been to all the main parks. The facilities are fantastic, the kids are never bored and the staff at reception are lovely. Very safe park, pool is amazing, location is perfect, can’t recommend highly enough. We stayed in the coastal villa and it had everything you could need for a family of 5. Highly recommend.

Awesome location, kids loved the recreational facilities, especially the indoor splashpark and pool. We had a blast. The only recommendation I would give is to upgrade the main toilet block facilities- they are quite dated and not enough toilets to accomodate the guests when the park is full. Otherwise a brilliant park and highly recommended! We will definitely be back!
The staff were very friendly and kind. Always pleased with the services provided. Lots of free activities and you can win great prizes. Would come here again.
Great place to stay, fun for all ages. Highly recommended

A great place to stay, I hired a cabin for four days, it was clean, quiet, everything worked and any issues were quickly resolved. Great customer service (thanks Dillon and Michael) and a central location close to Morwell and Loy Yang. Thoroughly enjoyable stay!!
Fantastic park very clean the STAFF here amazing there is heaps to do for the kids. Very nice clean amenities top-notch

Stayed here for an overnight stay, was a bit out of town, our site was drive through and close to toilets. Facilities were new and very clean, a credit to the cleaners. We didn’t use any extras but it looked like lots to do for kids and pool looked clean.
Absolutely great place for kids with 2 pools, big trampoline and the park playground (the pools are small, but are perfect for kids. We have 2 kids of 5 and 7). Kitchen, toilets are super clean.

• Lovely staff, the two ladies at the reception were very kind
• There were many empty campsites, but we were placed right in the middle
• Almost no trees, so very little shade – doesn’t feel very “nature-like”
• Lots of children, so it can be noisy
• Located right next to a busy road, so there’s noise
• TV room and kitchen are air-conditioned, which is nice
• Nice pool
• Jacuzzi available upon reservation
• Playground for kids within the campground
• Very clean toilets and showers
Clean amenities and site and great things to keep the kids occupied

PROS: We visited during Australia Day long weekend. It is perfect for families, with clean facilities, a great pool, a fantastic ropes course, mini golf and plenty of fun activities. Fantastic staff at the front desk area making the check-in and checkout process very smooth.

CONS: The laser tag service was disappointing due to unclear instructions and impatience with kids. Overall, a great stay with just that one drawback!
What it looks like in website is not what it is when you be there,Most of the kids play was closeCould not charge my ev on site it was just normal plug in…Need to update website and venue
Good place with plenty activities
sorry only good if you have kids otherwise to noisy
Great space for kidys

Unfortunately the recent reviews are a sad reference of the condition of this park. While the photos are promising, reality is, the park has a long way to go. The only reason I can give it one star is because the lady Jules at the front counter was so nice. I would give it zero stars if it weren’t for her and if I could.
The toilets were disgusting. There was urine and poo on the floor and walls. And it was like this for some time. Such a health hazard.
For such a family focussed park, the pool is ridiculous. Like it’s the size of a home pool. We were there on a hot day and we had to go and buy a misting system because we couldn’t even fit in the pool. It was so murky and looked like it could give you some infection!
The high ropes course looked great but does cost extra. There was bad weather so we could not use it. I had booked for it but was not able to get my money back, instead I was allowed to get things from the shop - which was pretty expensive. So that was annoying.
There was a lot of rain and it had tipped over rubbish bins. We called the office to tell them that there were nappies and all other things floating and settling right by our van but no one came in any great hurry and they were still there the next day. We were down the back of the park and the drainage was so bad. Lucky we were in our van, but the campers next to us were not so lucky!
For the price of this place we were expecting so much more. We will not be going back. And will not be recommending to our friends. The park needs to have more cleaning staff and a much bigger pool!

The place was packed when we were there with adults and kids everywhere - there must have been 2-300 people in the camping/caravan area.  Big problem - there are only three toilets and three showers in both the male and female amenities at this park. I don’t know what the health regulations are but surely these limited amenities are vastly inadequate?  Add to that, the fact that there is no urinal in the men’s toilet, the kids with their limited aim made the situation even worse!  Definitely need more amenities here!
It is a nice and quiet village with swimming pool, game room, BBQ etc…

Great place to stay in cabin accommodation. We stayed with our Daughter and Grandchildren in a 3 bedroom cabin. Movies each night for the children and because of the cold over Easter they have a token for the heated pool at the aquatic center.

We went there for a break and to use the Red Kidney Bus that was parked there at whiters .
It was so convenient and also staff couldn’t do enough for us . The nurses on bus were sooo good and kind too . We come each year and we are not disappointed!

I have been staying here regularly for work over the past 2 months. The cabins are beautifully clean, well equipped & comfortable. Close to shops & amenities with a coffee cart opposite the entrance gor my morning caffeine hit. My home away from home & Kylie couldn't be more helpful & delightful to have greet me after my long day driving.

PROS: lots of activities for the kids (and big kids), big sites, laundry only cost $4 (although they are small machines). Lovely, friendly lady at reception.

CONS: not enough staff, only one person on reception and there was always a wait. Frustrating when you need to talk to this person to book any of the activities, buy anything from the shop, order coffee, or discuss your stay! Bathrooms didn't feel like they were cleaned very often. Pool was TINY, nowhere near big enough for a large caravan park. Water was so murky you couldn't see the steps as you walked in. Activities also cost a LOT when you're there for a few days. I feel like it should be included in the price you pay for the stay.

Bathroom /toilets  are not cleaned and have limited toilet paper, staff complained about cleaning toilets whilst in there doing there job  very unprofessional 4 toilets for the men toilets is not  sustainable for the size of the park , and where dirty .   facilities are ok but I seen a child twice trip over as a result of neglect in the playground flooring there is a gab  that’s has not been fixed. Not often do I do I review. I have been to a lot of park and they are very clean  twice a day . This is not one of them  sorry but you need to pick up your game .

You will be completely disappointed if you visit this place after seeing the pictures on the website 😂 The swimming pool is small, accommodating only 4 to 5 people comfortably. Additionally, you have to pay for everything else: rope activities, mini golf, the train ride... even for a ping-pong ball..

This camp was so racisted. If you are from multicultural I recommend to not come to this camp or you will experience racism where the manager will treat you badly if you are not white. My experience was in the train where the manager said that me and my family won’t be able to go in the trine because we “didn’t have the tickets” but in fact we did and when I was talking to her she told me to “shush I’m speaking” and she didn’t let me neither my cousin to in the trine because the door was not closing BUT she let a CHILD that was around4-5 years old go in. The pool was so small and dirty and it had so much bacteria it was so small and you could see the pee. And You had to pay for EVERYTHING. I hope no one go to this camp because it is so ridiculous rude and racist. I was S8. DO NOT GO TO THIS CAMP EVER!!!

We recently stayed  here, and travelled so far to stay here, all looking extremely forward to all the parks activities to be let down as soon as we got here. Unfortunately it was nothing like the photos as posted on the website and just run down to say the least. Bathrooms were dirty and not to mention the pool with rubbish floating in it. The kids were crying as there was so much chlorine in it was hurting there eyes. The staff were rude and never on time for any of the activities they were running or had people booked in for. We cut our stay short and headed somewhere else. We love Big. 4 caravan parks but this has definitely been the worst! Highly disappointed.
Very handy for me while doing business in Traralgon. Very good staff. Lovely pool. Nice clean amenities

The rooms were super clean and we liked it very much but the SPA and kids playing areas were all closed which was a bit unlikeness.|We will try to visit again but we will make to ask if the facilities are available or under maintenance. December is considered a peak session but most of the facilities are closed.
We had our own van

Almost no staff who are more focused on customers coming in from outside the park. Would never stay here again. Terrible. Almost everything advertised is not available due to low staff or being shut. Wouldn't be hard to make it a good experience
VERY Nice hosts.

We booked on here for two nights, first on a powered site and second in a cabin as we were having our caravan serviced nearby. The park is clean, well maintained and dog friendly. After our first night in our van we dropped it in for service the next morning. As we were at the end of a 6 week trip we has frozen foods we needs to refrigerate. The friendly staff accommodated us and managed to get us into a vacant cabin immediately. Very happy with the park and service.

Very clean and tidy Park
Modern  Cabins are very large with carport and private outdoor area
Very nice place to stay

Disappointed as the room was very dusty, aircon filter had a thick layer of dust, appears to not have been cleaned for months if not years. Toilet would not stop running, hairdryer not working etc. There are several attractions for the kids however these are open to the public and most still require payment, even for guests which was very disappointing. The indoor play center was dirty and it’s awkward to utilize as a guest when there is a party/function running. Outdoor pool was not heated, so was freezing despite the good weather. Have been to many big 4’s and was very excited but was sadly let down and left a day early.

We had a lovely experience at Big 4 Traralgon from the time we arrived until our departure the next day. Our cabin was very clean when we arrived. We relaxed straight away and decided to have a BBQ dinner so wandered down to the BBQ area and met and lovely gentleman cooking his families dinner. The it was our turn and once again the BBQ area was clean and made it easy for 2 elderly women to use the BBQ. Simply a button press. The beds were very soft and comfortable and so to were the pillow. The overall experience was very pleasurable and we were disappointed we didn't have more time to use more of the facilities like the pool and mini golf. So much for people to do there like ride bikes, go carts, train ride, ropes course, trampoline, mini golf etc.
Comfy bed and great amenities
Always friendly and make sure we get a great caravan site

Great stay here. Perfect for kids.

The bathrooms could be cleaned 3 times a day not 2 as they get dirty pretty fast with so many people.
Great family location and to enjoy meeting new friends. Great water park and pool with lots of activities to do during the holiday period.
The staff were super friendly and there is so much for the kids to do to keep them entertained. Location couldn't be better and we had an amazing time. Thanks for having us, we'll be back for sure!
Lovely place

A great place to bring the family for a relaxing stay. Plenty of fun activities like the jumping pad, splash zone, bike track and pool. The cabin was clean and fresh. Has basic dining needs but no veggie peeler. Has kettle and toaster. The stove top is gas and the grill and over are gas. There are no instructions on how to use (not many people have gas ovens) and I burnt myself trying to light it. Be careful. The grounds are clean and tidy. A short walk to a cafe/ convenience shop, then cross the road for a beautiful calm beach. Further down the road is the towns shopping strip with cafes and shops. Definitely recommend stay here.
A lot of facilities for kids and adults to spend 4-5 days here. We enjoyed our time at big 4 inverloch.

Great place to stay with wonderful facilities  I'd just prefer if the path from our camp to our ensuit was paved so we didn't have to walk in dirt and get the ensuit floor dirty. Also the pool water was filthy with scum built up on the walls. The staff were super friendly and had plenty of things to do with the kids.

Thanks again for a wonderful stay. Kids had a blast with the indoor pool, splash park, jumping pillow, mini golf, scooter pump track, and games room. Stayed on an ensuite slab site which was perfectly located central within the park.

Lovely tine we stayed 2 nights had to chsnge room.
Perhaps could have gone a little smoother.
The girls on office amazing. Especially Jenelle.
We will be back.

We had 4 en-suite camp sites. Was excellent
Great park for kids, we had 30 in our group ages ranging from 2 to 67, highly recommended.
Site excellent  main issue was the toilet/shower block seemed to get really messy
Great spot for a family getaway. Plenty for the kids to do - even if the weathers not great. Staff were friendly and helpful.
We had a lovely stay. Such a kid friendly place.
Great friendly staff. Well appointed. Great play areas for the kids. Dog friendly. Well worth the stay
We booked a kids party for mini golf and laser tag. The kids loved it!

Went for the play centre. It was raining and took the kids for something to do. There was a party that was finishing and there was quite a bit of rubbish lying around, and food on the floor in the playroom. The facilities themselves were good. And it kept the kids busy for a couple of hours. The best part was the $8 wedges with sour cream and sweet chilli. Decent serving and delicious.

Really great place for a trip with the kids. I'd advise bring your own firepit and extra cash for activities for them.. downfall was we were charged $40 a night for a firepit (bunnings one too). Ended up purchasing a $30 drum from bunnings and firewood down the road for the rest of our stay and saved $$$.. Place was clean and welcoming and great for kids.

Fantastic holiday Park,cabins were well presented and reasonably clean. Competitive prices and the park is well equipped for kids with many playgrounds and activities. It's a dog friendly park which is great, owners were very nice.We enjoyed our stay and will be staying again when we are on holidays.

A fun winter break. The reception and activity staff made the trip so much better with their welcoming and friendly manner. We really appreciated the use of a camp fire as the park wasn't too busy. Being flexible and accommodating for guests is why parents recommend places like this to others. Thanks for a great short break!

Stayed here for 1 night in a Superior Family Cabin. We arrived late afternoon and the heater was already on for us as well as a light. The check.in was nice and easy and staff were polite. The cabin itself was quite spacious and had everything we needed. It was also relatively clean which was nice. Travelling with 2 kids under 3 and one of first stops was the play room after we had unpacked. The playroom was pretty good and would be great during school holidays when it's all going. Met some locals there since it's also a public use area. After the play had a quick walk around the park. It was around 6 so the golf and everything else was all locked up. There's pedal cars for older kids which seems to be a big 4 thing which I also like . This was just a stop over for us before heading to Mount Baw Baw the next day. This is a park where you really need to stay a few nights and during busy peak periods so you can make the most of the activities. After 6 hours in the car we slept like babies, even the adults. The only things I could pick and thats being picky is there is a noticeable lack of lights around the park and parking areas and it would be handy to be able to stream movies to the tv or have them unlocked for that use as this makes the TV's unusable with little kids. If travelling bring a chrome cast to plug in or alternatively a portable projector as that tends to get around hotels blocking TV's which is a common practice that makes no sense. Overall we enjoyed our stay and would consider staying again in the summer for a longer period.

During my 3 week stay while having treatment at hospital tha staff were amazing with cabin spotlessly clean and staff very friendly and went out of their way to make our stay warm and friendly for us and our cat
Thankyou so much to all management and staff
Great location, plenty of options for the kids. Definitely will be coming back for a longer stay!

I would not book this park if you have kids. We were extremely disappointed. They advertise they have all of these fantastic things for kids but half of the indoor playground was broken, the jumping pillow is down until further notice, the ropes course is only available on weekends and everything is at an extra cost including the mini golf despite the already hiked up accommodation prices. On checking in we were never told we needed to book in a spa time otherwise we would have done so prior to our departure instead the following morning we were met with an extremely rude member of staff that said we should have known to book in despite only just seeing a sign on the wall as we got there. Save your money and drive on.
Went for a kids birthday party and it was better than i thougjt it was going to be.
Such a great little get away with the extended family

Great Family Park. We always look forward to returning.

Loving the refresh of the cabins

We had a great stay at Ingenia Big4! We stayed with our two kids aged 6 and 3, the facilities are fantastic for kids and they both didn’t want to leave!|The park feels very secure with the gates around which was great travelling with our kids. It was so quiet at night too, even though it looked like there were a decent amount of people around. |We stayed in the basic cabin which was nice and comfortable, the kitchen was equipped with basics but next time we’d bring a few things from home to make cooking a little easier.|Definitely a great spot, especially for families and we’ll be back for sure!

We had a two bedroom cabin, very comfortable. The second bedroom has bunk beds with the lower bunk being a double bed. The bed had a very thin mattress, with wooden slat underneath, very uncomfortable and I had very little sleep in the 7 nights we stayed there .
Very quiet stay which was lovely!

Second time staying here and enjoyed it as much as the first time. Has everything you need to keep the kids busy. All facilities are clean and well kept. This is the best camp the family and I have stayed at. Can’t wait to go back thanks guys
Great park and facilities. This is our second stay and we will be back again. Best thing is you walk out the gate and 100 metres to the inlet which is a great walk. Great spot.

Plenty for the kids to do and reasonable area for a caravan only down side was the noise after hours on the weekend that went till the early hours and the washing machine likes to take money and not work , but overall a great location close to everything , very clean park and very helpful staff
Great park. We loved the water park and mini golf. The ensuite grass sites were a decent size and nice condition. Would definitely stay again.
Great place for a holiday with the kids could do with some updating to the kids play area and a spa fpr the parents wpuld be even better  but overall clean and good experience.

Close to beach and great location.
Had to watch for very young child on bikes and scooters that were unsupervised
Enjoyed our group camp with the Probus group.

Powered site. Men’s amenities,  very small cubicles, shower taps not labelled and falling off. No soap holder, door very difficult to close.
Ladies amenities.  Very small cubicles. Soap holder not working.
Step out of the van onto dirt.
Certainly not worth $60 per night!!
Huge family room. Security might be a bit over the top

Too many dogs!!! 2 x collies in one group that barked all the time and 2 x giant German shepherds in another that were very intimidating.
Be great if there was a dog free area. The difference at parks near national parks where dogs not allowed is significant

The park is great with plenty of facilities. The only downsides were the tightness of sites to get in and out where we were and the cleanlng of the facilities, disinfectant daily and proper floor cleaning of the showers daily would be appreciated.

Excellent caravan park with friendly operators who helped find a really nice available site for my van.
Whilst some are open mine was bordered by trees and shrubs and bet spacious and not too close to others.
Amenities were clean and fresh with a new large communal fire pit.
2min walk to Long Jetty and fishing if interested.
It's a lovely piece of Victoria down that way.
very tidy caravan park. Quiet. Nice amenities for families. Enjoyed our stay.
We had a caravan site with power and water. Big sites and a huge park. Situated at the start of the Long Jetty.  Clean amenities, spotless grounds a very friendly  staff. Loved it.
We stayed for 7 nights with our van. The amenities are excellent and clean. Very close to the Jetty.  Would recommend anytime.

I stayed in my caravan for two nights. I found the park to be very well maintained, tidy and clean. There was a new shower block, a well equipped camp kitchen and the sites were spacious.
Only a short stroll to the beach and Long Jetty. I would definitely stay again
Such a beautiful caravan park , I called last minute to get a spot to stay the night and got one straight away not to mention the very friendly staff
Very clean room and the staff is very nice and friendly plus it's just next to the long Jetty where you can cast your line straight away. Highly recommended.
Great place to stay. Helpful pleasant staff amenities very good. Well maintained grounds. We will be back definitely

The best park I've been to. Location perfect, sites are huge, even the unpowered.  Clean amenities and the staff are so friendly and helpful.  The price for my unpowered was not over the top. Port Welshpool is a very walkable dog friendly town and so quaint.  Staff at the General store were so kind and friendly.  Thank you Long Jetty Caravan Park and your lovely village Port Welshpool.  Hopefully we will be back.
Hidden gem, loved our stay!!!
Cabin 8 couples cabin lively spacious the only thing was 1 out of 4 jets on the oven top  worked otherwise fantastic cabin
Fantastic caravan Park we love coming here with our dogs such a peaceful place to stay can't wait to come back great site's and amenities.
The owner was very helpful and friendly.  The site was huge and private.  The toilets and showers were very well maintained and clean

Nice quiet grassy caravan park next to the ocean and the long jetty. Friendly staff. Clean facilities.  The jetty was rebuilt <10 years ago to include fisherman shelters and fishing rod holders /seats. Very delicious restaurant just down the road -The Rusty Gurnard is well worth a visit for week made meals in a very pleasant building. Would not hesitate to stay here again.

Took up the offer of stay for 6 pay for 4 which was great value.
The park itself has really good amenities including a fire pit, enclosed bbq area and recreation room. They are all kept spotlessly clean.
We had a dog friendly cabin which also was very clean, however we found the bedroom area and bathroom  very small. Another post suggested removing the bunk beds and extending the bedroom/ bathroom which would have made it a lot more comfortable which we completely agree with.
Kitchen could use a few more utensils and cooking pots/pans etc as the lack of shops in Port Welshpool means you have to do most of your own cooking.
A lovely park with friendly and helpful staff close to the pier which was convenient.

Great caravan park,  very clean amenities,  big powered sites, great access to long jetty and friendly and helpful staff. Have been twice and will definitely be back again. Good value for money with free nights.
Always a great stay . The staff are friendly and helpful.

We really enjoyed our stay at  the BIG4 in Port Welshpool. Good value for money with a large site close to the amenities block and great camp kitchen and rec. room. Friendly informative staff, just a short walk to the “Long Jetty”
Thanks Amanda & Jess for assisting great stay….. we’ll be back.
P.s. great food at the pub. 🤣

Great one night stay in the tiny cabin. So lovely to sit on the deck for evening drinks and morning coffee. Comfy bed, small but awesome shower. Very friendly and helpful staff. Perfect location for Great Southern Rail Trail

Very clean,  quiet park.
Will definitely be back.

Great facilities for the kids. The ropes course is excellent! Keep in mind there's a height restriction, I think it was 120cm. Mini golf is a great setup. There's also a playground, laser tag, indoor play centre, pool , pedal karts and games room. The jumping pillow was out of action during our visit. Definitely worth a look.

It was amazing light brake on the weekend :)
The cabin was good, a little bit annoying that we can't find some stuff in the kitchen (cut-board, colander, etc), but it is not a big deal :)
Place - amazing, staff - amazing, and more - the Park Lane Play is so cool! Especially, if you stay in the camp you have 50% discount to the rope-climbing, mini-golf and lazier-tag :)

The owner is NOT kids friendly, the park is ok
Just watch out for the owner and his dog!

We were pretty disappointed that our cabin had an awful smell in it. It wasn’t sewerage related but almost like the couch had gotten wet and/or stank of wet dog / mould or maybe even a dead animal. Staff seemed surprised to find out but there’s no way you could not notice this if you went in the cabin so we can only conclude someone knew and didn’t want to or didn’t think to do anything about it - because it probably required replacing the couch. We couldn’t get rid of the smell with any amount of airing / open windows. And it gave a general feeling of dirtiness and made the whole experience pretty disappointing. I get that it’s a cabin at a camp ground but for the price that’s charged per night you’d expect to not have to endure this.

The blow up pillow was not working and none of the air guns in the play centre were working. Other entertainment options pretty good and the staff particularly at the ropes course were really friendly and helpful.

Will be looking at other alternatives in future as better value for money…
Great park very clean will stay again

The room was not bad for the price. The location is not close to anything much. It has a kids play area with a ropes course, Go-kart and mini golf. The jumping cushion was out of order and the go-kart was a bit of a waste of $9 for 1/2 hour when my daughter got a broken kart. The ropes course is a good one to try, but other than that not much fun for kids older than 10 years. The board at the reception had a package deal for 3 kids activities for $20, but when we asked them about it, they told us they didn't know about it and it's not available!.. not a good service at all.
This place is amazing we all loved it but the bunk had some big on the pillow when we arrived

First time we stay there was a 4 year old fully unsupervised in the spa we said something to reception and was told it's not our responsibility it's a family park and it's upto the parents 2nd time we stayed when we arrived there was rubbish all over our sites my son asked a child not to splash him in the spa the parent threatened to hit us then the kids put water in our tent and the kids were yelling every morning at 6 am certainly wasn't enjoyable the kids are left unsupervised at all times within the park  the kitchen wasn't cleaned both times we stayed and management were rude on both occasions the indoor play ground was extremely run down but I guess if the kids are as bad as the ones last time we stayed it's no wander. We will not be staying here again it's the parks lost as we need somewhere to stay every 2 to 3 weeks for medical treatments.

Disappointed in the park as I took Grandchildren for 3 nights   aged 11 to 13   .yes payed money for 3 activities to do .Then no jumping castle  working.spar was supposed to be cleaned it was to closed .  Then reopen to use water was very dirty grey colour not good   .nothing for children to do  this park. This is for kids under 8  .As I went to office to ask why nothing free except pool witch is freezeing  spost. to be sola heated   .my answer was .there's a play eara over there .As I said yes with a big sighn saying toddlers play eara is on door    for $730.80 3 night  not good enough if you have bigger kids save money .as far as the units gos they were very clean up to date nice rooms
Great fun!
Excellent and helpful-helped my Mrs set up our newer van whilst i was playing in the VOpen
Pulled up for a break from the road excelent park with wide roads.  Supper kids attraction family friendly park.

What a fun stay! My kids had a great time. Comfortable with lots of family activities. Cabins are bright and colourful. How
wonderful to have an onsite, indoor play centre for the days the weather isn’t it’s finest.  An extra star for the delicious barista made coffee I purchased one morning! Thank you, looking forward to another visit.
Great place to chill and relax. We stayed in a superior cabin. Lots of activities for the kids - rain hail or shine. Staff awesome. Accommodation fantastic!

Rooms were clean and recently renovated, beds were clean and comfortable and the park has a really cheerful atmosphere with all the cabins painted in different bright colours.

Location is in the middle of nowhere, next to a hospital and no l by the highway, so you can't really walk anywhere of interest unless you're sick ;) this is mitigated by the pool, rope course and play areas, all at extra charge for people staying here.

Staff are very friendly and helpful from the outset.
Large Motorhome sites were terrific.
Great, clean facilities are a stand out.
Set up very well for families, given the abundant fun options on offer. In all I would rate it 10 / 10..
Stayed in an ensuite site. Very clean & close to bbq area. Will definitely be back
Enjoyed our stay very much only a couple of nights but all was good

Only had 3 nights but had a lovely time. (Wished we could have stayed longer)
Our cabin was clean & comfortable especially the bed. The staff were very friendly the facility’s were great.
First time here for us with our family & grandchildren who took their caravan
Absolutely loved it. Highly recommend 👍
The place is well kept with efficient staff. Smiles from everyone. The  facilities were great and plenty to do for the kids and adults alike. My favourite was the splash park. My kids loved it.

Beautiful friendly caravan park very clean and tidy with a great atmosphere. The staff were fantastic letting me change my booking last minute when I realised I’d made a mistake with the dates and even gave us a late checkout because we’d be arriving late. Will definitely be back!!

Location of park is ideal in terms of access to popular Long Jetty. Our cabin was dated and in need of some refurbishment. Park itself is well appointed and perfect for fishermen as boats can be parked adjacent to accommodation. Strangely there are some caravan spots right next door to some cabins.
Very enjoyable relaxing stay thank you
Beautiful spot close to Port Welshpool Jetty. Luscious green grass van sites. Great staff plus they just opened a beautiful communal fire pit for 5pm arvos

Stayed 2 nights in our caravan on a powered site. Sites were big and grassy and the kids loved the playground. On our stay there was a cinema night at the park which was a big hit with the kids. Caravan park is only a short walk to the jetty and pub.
Very fast efficient check in. Clean and modern cabins. Great for a fishing trip. Could not fault.
Love this place, sites are generous and lush green grass. Very well looked after and staff are great
Stayed tiny cabin, all we needed while riding great southern rail trail. Located next to the pier great spot.

Highly recommend this lovely park, sites are well maintained, amenities are very clean and staff super friendly and helpful. So great to stay in a dog friendly park. Amazing location with the beach and jetty so close. Loved our stay and will be back soon! 10/10 from us 😊
Good stay just noisy drunk people next door

I enjoyed my non-powered site. The bathroom block was clean, and the camp kitchen was spotless. The receptionist gave me immediate and accurate information, in a friendly manner. I enjoyed my walk to the pub for a great meal.

We had a very pleasant stay in our motorhome - sites are large and private   (but on the last day someone parked within 6 feet of us). Maybe we were not positioned properly for the site!!!
Bathrooms were clean and well maintained. Office staff were delightful.
Also dog friendly.
Short walk out the side of the park to access the long jetty.
Would definitely stay here again.
Easy to deal with. Beautiful location. Close to railtrail.

Stayed in the caravan park. Great facilities, amenities block modern, clean and great hot water flow. Good barbecue area and rec room. Great grassed large sites and because it wasn’t very busy vacant sites right next to us.

Booked a 2-night stay for our fishing trip. Location was great and the camp grounds were well maintained, clean and peaceful. Cabin was perfect, it had everything we needed. Glad that pet-friendly cabins are available and that our dog could come with us. Definitely worth coming back for another relaxing stay.

Camping at Long Jetty Caravan Park was absolutely amazing.
It was peaceful, with beautiful scenery, and there’s a long jetty right in front of the park that’s perfect for fishing.
All the facilities were clean and very well maintained.
Most of all, the staff were incredibly friendly and even provided helpful information without us having to ask.
Thank you for such a great experience.
I’ll definitely be coming back. Highly recommended!

The cabin was very spacious and clean. The caravan park is conveniently located right next to the long jetty. Unfortunately we didn't catch any fish or squid this time.

It would be nice to have medium height pillow. All of the pillows were too high for us.

Overall, we are happy with our experience
Had a lovely few days at Long Jetty caravan park. What a great park and such friendly helpful staff. Facilities very clean as are grounds. Will definitely be visiting again. Thanks everyone.

Great caravan park. Facilities are top-notch and keep the kids entertained for hours. Park has a nice vibe with plenty of chatty families to say gday to. A little out of town but nearly everything you need is at the park anyway.
Train/traffic noise abit bad during the night due to location
Did not go there
Good shopping centre with lots of options.
One of the nicest Big4's we've stayed out. The family cabin was so spacious, we didn't feel like we were in a cabin. So much for the kids to do and really lovely staff!

Overall, it is a great park to visit with kids or on your way to explore the Gippsland area of Victoria. It is a bit tricky to find what to do in the evenings, but the park has a great amenities and is well kept and serviced. It is easy to access and even have a caravan dealership right next to it. The park have powered sites and cabins.
Took kids for a play at the indoor park the kids had a ball and we enjoyed a nice hot coffee  ☕ 😁

We had a 5 day holiday booked for our kids staying in a cabin and what a disappointment it was upon arrival the cabin was absolutely filthy 😳
After reading other reviews and staff saying the problem will be addressed  back in November it obviously has not been addressed.
For the $1600 dollars I paid I was so disappointed when we still had to pay for my 4 yr old to do mini golf mind u she played with the ball half the time.
Pool is tiny to many people in at 1 time.
Jumping pillow is ripped around edges wich is a real hazard.
Our cabin only stocked 6 of everything though there was 7 of us so one of us leaving 1 to est with a spoon every meal.
Overall I did not enjoy  this experience though the ropes course staff are absolutely outstanding at there job the rest of the park needs attention.
Photos on line look great then u get there and its nothing like that.
Will not be staying again
If a review was able to insert photos you would be horrified.

Won't ever be staying at this place. Made a booking in late Dec 23 for late Feb 24 and paid deposit of $65. . 17/1 had to cancel for family reasons and requested a refund by phone. Someone was to ring back to explain their refund policy - no call.
Rang back again - will call back - again no call back. Later in the day received an email for refund of $15!!  Excessive $50 admin fee for just taking a phone booking - cancellation was made more than 30 days prior to arrival. Stay away from this place !!!
Fantastic facilities for kids! Indoor play center is awesome.  A tad pricey for the cabin. Good prices for activities though including decent 18 hole mini golf for only $5

Big 4 Traralgon was great stayed for a few days- great family room, comfy beds, friendly service & park guest.  Offer pools, park, indoor play area, mini golf & rope course.. which the staff at the rope course were amazing can’t remember names however they were amazing with my daughter & niece doing rope course for the first time- encouragement and patience goes along way- so thank you had a wonder short stay at the park.

Only down fall is I think the pools area, yes they have a very small little pool & bigger pool area. However the bigger pool is so deep no shallow end, it’s had to be mindful of your children in the water & watching them- due to level of the water. They couldn’t swim & enjoy. Without an adult assistance near by. I think harder for parents with more than 1 child over 5 who want to swim in bigger pool other then that great park lots to do.

We were very grateful that the Big4 Traralgon could accommodate us and our dog at the last minute after another booking fell through during the Christmas break. The room was exceptionally clean and had nice facilities (fridge, basic kitchen facilities, AC). The spa was very busy. Great place for kids but a bit busy for us.

Went to the rope climb, staff were great, slight issue with one of fasteners but overall a great time. Rope climb was fun, everyone enjoyed it, will definitely go back again as we didn't do everything in 1 hr.

It was a good camping experience for kids specially with the Rope Lessons and Laser Tag game and the indoor play center for Little ones. We enjoyed the Spa(lucky that it was a quite time and there was no rush) and kids loved water Balloon pool fight … Staff is amazing and extremely helpful. Disappointed with provision of only 1 Camp Kitchen and 1 Main toilets(which was not in a state of cleanliness since the cleaning scheduled to be once a day only!!!!! Camp kitchen also not very spacious considering there was numerous Tent campers and the rest camper trailers …. Needed better toilets and Camp kitchen to match the standards of Big4 caravan Parks..

We have been travelling to the different BIG 4's. Traralgon has been our favourite experience so far. The staff are very attentive and welcoming. There's mini golf, a ropes course, laser tag, a play center and swimming on site. It's also close enough to the town center, shops and restaurants etc.  My only criticism is that not all the standard cabins (for example) are the same. Some have better couches etc. than others. Ours had a ripped couch and smaller than the one next door from us. It's a bit of a luck of the draw. The oven was not great for cooking. Outside of these small things, it was a wonderful place to stay and we've already said we'd definitely come back in a heartbeat.
Good Experience . Pool and Spa area a little smaller. Indoor play area closes at 6 which is too early. Room was good for the $ you pay.

We booked with Big4 because of its proximity to the hospital, but unfortunately, it was below the standards we are used to for Big4.

We paid $250 for a cabin, my sister was charged for $350 for the same thing, same amount of people, both of these far exceed the value of the experience. The bunk bed was not made, no dish rack, broken tiles, splotchy paint, curtains that allowed the 24 hour light through (we might as well have stayed in a tent the curtains were that effective). The bouncy pillow was so low in inflation that our feet hit the ground around the perimeter. This is not a ‘how’s the serenity’ advertised big 4: the closest thing to aesthetics was the intermittent sound of truck air brakes throughout the night.

You should also be aware that the ROPES and LASER TAG course is ONLY open on the WEEKENDS (we were advised later that this was in the fine print- which doesn’t help if you book in person).

While the people on the desk were lovely, this was the end of a positive big 4 stay.
What a lovely hidden spot, we'll be back.
Amenities were spotless, as was the park. We stayed in caravan so cant score the cabins. Staff on arrival were very friendly, helpful and informative.
Great Caravan Park. Friendly staff. Clean amenities.

We recently stayed at BIG4 Port Welshpool Holiday Park, and I had a really positive experience.
From the moment we arrived, the welcome was warm and friendly, which made our stay very comfortable. The park itself was well maintained and had a great atmosphere.
What truly stood out, though, was the exceptional honesty and kindness of the staff. After checking out, I realized I had accidentally left my pouch with important cards behind. Alica from one of the staff contacted me promptly to let me know and reassured me not to worry. She personally followed up, retrieved the item from the lost property, and arranged to have it posted back to me.
I’m incredibly grateful for her effort and care. It really shows that when the staff are responsible and genuinely helpful, the hospitality of the place shines through.
Highly recommend this place, not just for the stay, but for the amazing people who make it special.

Great park to stay with family.
Did some fishing and site seeing around the area

My first time there . With my caravan  it was very comfortable showers and toilets were good for there age.  Good water pressure etc plenty of buildings for entertaining in and cooking in if needed a great spot if you love fishing  with the long jetty just out the gate  . It's a well run park  thanks.
We love it here, friendly staff, and it’s a place we always come to for a getaway.
Staff very friendly.  Powered camp sites large.
Love camping at the long jetty caravan park, staff is always friendly and helpful. The amenities are very clean and tidy!!
A beautiful location and dog friendly caravan park. Modern and clean facilities. Lush green and  flat sites, generous in size. Highly recommend
Very nice and tidy place, awesome showers and toilets and kept very clean
The Long Jetty Holiday Park always delivers! Great spot for a relaxing holiday to recharge the senses! Good fishing too. Love the location directly by the sea with easy access for watersport.
Great park, nice surprise for the kids, Easter eggs, also had a food  truck come into the park was delicious.

We needed to get away 🏃 and what a superb rest we had. Our site was magnificent, secluded, private and peaceful.😌
Thank you see you next year 🙂
Very comfortable. Jason was very helpful. He went out of his way to help us. We will stay there again

The welcome was wonderful.
Great location close to town, easy walk to the waterfront, beach and shops.
Unit and grounds well maintained.
More crockery and glasses would be great.

Unfortunately not the best experience with this park. It is a very small park with narrow roads and tight turns. Sites are not big enough for a caravan and your car, you either have to park your car elsewhere or on another site. The park was also aware of our length (8m) when booking; I feel they should not take bookings for vans beyond a particular length.

While the owner of the park suggested we take up multiple spots to fit, the access into the sites was so tight we decided it was not worth it. The owner then continued to make comments to us stating her “nephew owned a ram and would be able to fit”. The owner of the park then proceeded to take photos and videos of our van and car later emailing me to explain she had done so to document the length of the van and to prove it would fit. I feel it would be difficult to measure a van and car from a photo.

They also requested the storage of our bank details on checkin to pay for possible damages such as “a hit lamp post”. We have never had to give bank details to a park and we explained we had already paid through Big4 so surely if any damages occurred during our stay, it would be an insurance issue. Confusingly the lady at reception explained that they are not associated with Big4 anymore but Big4 still take bookings? She still required my bank details.

Overall we asked if we could have a refund for the two nights booked to which the owner declined. We filled up our water tanks and ended up staying at the beautiful winery Wyanga up the road.

Location is very good but carpet and furniture in the rooms stinks.

They also charged extra amount without any reasonable answer.

The activity room is very small and half of the equipments were not working

This was our second [and longer] visit here.  Such a great location, right across from the water [ocean a little more challenging to get to, for us] but great for daily walks along the water wall one way and shops on way back or vice versa.

Anyhoo, now about the park and its people.  John and Kelly are great hosts, helpful in every way possible.  The grass areas in and around the sites were kept neat and tidy.  i can't speak for the facilities as we never used them but I have no doubt they would be as good as the rest of the facilities.  The camp kitchen was always clean as was the games room.

When my partner was taken to Bairnsdale hospital by ambulance and later the Melb heart hospital, they had no hesitation in saying yes to extending our stay so Gary could rest up when he returned [thank God within 5 days].  We would have extended another week but needed to return to Melbourne for a cardiology appointment and needed to take our time getting there as he tows the van, not me.

The Pizza shop in Myer Street [an easy walk to from the Park] was wonderful [Pizza on Myer] as was the food in the Lakes Sports and Community Club - a great schnitzel, crispy chips with some skin on] and lovely salad.  Staff were very friendly.

Anyways, if you want a really lovely place to park up your van and some lovely walks along the water wall with wood ducks, black swans, plovers and gulls and some marine life [jelly fish and the like], you can't go wrong.  So much to explore.  Shopping wise there's a Woolworths/Reject shop and K-hub within walking distance plus loads of cafes/bakeries, variety shop, fishing tackle shops, surf shops, a Jaycar, A NAB, Post Office, Newsagent, donut shop.  Even an ALDI!  [BONUS!!!!]  AND a handy IGA in Myer Street as well.  More fish and chip shops than you can poke a stick at.  Spoilt for choice!

Not to forget it was only a hop, skip and a jump to Lake Tyers up the road and quaint little Metung [on the way to or from Bairnsdale which is only 30-35 ks 'up the road'.]  Metung also has a hot springs.  We didn't indulge but i'm sure many would want to.  You could easily spend weeks in Lakes Entrance and not do all there is to do.  Boating, fishing, swimming, Segway hire, boat hire/cruises/charters etc.  Lots for kids to do on the 'foreshore' not far from Waters Edge.  Loads of info in the office at The Waters Edge caravan park and no doubt the wonderful hosts could tell you even more.

We'll NEVER stay anywhere else!

Fiona and Gary
17/3-14/4/2026.
Clean amenities great camp kitchen and friendly and helpful staff
Lovely cabin with plenty of room. Parking space a bit tight. They were very obliging in changing our booking at the last minute when I had booked it for the next month.

Nice campsite close to main st. Good camp kitchen, has everything you need. Except cutlery, which I assume other campers taxed. Doesn’t have a play ground anymore, but has a big jumping pillow, pool and games room. But games room shuts at dusk, ideally be 9pm.

Loved the service. Rooms were cleaned thoroughly as my Husband has allergies to dust and have stayed other places where hubby couldn't breathe due to room not being cleaned properly. Receptionist and John were both so attentive. We walked everywhere as amenities was close and walking distance. Beautiful place and would come back again.
Muy buen camping! Totalmente recomendable
A really modern and well maintained cabin
Kelly was fantastic in helping us get everything arranged!

We were 2 people that stayed in a ensuite spot. There is only 2 ensuite for caravan/campers. It was very clean and private. Not a big place but looks like limit camping and more for people who like cabins. Swimming area had 3 pools and a kids playground.  Service from staff was good. Easy communication and friendly staff. Would return.

My husband and I stayed in a clean, well designed 2BR cabin for a week.  My brother and family also booked for 3 days and the friendly camp management helpfully ensured their basic cabin was just across from ours, which delighted his kids!  Their cabin was snug, as expected, with just enough room for them but we were all able to comfortably share main meals in our larger cabin. We enjoyed our stay. The location is excellent, close to shops, the water, a park, a playground and the pedestrian bridge to the surf beach.  It's a good holiday park to choose for a Lakes Entrance stay!
Great 👍
Gerne wieder

Stayed here for two nights on our way up the coast as our original campsite got rained out. The lady at reception was very accommodating to our needs. We had a camper trailer in tow and she was able to sort us out a couple of parking spots on the fly. We stayed in the most basic cabin, and while it was indeed, "basic", all amenities were clean and it was well serviced. All staff were very friendly and happy to assist where needed.

We stayed a few nights in an Ultra cabin.

The cabins are compact, but quite modern and comfortable.  Location is brilliant, very close to Myers St (shout out for Sea Shanty and for Kelli's Cakes) and to the footbridge across to the surf club and the main surf beach.

Very cramped small sites. Very difficult to get in or out. Jumping pillow not opened till 9am despite advertised opening at 8am - very important for kids. Pool was clean but very run down. Dump point was outside women’s toilets and had no tap to rinse area if needed. Bbq only one worked. Security of toilets was very bad: doors were kept open with wooden blocks, not using the security code, I felt very unsafe. However women’s facilities looked clean, but they smelt of cat piss. Good facility for low budget option but I wouldn’t stay here again, it needs some funds and maintenance invested into it. Front office looked like it was in a state of shambles. Staff were kind and polite though, just the facilities were very run down.
Great park to stay in the amenities are brilliant. If you have children they will love this park!

We stayed there last week and had our kids bikes stolen within hours of arrival. They leave the double gates open at the rear of the park so anybody walking on the footpath of a busy street, can peek in and help themselves. An electric scooter was stolen from a neighbouring campsite the next day so clearly the locals are seizing upon the lacklustre security. Avoid the place and spend your money somewhere else
Just stayed for one night. Right by the water. A lovely spot.
Very average. I’ll be looking for another caravan park next time. One of the units looks okay.

Nice quiet little camp site on the main road, plenty for kids to do, amenities clean, plenty of hot water, laundry good & easy to use, we had a good pitch but some looked a little cramped ?|Would go back again

This is the worst Big4 experience so far. Poorly maintained ground, tight sites, narrow roads, dirty swimming pool, and blocked bathrooms, et C. The only advantage is its location. We enjoy Lakes Entrance and plan to return for holiday, but not at this park ever again.
Very tight spots for caravans to turn around the corners to their sites. You struggle to get in your spot with cars cramped all over the place
Good location close to everything
Fantasic location close to shops, bars,beach and lakes also had carnival on across the road which was great for the kids. Would definitely recommend and would definitely stay again.

Travelled here with our 10m caravan, was told that there was plenty of space for the van and that we would fit easily. Sites were actually a good size, but roads were not great, very narrow with tight corners, seen many people with much smaller vans struggle to get their vans in the sites also, the roads are much too narrow to be able to reverse any size van in or be able to manoeuvre around the roads, especially if you are not very experienced with towing/reversing a van. So I don’t recommend taking a medium-large van here. Perfect for tents and smaller vans though.
Other than that, the swimming pool & games room was really good for the kids, bbqs need a bit of attention, the plates are very warped, a bit disappointing when you need to pay to use them, toilet/bathroom facilities were clean, showers were pretty good.
Great location.  Good facilities.  Good communication.  Good price!

Would not recommend, definitely not a real Big4 experience
We recently stayed at Waters Edge Holiday Park in Lakes Entrance and were extremely disappointed — it shouldn’t be advertised as a Big4-level park (many guests expect that standard, but it falls well short) 👎. �

First, the park is cramped and overcrowded. Sites are tiny with barely any room to manoeuvre — if you have a large caravan, good luck without asking neighbours to move their car just to fit in. Tent spots are so tight there’s barely enough space to peg out properly. �

Cleanliness was shockingly poor. The shower blocks were disgusting — taps falling off, no soap in the basins at all, and a terrible smell in the toilets. Power outages during our stay meant we had to throw out all the food in our freezer because it kept cutting out.
The grounds feel run-down, not maintained to any decent standard for something calling itself part of Big4. Despite what the official rating claims, you often feel packed in like a cheap roadside stop rather than a holiday park. �

Many online reviews praise the location — yes, it’s central — but that doesn’t make up for the basic issues here. If you expect the usual Big4 quality or value your comfort and cleanliness, look elsewhere.

On our lap around Oz this park has been the worst. It was beyond full fitting in tents between vans, fire safety not being met, park run down but worst of all were the rude staff. Sorry we interrupted the receptionist’s person call to check-in, maintenance guy was rude too. We had to ask 4 people to move cars etc to get out. Why they wouldn’t have put a 9 meter van in one of the front sites I can’t understand. This park was in the very best location though so if you have a smaller van and not going in peak time it’s worth a go.
Great people, great environment to relax, very clean and friendly.

Location is perfect walking distance to EVERYTHING, amenities are beautifully clean, but nothing beats the wonderfully efficient management. We really appreciate you taking care of things, it was very reassuring to see that you hold this park to a high standard in every aspect. Thank you again, we hope you get a well earnt break after the busy season and all its happenings. Incidentally, we stayed on site 90 right over from Adam (review below this) and his lovely family and fur baby! This trip was a last minute miracle getaway for my birthday on Monday, we spent the day across the estuary and wandering the street eating fresh fish and chips, before checking out the carnival over the road. Couldn’t have picked a better spot and will be delighted to return to this park in the cooler months too. Thanks Kelly and John, fabulous service. Kelsey and Aidan

What can I say, we can here as first timers for Christmas holidays. Best choice I could've made. Very family friendly, the kids can ride there bikes, scooters or roller blade around the park safely. The sites and park are very well maintained. The amenities are spotless and the showers actually have great water pressure and HOT water instead of just warm. Let's not forget john and Kelly who manage it. They have been more than accommodating. Very easy to deal with and helpful. I can not rate waters edge high enough. Don't worry about even trying to get accommodation anywhere else. Just book here, you won't regret it.
Adam.

My kid absolutely loved it here. Heaven on earth for a 5 year old. Games room, jumping pillow and cute little cabins. Could prob do with a few upgrades here and there but its location makes up for it.
Superior cabin had all that we needed for a short stay in Lakes Entrance. Very clean. Fabulous location
Great place to stay, very friendly and efficient staff, best and cleanest amenities I have seen at any park and the only park I have stayed at with a Barista machine. :-)
Staff know how to handle people, halloween event great, sites level, facilities clean. Sharp contrast to other parks in the area.

Our holiday at Park Lane was nothing short of wonderful! The staff were just lovely from the minute we arrived. They were happy and enthusiastic and loved my little girl and made her feel so special. The facilities are impeccable! Great playgroup, super fun bouncy pillow, mini golf, pedal cars, pool, spa, a play centre and a ropes course?! There’s even a little train that does rides around the park! A kids dream! We had some rain and some sun, and we had something to do for all kinds of weather! My little one loved making friends with all the other kids. Everyone we chatted with was happy and friendly, which I would say was a product of the fun, easygoing atmosphere created here. The showers, toilets etc were super clean and nice. The kitchen was the same, clean and well kept. We will definitely be back! What a wonderful place with wonderful people!

Beautiful park,  ensuite was spacious and clean. Perfect if you have children but also suited our purpose (waiting for  house  settlement and furniture arrival). Site level and large enough for car and 22' van. Would highly recommend  this park. Not a top priority but internet was excellent

The 5 star above goes to sherry from trarlgon Park lane, I haven't even arrived at the property yet and the way she handled my enquiry and spoke to me kindly was above excellence. Looking forward to my stay at your park. Thank you sherry 😊
Amazing,  fantastic,  clean,  friendly, awesome family place.

One of our favourite Big4 caravans parks! Perfectly suited if you have kids (or not). Plenty to do such as mini golf, swimming pool, heated spa, indoor play centre, outdoor undercover playground, ropes course, laser tag, pedal kart, jumping pillow. The ride on train is a bonus!

Our 4 year old loves coming here. So much so that on the way back home from our trip driving past, he cried when we didn’t stop because he loves it so much.

Laundry takes card which is also a huge bonus if you need to do washing or drying as we always seem to be out of change.

We’ve been here many times and it’s never disappointed. Staff are wonderful as always.

First trip to the park was amazing!!
Second trip -
DEFINETLY NOT FAMILY FRIENDLY!! Cabin was unsuitable, dusty and had pop rivets left for my 2 year old child and dog to pick up. Screen doors dont secure and when this feedback was given to management - we were lob refused entry and left without a roof over our heads for 5 days on a long weekend - whilst 900km from home!!
Disgusting service!! DO NOT RECOMMEND!!
EDIT - no colorful language was used until we were kicked out
Keep in mind u actually were going to delay my refund and threatened me with police until i confirmed i would have you charged with theft!!
I have a witness too! Alot of this could have been avoided with good problem solving management!!
Great place for vacation with a dog.
Nice facilities, massive sites. Good activities for for kiddos

It is a great way to escape Melbourne for a weekend without having to travel too far. Our cabin was immaculately maintained with everything you could need for a few nights, including a very comfortable bed. Fantastic activities for families, especially those with kids.
Being only 5 minutes from the heart of Traralgon shopping and dining was also very easy.

We stayed here for a few days. It’s located really in the middle of nowhere and around 7km from any shops.

The house itself was pretty big, but the rooms were a bit cramped. There was one master bedroom and another room with a double bed and another bed on top of it, where you could not sit up straight.

They cleaned the house nicely when we were out. Didn’t get to try the mini golf. The gym was really small and had a bench which wasn’t really useable.
Provided great customer service

Great kids activities, very clean indoor play room. Not walking distance from anything, however 2 big towns within 10 mins drive. Stayed in pet friendly cabin, clean enough but could do with some upgrades. Overall lovely staff and a good holiday spot with 2 young kids
Had a birthday party in the playroom ..very clean big 4  park
You have to pay separately for the activities, which they don't tell you until you book.
Going back

A lovely place to stop. Very friendly staff, excellent facilities and very well maintained. Decided to stay another night. However, our site was already booked and that site was requested. The lovely lady in the front office called and asked if the person minded changing. They didn’t mind so we stayed. That is exceptional service in our opinion. Will happily return.

We found this to be the most relaxing peaceful well maintained lawns and amenities etc
Sites are large and grass is great !
A big shout out to Alisa in the office for being so accommodating and knowledgeable 👍
Great feel
There is a pub and general store walking distance.
The town of Welshpool is only a few minutes away for supplies
We will be back

Bloody amazing place this
The rooms were very tidy and well set out
Plenty of room for parking boats and cars
The staff were fantastic to deal with and will definitely be back soon
Also very child friendly so will be bringing the family along .
Just returned from a group weekend away for 15 people.  Great cabins and service and facilities

This park is great. So spacious. Caravans are not crammed in. Cabins are well spaced. Close to the amazing Long Jetty and a short stroll to the General Store and pub.  The general store has best fish and chips and burgers, whilst the pub has exceptional meals.  Overall worth a stay at Port Welshpool.
Absolutely brilliant, very happy with this caravan park. Friendly helpful staff and amenities always clean. Lawns neat and tidy. Great place!
Friendly and helpful. Great location. Good clean facilities and not too expensive.

Caravan site was large with power and water. Tv reception good. we had shady trees. Long jetty was a great walk we had a fish but no luck. Would definitely stay again.
Cameron and Trina
Unpowered site.
Nice caravan park, roomy sites and friendly staff.  Location great but clearly best for people who are there to fish
Caravan sites really large and private. Amenities nice and clean. Had an dxcelkdnt 3 nights.
Fantastic caravan park good fishing

Awesome caravan park! Extremely well kept & clean. Caravan sites are spacious, kids loved the play ground. Great location for fishing off the jetty, can buy bait at reception too! Would recommend & I’ll definitely return!

Unfortunately it was raining when we arrived and our spot was a bit boggy but was helped out straight away by the lovely staff and mived to a dryer spot. Only stay a couple of night while in transit but enjoyed our stay regardless of the weather
Nice clean Park  friendly staff
Off season travelling, so nice and quiet.  We had a Spacious site backing on to the community bowling club, which hubby enjoyed a few games. Walked the jetty. Well maintained.
Great stay

Very well designed cabin, extremely comfortable and clean. Pleasant n helpful staff, would definately come back again, as well priced n good position, everything we required for a enjoyable stay in Lakes Entrance.
A friendly and helpful greeting upon arrival and clean, well  appointed amenities block.|A very enjoyable stay.|I’ll be back🤓
Really lovely staff and clean accomodation. Great amenities & always clean. Thanks for a good stay!
Clean and good location

Great central location walk to beach, shops, Pubs, Sunday market
Amenities a little dated but very clean
Just what I needed, locality, cleanliness, price and hospitality was great
It was value for money. The location, friendly staff and cleaninless was very good. The laundry had a very good dryer. Grounds were neat and tidy. Would recommend.
Friendly staff, clean, well equipped cabin in a great location

We stayed 3 nights in our caravan.Ideally using the front of the park during winter.
Close to amenities and laundry.Was amazed at being charged $7 a load for washing,most expensive in our 4 mths  away.🙄
One of the best amenities block though unfortunately let down by tepid and low pressure showers
Although this wasn't the case for the men.
Have mentioned this  to reception interested to see if there is a difference tomorrow.
Walking distance to the town and the 90 Mike beach walk...
Yes ,would recommend this park.👍

No fuss check in.  Plenty of room for 2 of us in the 2 Bedroom Cabin.  Bed was comfortable with fluffy pillows.  Linens all appeared near new & white.  Cabin had signs of recent renovation, comfortable for us.|Walking distance to local attractions, river & shops.|However the lounge chair may need an upgrade soon!
Terrific little caravan Park close to everything
Great place to stay walking distance to everything, either a powered site or a dog freindly room you will enjoy your stay and the amenities are clean and plenty to do for the kids.

Excellent central location, close to the footbridge with access to the beach. Nice neat and tidy gardens, and really good value for money compared to some other sites. I chose Waters Edge so that I could bring my two dogs with me. We therefore got the budget cabin, which was a little tired and basic, but that's to be expected where dogs are allowed. As I shared with an employee, the screen door didn't lock, there was a broken handle to a kitchen cupboard, and the net curtains had holes in. My stay might have been better if my cabin was not next to the main road. All in though, great value for money and a great spot. I would like to return (when the weather is warmer!)
Had a amazing time  good and friendly staff

Could have been better. Great location however we booked a powered site. They put us on one where the power wasn't working  waisted 40 mins trying to solve the problem. Went and spoke to them and said you need to connect here  Go figure no apology. The camp was 20 % occupied

Stay here quite frequently for work, this last time for a whole week, disappointed that not once do you get clean towels, a cabin that sleeps 5 had 2 towels for 5 days, no room serviced at all, saw another review saying the same with response “this is not a motel” well you can stay in a motel for $100 we were charged $175 per night for a little cabin. Have stayed in hundreds of places that service the cabins during your stay.
The second bedroom doesn’t even have a door just like a divider slide across curtain that doesn’t even close properly
Very poor service for the price
Asked for clean towels and was told to goto reception before 6pm 🤯

Pretty good place to stay but camp fires are prohibited 🚫  which I'm not happy about

There's only 2 washer and dryers for the whole park and they get busy straight away making it hard to do laundry.

You need a 20 cent coin to use the bbq which is a bit silly in these times, also somewhat unfair given you pay to stay where there are these facilities.

The sites are far too small. It was over crowded.We were supposed to stay 3 nights but we only stayed for 1. Couldn't wait to get out of there...
Management were great.

First of all the staff are accommodating..My first sight was under a tree at the back of the park, dropped stuff on the roof all night.
It i was also stuck at the back of the park which is pretty dingy.
They happily moved me to the front of the park on a beautifully grassed site very close to the Esplanade.
I'm delighted to spend another 3 days there.
Generally the park is older and rather than picturesque it's functional.
Obviously there are some nicer spots than others.
It's very close to the bridge to the surf beach and shops.
I took my campervan to the all day paying bay close by the water shops and bridge.
Now I've got this new site I probably won't in future.
It's a mixed bag here but most of the parks in Lakes seem to be older.
Great place with caravan but peak time be aware peak times there isn’t much room sites are small. But apart from that not too bad

Smallest sites I have experienced in 20 years camping and caravanning .
Both vans in our party had to get cars moved to get in and then squeeze our car on edge of the road to get all on the site . Our awning almost touched our neighbours van.
It was like developers cutting up a metro housing estate….shameful . Your neighbours almost on top of you.
Never again … no longer Big 4 too.

1 star too many for this place.

What a shocking excuse for a campsite. Should not be sold for caravans. The pitch was so small I couldn’t get my awning out properly.
I had to move my car over 10 times for people to get in or out as there is no space.

Shocking site for the price. Will not be back. Will be back to the lakes just not this camp.

Highly recommend the Executive Studio. Location is on main street, couple hundred meters from shops - we walked everywhere! Studio looked new and was very clean. We didn't use other facilities in park - pool, games room, kitchen, for a small holiday park it was well equipped.

I had an amazing time at Waters Edge Holiday Park!
Waters Edge Holiday Park is a great place to take your kids as it has many fun things for them to do! They also have events for the kids on the holidays because the place will be full of kids. Some of the fun things are, a pool, a jumping pillow and a totem tennis area. And for the holiday events, I only know for easter because that was when i came. So for easter it has an egg hunt and whenever you find an egg, if you open it then it had a prize in it! There was also a cookie decorating competition!
In my opinion, this place is great for family vacations.
Everything you’d expect from a budget cabin and more!

Wir hatten vorab einen 5-Tagesaufenthalt gebucht. Als wir kurz nach Büroschluss dort ankamen, wurde uns aber nochmal zum einchecken geöffnet und uns alles gezeigt. Auf Wunsch haben wir den Stellplatz direkt neben dem Hüpfkissen bekommen. So konnten unsere Kinder vom Wohnmobil aus direkt zum Spielen. Auch der Pool hat an den heißen Tagen seinen Zweck erfüllt. Sonnenschirme wären aber bei der prallen Sonne schön gewesen.
Die Stellplätze sind alle eng beieinander, aber bei der Vergabe hat das Personal darauf geachtet, dass - so lange es die Kapazität hergab - immer eine Platz zwischen den Campern frei blieb.
