Die Toiletten und Duschen sind etwas veraltet, aber ausreichend vorhanden. Außerdem gab es einen Wäscheraum mit Waschmaschine und Trockner, sowie ein Kinderbad, mit kleiner Toilette, Waschbecken und Dusch-/Badewanne.
Der Spielraum ist etwas lieblos eingerichtet. Dort kann man AUD gegen Token eintauschen und damit dann an diversen Spielautomaten spielen.
Zudem gibt es die Möglichkeit, gegen 1 AUD am Gasgrill zu grillen und sich Eispakete an der Rezeption zu holen. In den Ferien kommt 2-3 Mal pro Woche der Eismann mit dem Eiswagen direkt auf den Campingplatz.
Das WLAN hat leider überhaupt nicht funktioniert.
Strand, Spielplätze und Einkaufsmöglichkeiten sind fußläufig zu erreichen und übermäßig vorhanden.
Alles in allem, kann man den Campingplatz empfehlen.
Very close to the shops and Restaurants. Walking distance.

Were given a site with concrete slab on wrong side, didn’t advise us. We had to change sites.
All sites are very small and hard to navigate with a 8 metre van.
Positive feedback is great location, close to town, shops and walking tracks. Toilets and pool all kept very clean.

Spacious sites with plenty of grassed areas and easily accessed. Perfectly located for access to the boardwalk along the waters edge and parklands. Amenities were clean and tidy and included what you would look for in a holiday park. Staff were helpful and accommodating.

Having stayed in may parks, i wouldn't rate this a park as somewhere i would love to stay at again. There is nothing super wrong with it, it's fairly well priced and short walking distance to shops but the site are very small and the facilities are a strange set up and showing there age. Dump point really needs to have a clean-up hose. People are spilling sewage around the toilet block with noway to clean it up.

Just at the edge of shops heading to Merrimbula, easy walk to everything though and straight across from board walk and a small beach area for kids to play.
Park is getting a bit old, but the managers more than make up for that, always out and about in the park, its very clean and tidy, we weren't happy with our sites so they went out of their way to place us in  a different one, However the park sites are very tight, roads very narrow, would be good if owners could upgrade to suit the size of caravans and cars these days....all drive through sites would be much better, or use a "marriage saver tractor" to park vans up.....something needs to be done to make the management's life easier, they don't stop all day.

Great place and great value for your dollar. Not too big not too small, family friendly animal friendly great facilities but the highlight of the trip is the hospitality. The owners are super friendly and incredibly super nice humans to deal with. Highly recommended in all aspects. A++++++

Very small and run down, sites way to small and impossible to access if vans are coming and going. Sites so close you are kept awake by people talking next door. Second had smoke an ussue with sites crammed so close.2 showers were hot but only 2. Muddy underfoot, drains not cleared so water sitting on sites and roads. Not all sites have slabs. Grass unmowed. Very close to trees so cars and vans covered in droppings. WiFi impossible to access. Pool Very small and not always clean. Great position near the water and relatively close to shops and walks. Office staff efficient. Wouldn't recommend.

Our site was the first on the left after the cabins as you drive in. On the day of our exit we had to go left down the narrow road as there were too many obstacles to go right and straight out of the park. Both gates were closed at the other side of the park so we had to find somewhere to turn around and go out the entrance. This was after 10am. Single lanes, tiny, lumpy bumpy sites. Not our favourite park.
Sites very tight to get into when sites around are occupied, not much room to have awning out as well as car space.  Sites at back of park needs maintenance.
Great place for a get away, Clean and very functional Cabins at a reasonable price. walking distance for a fishing venture at the Long Jetty
Great location with sites spread out to give  plenty of room. Close to the pub for dinner. Facilities were  clean.

Another great stay. Extended our stay from 4 days to a week because the weather was so good. Took the new boat out and managed to land some gummies and flathead.
Thanks again for another great stay. See you next time.
Highly recommend Big4 Long Jetty Caravan Park. Staff are friendly everything is clean and the updated camp kitchen looks great.
Big thumbs up

Great place for camping and fishing.
Kid friendly and very clean facilities.
The only downside there is a blanket ban on canp fires for half of the year in this area.

v clean, well equipped and appointed, manager very helpful with items I left behind by dropping them.off at the local post office.
will stay here again no worries
Big grassy sites and spotless facilities, will stay here again.
Was everything we needed and wanted for our weekend away. We had a pet friendly cabin which was great. The quilts on the bunks weren’t super fresh but the sheets were clean.

Great park, huge campsites.
We will definitely be back

Great park  for camping not cramped up near others

Only missing a pool
But close to beach makes up for it

Will be back again
Friendly service, sites have plenty of room for van and camp set up everything you need . In a great location walk straight out of park onto to beach
Extra large caravan sites, with a comfortable rec and games room for all year use. Nice touch having free afternoon entertainment.
Beautifullly presented caravan park  lovely & green, plenty of space at rear near gate to long jetty.

Awesome caravan park , great location.
The manager that booked us in was very polite and professional. Don’t think I’ve ever been happier with a caravan park . 100% the best park ever
Water taps leaking and told the office,but no one came to fix it and the midges were not good but other then that we had a good stay
I’m not going to deal with a virtual assistant to book at this caravan park, it’s ridiculous when you want to talk to a human about different aspects of the park, so you’ve missed out on a booking
Great spot but found the sites extremely small.
Very hood pricing. Handy location to most things. Very tight spacing between  cars

We've stayed at waters edge for a week.  We've particularly booked this caravan park as it belongs to Big4 and our kids were looking forward to staying at a Big4.
Unfortunately it was not at all what we expected. Small and run down place, our campsite was right next to the wheelie bins and we could hear people on the toilet. Only 2 out of 6 ladies showers had water pressure. 2x six packs of beer were stolen from the community fridge in the kitchen and the small pool my kids were sooo looking forward to, was closed for the first 3 days due to pump issues. Advertised free wifi didn't work and when we questioned it at reception,  we were told that the signal of the modem might not be strong enough to reach our campsite. We got the staff one no problem though!!! To top it off, the last 2 days we found our campsite in the middle of 4 families travelling together with a total of 10 toddlers. It was like holidaying at a daycare centre. I'm sure it's great fun travelling with friends and for the kids it must be mega fun. Unfortunately it is not for all surrounding camp sites.  It would've been a lot nicer for all involved if they had a corner to themselves instead of setting up camp right in the middle of the entire camp ground.

Terrible service received and rude staff with 0 tolerance in special circumstances. Was in hospital and they didn’t offer change of dates / camp credit for future use. I’d rather leave my campsite and empty than cancelling. But they cancelled my booking straight away for others to book (earning double money). Disgusting.
Great location but as a caravan destination very ordinary and oh so tight. Not worth the $53 per night

We had a fantastic stay at this place and would highly recommend it, especially for families with kids.

Be sure to book in advance as the park was fully booked with people, kids, caravans, and boats everywhere. Although it was a tight squeeze, the location was ideal, with the water just across the road, as well as pubs, shops, and local produce pop-up shops within walking distance.
I thoroughly enjoyed the delicious fish tacos and prawns from a nearby pop up and boat. The site was well-equipped, and the park's bathrooms were clean and modern.

The only issue we encountered was difficulty in contacting the property to book an extra night, but the friendly staff apologized and made up for it by offering an early check-in.

If a clean pool and toilet block are important to you…thiss is definitely not the place for you. Would not recommend or be visiting again.
Convenient location though.

Stayed on a powered site in our caravan. Unfortunately our experience was ordinary due to the partying, bad language, drinking and urinating behind the permanent site 104 and 105. Understand New Year's Eve people party to 12pm but to still be going at 2pm is inconsiderate. During the day on the 31st December they were urinating against the fence and boasting of getting alcohol without showing ID. Other nights they were also drinking and talking loudly with music pumping well past the 10pm time limit in the park rules. Management acted on our second last night.

The showers at night were not warm for half of the week and the amenities basic.

The sites are also very tight with narrow roads, which seems to be an issue at most parks in Lakes Entrance.

The park is very close to the town main shopping strip so location was great.

New owners took over this park just over a year ago and have done a great job bringing it back to life. 3 years we've come and stayed the same dates and the same cabin and are very impressed. Location, location, location, close to everything you need in town. It is not large, it is crowded, you are close to your neighbors, go stay out of town if that is not for you.

New owners took over this park just over a year ago and have done a great job bringing it back to life. 3 years we've come and stayed the same dates and the same cabin and are very impressed. Location, location, location, close to everything you need in town. It is not large, it is crowded, you are close to your neighbors, go stay out of town if that is not for you.
Awesome location, minutes from lake/city center and surf beach. Very friendly staff and great amenities. Ideal camping location for lakes entrance. Kids loved the pool and jumping pillow 😀

Perhaps one of the smallest parks in the Big4 family. Narrow streets; difficult to park vehicles and cramped camp sites. Reception has only 3 parking spots, and the people line up behind at the Check-in time, ending up blocking the road.
It has a small pool, a kitchen, a gaming room (BYO coins); no slides etc for kids.
Great value for camping, shower pressure was good and facilities nice and clean. Thanks

Enjoyed the stay. Across the road from the water, easy walking distance to shops and woolworths.
Good for the cost, we stayed in budget cabins with our dogs.
It had what we needed and the cabin and site had reasonable facilities.
Pool was not heated. Has a bouncing pillow and games room.
It can be difficult for find a place that allows dogs. Would come again.
John

We had a powered site for our caravan for 3 nights here. Nice and close to the shops, right across the road from the water. Kids enjoyed the games room with arcade machines and the jumping pillow. Pool was ok- bit dirty and underwhelming. Bathrooms were fine. Unfortunately had a mishap with a water pipe overflowing and leaking water all across our campsite, leaving our clothes, kids toys and annex matting wet… We were disappointed by this but overall an alright stay.
Great spot,ideal location . Nice grassy sites.

Booked a Pet Friendly cabin. Was horrified that Waters Edge aligns itself with Big4, far from the quality expected.
The interior was so old and dilapidated, chipped and ripped furniture and unrepaired hole in a door. The exterior had multiple trip hazards.
The receptionist stated it was in this condition as it was Pet Friendly! I handed the key back after finding somewhere else to stay.
DO NOT STAY IN THIS CABIN NOR THIS PROPERTY.

We stayed for a night in one of the new cabins. They were incredibly well equipped with anything you’d need and it was super clean. We had an isssue with the stove in the morning and the staff were very quick to help and make sure it got sorted asap. We would stay again
Nice and quiet. Very clean and close to everything. Very happy. ,

Clean, comfortable, and great location. Staff are friendly.
Had a great week ☺️
This was our first stay at the park and had a great 👍 stay amenities were clean there was plenty for the kids to do the location was great we will be back again soon 😀
Great little caravan park. Friendly park managers. Clean amenities. Great camp kitchen.
What a dump! Very disappointed. We will never stay here again! Disgraceful. Seems like I am not the only one with bad reviews.

Good  sized  grassy sites,  manager helpful  and  friendly, amenities  oldish  but  clean,  pet  friendly,  park  beside  main  road  but  no  night  noise,  walking  distance  to  shopping, across the  road  to  lakes  entrance  water  attractions,  tremendous  bicycle  paths  along  waterways.

Lovely park. Facilities are good and in a good location walking distance from the front and the shops. Kitchen just needs more cutlery and crockery but otherwise great. Would stay again if passing through. Recommend to anyone staying in Lakes Entrance.
Love the place- 2nd trip with our Caravan.. really enjoyed and Kids loved it. clean and tidy.. staff are great.

Excellent park and facilities especially camp kitchen. Very clean and neat. Staff extremely helpful. So good that we stayed an extra night. Great grass sites and location is prefect. Ross Peterborough
Simple with everything you need in amenities. Very close to town and water.

Nice quiet park with really good camp kitchen. Close to shops. Ample space for those with large or small caravans. Amenities were clean, close and  showers hot. The area looks very touristy with ample selection of coffee shops and fish n chips
Quite small van spaces all the parks here seem to be. This was the better located park.
Easy access to the Esplanade and the beach. Walking distance to the shops.

I don’t normally leave ratings but this needs to be said
As a big 4 member I was very disappointed with this park is far below big4 standards
As a family we book Big 4 parks so the kids have things to do.
Upon our arrival the playground was closed due to the slide broken the jumping pillow was also closed and a notice on the gate saying it has been closed since December 2023
Than walking in to the games room and everything was switched off
The pool had dead cockroaches in it and was unwelcoming to swim in
The the baby bathroom was locked and the code given wasn’t working
While the amenities were clean they are dated and most of the taps need nuts on them as they kept falling off while trying to use them
Then on day 2 of our stay to have some one from the park approach us and say to us after my wife let them know we weren’t impressed that every reason we booked this park was close . The gentleman approach us with a racists remake about a group of people how were to stupid to understand that the slide was close so it was just easier to close the whole playground

So in summary every reason we booked this park wasn’t working or not available the park is under maintained and should not have a big 4 name attached

While the park was located in a great location and cheaper than most we were left disappointed with this park and won’t be back to this one
The lady in the office was nice and helpful with things to do and places to go
But overall impression was underwhelming

My stay was the second time 12 months apart. The camping grounds and facilities are magnificently managed and maintained. Office staff are kind and helpful
Other campers seemed considerate of others around them and mostly up for a good morning and a chat. Met some nice folk

Basic but clean and tidy holiday park. Kids loved the playground. Very close walk to the Jetty. Not as much to do at the actual premises as some of the Big4's but beach is close, good if you like fishing. Very quiet and relaxing.

Lovely caravan park, park amenities were spotless. We didn't use the camp kitchen or playground but they look fantastic too. We stayed in a drive through powered site in our van. Short walk to the Long Jetty, even caught a fish. We will definitely be back again.
Peaceful Park,Excellent ammenities.

Very friendly helpful service. Stayed on a large powered site. Park is clean and tidy with good location, jetty across the road and a short walk to pub and general store. Was able to do some of the rail trails also.
Lovely quiet park, large grassy sites, amenities block clean, but could do with an update

Great Caravan Park ,Good size grassed site , Clean amenities and the Park is Dog friendly, Access gate down to great walkways , Long Jetty and the Waterfront, Friendly Staff,
Whilst Port Welshpool is sparse it is a great base for exploring the South Gippsland area 👌

Beautiful park lovely powered sites and great amenities thank you
Cheers Steve
Great spot with very roomy powered camp sites. Good boating and fishing.
Brilliant Small Caravan Park ,Good size sites and Very clean amenities
Nice park with good amenities at a great location

Enjoyed our weekend away even though the weather turned. Extremely clean and well kept.
Also Pet friendly

Car tenting holiday, stayed at Port Welshpool long Jetty park, all staff helpful & friendly, camp kitchen had adequate equipment & very clean, relaxed in the activities room, also very clean & comfortable, other amenities also clean, I liked there were trees around many of the sites, for us was a good wind break with the tent. Will stay there again.
Great location for a Van park loved the camp kitchen and large grass sites

What a lovely place- a town like the ‘old days’  no trendy anything, one shop ( post office & fish n chips in one, true!) along shore- beautiful beaches to walk along, a really long jetty as the name suggests… super dog friendly - one polite sign and none of the NO this and that bull… park itself is great, friendly and helpful staff, nice modern facilities and easy access to Terra Bulga NP rainforest and other local paces- highly recommended! ( stayed in tent so can’t rate cabins/ rooms)
Sehr nett empfangen worden 😊 Saubere Toiletten & Duschen, wie auch Campkitchen & Aufenthaltsraum.
Great camp with roomy sites, clean facilities and great walk to the Jetty and beaches

Very run down park with broken down facilities (playground and jumping pillow out of action, luke warm showers).
Close to main street is good.

Amenities are pretty good for a caravan park. Sites are just big enough for a caravan and car. Staff are friendly and helpful. Pool is cold and jumping pillow (like most these days) has been deleted. Great location close to the town and water. Playground needs to be repaired and/or upgraded.
Small crowded park with run down facilties, I'm surprised Big 4 are happy to put their name to it. Historically I've never second guessed when booking big 4, but will be more cautious in future.
Not well layed out, very tightly squeezed in

Not up to scratch as far as Big 4's go....

People were lovely - couldn't fault their cheeriness and politeness, but the park itself left a lot to be desired.

Playground was in a terrible spot (right beside the main road) and majority of it was taped off like a crime scene due to broken plastic/unsafe equipment.

The jumping pillow looked as though it had been slashed and buried in the sand (my 6 year old actually asked if it was a "dirty sandpit") and as a result it was chained up with "Warning Do Not Enter" signage.

Van spaces were definitely on the smaller side and most caravaners had to ask neighbours to move cars or vans to be able to back in.

Amenities were well maintained - clean and definitely user friendly.

Short stay on the way up the coast, but unlikely to return.

Probably the worst BIG4 I’ve ever visited.

Arrived after front office closed for the day. Information packet left in the locked safe for us. Went to the assigned campsite and someone had parked in the lot. Called the after-hours number and was told to call back in 15 mins as they were waiting for information from management. Was told that they could not call me back (despite having my contact number) to update me. Very weird. Was eventually sorted out after call back.

I felt that the sites were cramped close together.

Bathrooms were dirty and smelled bad. Lock code provided but the door was never closed properly.

Camp kitchen was adequate.

Overall, nowhere near the normal standards of a typical BIG4. Seen a few reviews asking why the park remains affiliated with BIG4, can’t help but wonder the same. Would not recommend.

I enjoyed my stay. The park wasn’t to big so there wasn’t heaps of people. It was so quiet and peaceful.
Amenities were nice and clean. Great kitchen with everything you need! The pool was an added bonus, I didn’t go in but great for the kids.
Location was perfect, walking distance to all the shops and restaurants along the esplanade and it was so good waking up and hearing the sound of the ocean in the morning.
I highly recommend.
Great position, amenities are clean , site was level and a bit tight , Our power pole definitely needed some seeing to , Logistics are a bit how you going , But it is a nice park

We stayed at the park for Australia Day it was the worst experience here is the letter I wrote to complain to Big 4 head office they informed me that all parks are run independently and that the park has been notified of my complaint I still have not heard anything to date from management of the park.

Dear Sir/Madam,

I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to express my profound dissatisfaction with my recent stay at the Big 4 Caravan Park - Waters Edge. As a loyal customer who has never lodged a complaint before, I feel compelled to bring to your attention the numerous issues that have marred our experience at your establishment.

Firstly, I would like to highlight a significant discrepancy in your advertising. Both on your brochure and at the jumping pillow area, it is clearly stated that pets are not allowed. However, during our stay, we have observed numerous instances of large dogs in cabins, off-leash throughout the park, and even in the pool area. This directly contradicts the advertised policy and has resulted in an unsanitary and uncomfortable environment.

Furthermore, the amenities at the park were shockingly dirty. Broken playground facilities and a pool contaminated by dogs drinking from it were particularly distressing. The lack of proper maintenance reflects poorly on the overall standards of cleanliness and safety at Waters Edge.

The issue of multiple cars per site was also concerning. While I had assumed a limit of one car per site for safety reasons, it appears that this policy is not being enforced. This has led to a chaotic and potentially hazardous situation within the park grounds.

Lastly, it is disheartening to note that the current management, who also own a barking dog, seems indifferent to these issues. Our group, consisting of four other vans, shares the sentiment of disappointment and disgust with the current state of affairs.

In light of these numerous grievances, I kindly request that your head office addresses these concerns promptly. The current 8.2 rating advertised on your website does not align with the reality we have experienced, and I believe a more accurate reflection would be closer to a 3 star rating

I appreciate your attention to this matter and look forward to hearing from you soon to discuss the steps that will be taken to rectify these issues and ensure a more positive experience for future visitors.
Regards
Peta Barnes

I'm surprised this part of the big 4 group we have stayed on 2 previously and they have been outstanding, I can only presume its because of location, certainly not facilties, pitches were poor and amenities v poor, my partner had to try a couple of showers before getting hot water.. the mens facilities were very tatty peeling surfaces and generally  grubby..

We have stayed there from 24/01/2023 to 28/01/2023. Not a nice place to camp. Not much place to ply for kids. Toilets are too old and not cleaned much. Just like most of the hotels in Sydney cbd, rough and dirty.

We recently stayed at the waters edge holiday park. Being a big 4 park we were confident that it would be a good stay however this was not the case.

Some things were annoying were as others were not safe! I have listed them all for follow up

Issues with cabin 43:
- Air con leaked out the front of the unit.
- Tap in the bathroom sink wouldn’t turn off.
- Plenty of water damage on almost every wall.
- One hot plate (of two) didn’t work
- Electrical short in the bathroom light that affected microwave and fan.
- The doona was horrendous. Cheap plastic blanket from Kmart.

Our friends had a more modern looking cabin but had the same issue with the bed and bedding and their air con unit made so much noise they couldn’t use it at night or it would wake their baby.

General park issues:
Broken ‘shop counter’ in playground
Broken slide in playground
Both of these were plastic and brittle from the sun. The slide was broken where the little bums actually slide down!!
One of the pools (the kids one) was green!
And they had banned use of the jumping pillows

I tried to raise the issue of the air con and leaking tap with the park who didn’t seem overly phased but said they would send someone over, however the person never came.

Probably the worst Big 4 I've stayed at. Still a bit baffled how it is listed as a Big 4 given lack of facilities for the kids.

Their jumping pillow was deflated due to an insurance pricing issue and in the interim they have just locked the area off. The playground is in a terrible location at the front of the park right on the road. Also, when we went the plastic has started cracking so badly that it had to be taped off so no one would injure themselves (The whole playground needs replacing).
Their pool has a little toddler wading pool to the side which was green the entire time we were there and staff would just mention 'Dont go in that pool' as they came past.
There was a games room but everything was coin operated except the ping pong table.

Aside from this, the sites are terribly small and you get packed in like sardines with no room for the kids to play unless there are empty sites. There is a main road through the caravan park which goes from the reception to the back gate and a lot of sites and cabins border it. Family's had a great opportunity to repeatedly teach toddlers about road safety as the amenities were on the other side. I heard a lot of 'Watch the road!' or 'There's a car coming!' or 'Stop! Kids, get off the road's. Added a huge level of unnecessary stress for a number or families.

The amenities were also poorly maintained. Soap dispensers were empty for days in a row, there was diarrhea splotches from someone who didn't make it to the toilet in time over the concrete path that lead to the amenities. Despite this being reported to the staff it was just left there to dry out in the sun while unknowing guests walked through it.

Overall, I would not recommend this park at all and feel it should actually be stripped of any affiliation with Big 4. I can only assume it is still afloat because it is located close to the beach and main street.

In die Jahre gekommen. Gestört hat uns, dass mit Leistungen geworben wurde, die nicht mehr verfügbar waren. Bspw. das Hüpfkissen für Kinder. Auch der Spielplatz war ordentlich in die Jahre gekommen. Barbecue kostet extra. Küche gut. Auch Duschen und Sanitär gut. Lage zur Stadt sehr gut. Spielraum wirkt schmutzig und vieles nur Bezahlautomaten.
Best caravan park in Lakes..

Great caravan park. The amenities are very clean and well maintained.
A great location central to all of the key things that you would want to do in lakes entrance

One of the best caravan parks we visit on regular basis.
Excellent Hosts
Not a bad camp area, plenty of room
Very well priced and very new / clean amenities

Park Manager very helpful and easy to deal with. Amenities are modern and spotlessly clean.  Caravan sites are large with easy access, majority have lush bare foot grass. Laundry 2x washing machines 2x dryers card payment. Greyhound club next door (easy walk) with good meals at a fair price. Just outside main gate is a large Caravan friendly petrol station.

Great place to stop while travelling. We came in from bush camps and happily charged up and caught up with laundry. Very clean facilities and the staff very friendly. They were were very accomodating when we found a mechanical issue with our van. Thank you. Will be back when passing through again.

This place is awesome - great location, sale is beautiful - park is great - amenities are gorgeous even though they are 8 years old already. The pride Liz shows managing this part is up there with the very best. So happy to have landed here!
Nice place lots of grass and good price $40 Per night.

Claiming to be a caravan park is a big stretch..amenities really clean, but not secure, was an event at showgrounds all the park facilities were locked open for members of public to use... no camp kitchen available was locked, concerning when men wandering into women's showers constantly ...unpowered no water site $40 a night crammed like sardines as only space usable was in front of a shed as showgrounds booked for event ....if booked thru show grounds power and water for $35...felt very ripped off..proper park down the road found only $6 more.  Reception ok although not appropriately dressed in our opinion crop tops aren't a professional image .
Its nice place to stay and beautiful people running it that Will help you ,%100.

Great place to stay, friendly management and lovely food at greyhounds and lovely toilets blocks and lovely showers also lovely cleaned and detailed and well looked after including beautiful laundry always tidy and looked after and lovely vouchers with 20% for us to us to use at the greyhounds what a kind thing from management.
Nice toilets and showers ....plenty of room to park
Worst camp in 10 weeks. Expensive and they spent about 9 hours on the noisy ride on mower.
I decided to stay at this van park for the weekend, the staff are friendly and welcoming, the amenities are well kept and clean, and l had a most enjoyable time
Great place to stay and unwind. Verry well maintained , sites, toilets etc. Really nice managers . Thanks , stayed  several times will be back

Really friendly and helpful manageress and great facilities - beautiful trees and a green, green oval.

BUT, $35 a night is too too much for an Unpowered site …..

A well maintained showgrounds with everything you need for a wonderful stay. Green grass, big sites and new and clean amenities. To our fellow travellers please have some respect for the caretakers of these places and abide by the office hours and check in times - it's not that hard to do! These caretakers generally work 6-7 days a week! I am pretty sure you wouldn't want to take a call or check in someone at 7am or 10pm. Thanks Liz and Co for a wonderful stay. We appreciate all you do!

Great place to stay whilst visiting Sale and what a great place Sale is. Within easy walking distance to town. Close to local Club that runs a happy hour that seems like every hour. Caretaker (Liz) was very helpful and informative of what was happening over the period we were there. Good size sites and reasonably priced laundry.
Spotlessly clean.. better than most caravan parks
Stayed over the weekend while the swap meet was on. Amenities were Extremely clean, receptionist was lovely and welcoming  and a very nice environment for the kids

Sale Show Grounds, Great place to stay, good price and amenities I would say are in the top 3 we have ever seen, spotlessly clean. Grounds manager Liz is very welcoming,  friendly and informative on the local area for almost anything. Would definitely stay here again. 10/10 😊
Note: a must see when you’re there in Sale is the “Port of Sale Art Gallery” which is Awsome.
It was so lovely their we stayed an extra week. Lovely clean park. We were in a caravan. Grounds nice. Owners/managers very friendly.

Staff are great and informative.   Helpful to length and width of your vehicle on site.   Relaxing and enjoyable place to walk, ride a bike to grab fish and chips at the general store, or go to the pub for a meal.  All looking at the water

We stayed on an unpowered site at Long Jetty Caravan Park and found it to be excellent value for money. Our site itself wasn’t very large, but there were definitely bigger sites available throughout the park. It would be worth checking the park map in advance and requesting a specific site if space is important to you, especially if you have a larger setup.

The location is one of the standout features. Being right next to the Long Jetty and the beach makes it perfect for relaxing walks, whether during the day or at sunset. The coastal setting gives the park a peaceful and laid-back atmosphere.

The amenities were well maintained, and the bathrooms were clean and within easy walking distance from all areas of the park, which made things very convenient.

The camp kitchen is simple but functional and has everything you need. It uses portable induction cooktops, so make sure your pots are magnetic. If not, the kitchen provides compatible pots and pans, which was helpful.

The playground is basic but more than enough for kids to enjoy, and ours had a great time playing there. It’s a very family-friendly environment overall.

This park isn’t luxury, but it provides everything you need for a comfortable and affordable stay in a fantastic location.

We would happily stay again.
Great location for a fishing weekend,  clean facilities and lovely owners. Good caravan sites out of wind.

Fantastic place to stay, awesome sights and great for fishing, whether in a boat or off 'long jetty' - which is so well equipped & a very short walk from the CP!
The Caravan park is clean and well cared for. Great service on check in.  Loved my plot! Will specifically ask for that one again ;-)

We stayed in unpowered, on grass and only a short walk to the sea side. This park is highly recommended to stay at, well kept ground with options to choose from with site. Updated amenities, well equipped camp kitchen with BBQ and entertainment for kids.

My name is tunjay ozer I'm from Melbourne first time stayed in port welshpool
The caravan park was perfect the cabins were perfect clean
I will be going back soon
The staff were very professional and kind
I do recommend to anyone going to Port welshpool to stay there ..👌👌👌👌❤️
Absolutely amazing close to beach great paths for riding a bike, staff are pleasant and caravan park is lovely clean amenities grounds are well maintained.
Great camp kitchen!!!!

We stayed overnight on the powered camp site tucked into a cove out of thewind . Sites were great size access to water and power . Easy to get into and close to very clean amenities . Access to long jetty at back gate and great besch to walk dogs and ourselves . Owner was very helpful .

Was great to be able to stay in a dog friendly cabin.  Was really tight with the 3 of us and a dog.  Would be great if the bunk room was removed altogether and had a sofa bed in the living area instead.  That would free up a bit more space.  Would also be great if there was a small fenced off area , including thr balcony so you could sit outside with the dog comfortably.  The shower was a good one.  Otherwise the park was nice.  Other guests were lovely along with the staff.  Other guests were very courteous and considerate.  No issues with phone reception.  We visited Foster which was a nice town and Agnes Falls.   We made some nice memories with our kiddo and first holiday with our pup.
Was lovely  quiet peace full  amenities four and a half star
kindness, excellent cleanliness...large spaces

Lovely caravan park. The site we had was spacious and private, despite being close to everything. Facilities were spotless. A big thanks to the park manager who very politely asked our neighbour to quieten down after 10pm.

Lovely camp, pristine amenities. Management was accommodating to our needs.
Local pub and fish and chips were great. Highly recommended.

The sites are absolutely massive The amenity blocks are very clean. My overall opinion of the park is very satisfying I will be definitely making a second booking. Staff are very friendly and helpful.

We arrived late and had a hassle free check in.
We stayed for a night in a unpowered campsite. The facilities are clean and easy access to where we camp overnight. Spacious.
Easy walk to the long jetty which what we came for to see and explore. This place is great. Had the most amazing time walking the long jetty, serene and peaceful.

Public toilet and bathroom is clean.
Good security.
Nice kid playground.
Only one problem is some one from caravan let their dog popping on the other empty camp site. They repeated that next day (they’re from slot 26). Even they picked it up but I don’t think that was allowed.
Would go back for the cleanliness alone! Cleanest place we’ve ever stayed!

Great location but the weather was not very kind to us so not able to walk everywhere. The park is quite run down and needs some TLC. I am at a loss why they give a code for ladies toilets when they are constantly open, appears lock may be broken.  The roads in the park are very narrow and the sites are not equipped for large vans and not marked very well.  Maybe they should redesign to have bigger sites for large vans and cars.   Certainly not like any of the other Big 4's we have stayed at they have been more updated.
Lovely place to stay for a couple of nights. Straight across from the beach. Close to central hub of Lakes. Facilities are plenty but in need of maintenance
Great quiet park, lovely pool, jumping pillow for the kids, great sites and camp kitchen
Great place at Lakes Entrance to stay. Very nice clean campground friendly staff, toilets showers and amenities were very clean. I would recommend staying there and I will definitely be back.
Stayed at a powered site level flat and close to all amenities. Showers etc a little old and needed some attention. but otherwise clean. Camp kitchen good and on par with other Parks.

Karen and John were most helpful in organising the site and cabin and help with parking the van. The site was large and the infrastructure was very good. Possibly a bathroom overhaul might be worth it, but for the price it was comfortable and functional. I hope to be back soon.
We found the cabin to be clean and well appointed

I stay at this caravan park during Melbourne cup long week end. I would rare this caravan park 2 stars for the following reasons:

1. Advertise site with wifi. But i couldn’t find wifi from my site. When I ask the staff they told me my site was too far from the reception. It is not fair.
2. Caravan park very packed, Driveways very narrow, very hard to park the caravan.
3. My Trauma heater/airco stop working at the middle of the night. I brought my caravan to repair shop few days later and the repairman said the fuse was dead and likely caused by power surge at the caravan park. There was no storm during my stay so I think there is something wrong with the electricity. If you plan to stay there, make sure to bring a power surge protector!!!
4. Showers not clean
5. Water has plastic taste, pipes may not be food grade quality
6. Very noisy, there was 7 people in the neighbourhood site . From my view too many people per squares meter.
7. My “Grass” site had literally no grass, all dead. Lot of foods remain and insects
8. The “heated swimming pool” was horribly cold. My kids didn’t want to swim.

However good location and staff very nice.
Great park stay there every time we stay in lakes.

Great place for a family stay
👍
Great location walking distance to everything. We took our caravan, good flat areas for caravans. Great activities for kids, great amenities. Would recommend
Great location, facilities were always clean, service was friendly, if anything the caravan sites were too tight, but other than that good park.
It's okay, but a bit old, my wife thought the hot water wasn't hot enough.
Friendly staff , good amenities,
Not happy very runned down 👎
Paid $200 per night (off peak) for the most rundown park I’ve ever been to. In desperate need of some TLC. Do not recommend.
Great location and cosy cabin however cold tap in basin is seized so only hot water to wash face. Used kitchen sink instead

We had a powered site. It was the biggest site we have ever had and a great price.
The site was very private and sheltered.
The park was very clean and tidy, as were the shower / toilet blocks. The park is so close to the jetty and walking distance to the shop, pub etc.
The young girl on reception was very friendly and helpful. We will be back.
Beautiful location, our fur baby absolutely loved her time there.  She sulked when we returned home. We qull be back.

I booked the "Tiny Home" for a couple of days of fishing on the Long Jetty. The cabin was exactly as described on their website. The cabin was comfortable, very clean, well equipped and everything work fine. It had a comfortable bed, TV, powerful, quiet reverse cycle air-conditioner, table, 2 chairs, power outlets,  ensuite bathroom plenty of clean towels, liquid soap, shampoo and a front deck with 2 chairs and table. The kitchenette had a large fridge/freezer, plates bowls, glasses, cutlery, tea towel, sponge, detergent, microwave, kettle, sink, toaster, air fryer, complimentary UHT milk, instant coffee sachets, teabags, sugar. As other reviewers have stated, the park is very clean, has great environment, facilities and is serene even when full on an Australia Day long weekend. The staff are friendly, helpful, knowledgeable and switched on. Thanks for a great stay.

Stayed at big4 Long Jetty Caravan Park over the Australia Day long weekend 2026. The caravan park is super clean and tidy with green lush grass. We stayed on a powered site and the site sizes are very generous is size offering you some privacy and space between other campers. The park offers great facilities with a great camp kitchen, spotless amenities, playground for the kids and even a coffee van. It’s pet friendly for sites and selected cabins so you can bring your fur pets along to enjoy your holiday with you. Just a walk through the gates and you’ll be right by a stunning bay with views of the long Jetty which is a lovely walk. In town there is a pub (Rusty’s) which offer great meals, a general store, boat ramp if you decide to bring the toys along. It’s very peaceful and relaxing and the reception and staff are very friendly. Great spot to go exploring the Gippsland region, Wilson’s prom and many stunning beaches. Enjoyed and stay and hope to be back.
We stayed at the caravan site. Camp site was big and well maintained. Park was clean.
Can't wait to visit again at Easter time
Good place, good fishing, but sand flies just too much of a downside
Even though quiet and enough room  , only allowed to have one car on site
Good spot close to long jetty straight out the back door, make sure you try calamari from general store 10/10

Loved our stay there can't wait to come back again staff was friendly as people were great and friendly it was great seeing so many dogs in the park great location to the water and seeing the seal yesterday was amazing

Friendly helpful staff and well maintained campgrounds.
Bathroom and shower facilities was always
clean during our stay.

We had an amazing time at Long Jetty caravan park. The grass campsite was very spacious, with green soft grass. The facilities are new and very clean, the kids loved the play area and the Jetty is right out the back gate.

10/10 we loved our stay at the Long Jetty Caravan Park. Site was perfect and huge in size. The facilities spotless and close by to our site. Our grandson loved his daily icecream brought at reception and the great play park. Our favourite thing was the Charcoal Meat Man Food Van visiting the park, no need for cooking. The park is so close to so many lovely walks. The local pub the Rusty Gurnet only a short walk and great meals and happy hour. We will definitely be back again soon with more family members.
Fantastic park with great facilities and management. Experience was made even better by ‘That Charcoal Meat Guy’ serving up an assortment of goods for dinner. Definitely recommend.
We had a large site and grass was great.

Reasonably priced but basic facility. Operators helpful and welcoming. Mobile tower on site for great reception.
Issues - yes its dog on leash friendly, first thing that greets us on arrival is a steaming dog shit on our powered tent site. Also, these style of accom tend to attract ferals - had police on site during our stay due to a linatic teen who could not live without its phone for a few hours 😡

After free camping for nearly two weeks, it was such a treat to roll into the showgrounds and enjoy super clean amenities. Liz set us up under some beautiful trees with power and water, and it was wonderfully quiet.

At $40 a night, it felt like an absolute bargain—especially given the cleanliness. We’ve stayed in caravan parks for $60–$100 back in May, and this was far better: more space, peaceful surrounds, and not crammed in like sardines. The dump point was easy to access.
That’s for a restful 2 nights.
Absolutely loved our stay here. Professional and really friendly manager, Liz. Great environment, great facilities. Plenty of room and also great for groups if needed. Would highly recommend.

Here for 3 nights, power, water and sulage hookup. There are clean toilets and showers with hot water. There's a laundry and clothes lines.
Some of the camping area was closed off when we arrived due to weed spraying. We had the choice of 3 spots and took one along the fence, site 3. The Maffra sale road is busy and noisy but I think it would be noisy wherever you parked. The road is quieter during the evening and through the night, it didn't disturb me in the morning when traffic started up again. 40 dollars a night is the most we have paid at a showgrounds, so maybe on the expensive side. Not sure. We thought it was okay but we are happy anywhere.😆 So long as we aren't in work 👍🤭
Nicely kept flat grassy sites. Great amenities, including a card pay laundry. Dump point. Pet friendly on a leash. Short stroll to race track.
What a great place to stay! Awesome facilities and the caretaker was the best we have have delt with. Keep up the great work Liz!
Nice place

Facilities are very clean and modern.
Caravan sites well grassed and well maintained.
Jack, the caretaker,  was extremely helpful.
Great place to stay.
Dog injury caused mass pain and sound, people wouldn't put it our of its misery 😥

Four and ¾ stars.
A very nice dog friendly place to stay.

I have to admit I am in love with the amenities block. ♥️
Clean, well laid out and still looking brand new.
Great, clean and good value
Great Facilities
Well worth stop.

Last minute stop and met Grumpy " he named himself not me" but he is totally the opposite. Great site with modern facilities and good well kept grass and the surrounds. Checked in on us as arrived very late next morning, instructions were perfect and everything was just fantastic. Great personality and one of those places that you just know is clean, tidy and friendly. Thank you Grumpy.
Great venue

Powered caravan grass site. To be honest yes we've stayed at better but this caravan park was clean and tidy. Our water tap was under repair so we had to use another one, no big deal as when we stayed it wasn't busy. There was no concrete slab for us but we take our own mats anyway so that was ok. When you're travelling around you have to go with the flow. If you expect all parks to be 5 star all the time, then expect to pay for them. This was convenient to everything, close to shops and the lake was across the road. Its a little tired looking but was very budget friendly and the important things are great, clean amenities and lots of hot water 😊
Great location, very clean and well presented park! Definitely return next time!
Great park, close to everything and really good bike tracks along the esplanade.
Spotless amenities. Close to town. Very enjoyable stay

We made a booking in the morning 5/10 ,we arrived later that afternoon the receptionist was still there to show us to the powered site and so we can pay, we went to the site where we were supposed to, my husband had to use the toilet he was very disappointed he couldn't close the door and there were 2x other toilets but they were locked so my husband was not very so we left I tried to call the office to let them know but they had already closed I left numerous messages but they didn't call me back, we wanted our refund because we didn't even plug our van ,we're very disappointed with this park was very runned down service was shocking, still waiting for our refund not a big 4 at all would never stay there again!!
The site met our expectations and service was good as was facilities

Cabinet was clean, tidy. Had most items required supplied in kitchen/bathroom. Great to have a separate toilet room and not  with shower. The doona in main bedroom was not great to sleep under as had a paper type cover zipped on it, making it very noisy when sleeping. Great position and able to walk into town. Overall good few days.
We had a powered site so no review of rooms. We have stayed here before and that is why we picked it again. Nice tidy campsite. Amenities are clean and user friendly. Would stay again
Lovely smaller park close to shops lake and beach.
A great caravan park. A little dated in their colour scheme of chalets and ablutions. But overall clean and well appointed.

Had a powered site
Nice clean sites
Just next time I will ask not to be the bouncing pillow.
I am a teacher and would have loved to be away from kids.

A very nice, convenient and friendly caravan park that is centrally located to the town and close to the beach and waterways. The WiFi wasn't great but other than that, we have no complaints and would recommend this park.

travelling with our small dog we shied away from the accommodation we'd prebooked online as it looked unkempt. Late in the afternoon we approached Waters Edge park and the managers could not have been more welcoming and helpful.  the whole park & amenities were neat, clean & tidy and the onsite pet-friendly cabin we were given was perfect.  Very clean, neat & all the necessary basics. Stroll across the road to the esplanade boardwalk & over the footbridge to the surf beach. Fish'n chips, supermarket & bottleshop close by.  Congratulations to the management on your attitude and customer service. Thank you
Great location, just across the road from the Waters-edge and close enough to everything that you might need.
Magnificent place very friendly
1st time towing our van, arranged for a drive thru site for us.
Very helpful at reception. A lovely studio room with everything to make for a very pleasant stay. Well situated near the water. Would highly recommend
Supermarket, shops, clubs and water activities less than a five-minute walk, very clean amenities.

We stayed on a Caravan site x
Excellent position. Can walk to the shops and the beach .
Very Clean and staff very friendly x
Would definitely recommend ❤️

Check in time isn’t until 2:00. Sites are very small and not easy to get into. Would not recommend for large family van (ours is 22ft). Caravan park is very dated and not well maintained. Positive- in a very close location to town, can walk or ride bike to see local attractions.
Clean, comfortable cabin with friendly staff.

It's good to have a "basic"  place to pull into at a reasonable price.
Being over 50 years of age, we have very little interest in paying a fortune for all the "extras"  that we'll never use.
The ease of parking was a bonus, as was being able to speak directly to the office staff at the Park.
We felt like we knew the owners before we arrived.
It was also a great bonus in being almost beside the waters edge & the walkway that takes us into town.
Looking forward to returning in the warmer months to go swimming & fishing.
Gary Frame 06/07/2023.

Had a large site for our caravan.
Amenities are very clean. Recomended.
Great spot, close to everything, good sites, great amenities, highly recommended

Absolutely fantastic location
Lovely care takers
Clean facilities
The friendliness caravan parks we have stayed in lately, great for family and meeting people,pet friendly.
Lovely spacious land.  Very quite.
The park is professionally run, and any requests we had were handled quickly, efficiently  and with a minimum of fuss. Highly Recommended.

Pretty, but right on the highway, so sleeping is not easy, with trucks accelerating past all night. Apart from that, it was fine for the price.
Things they could improve:
Provide at least one sharp knife and a bread knife. There is only table cutlery. Need a chopping board and salad bowl too.
Provide a coffee table.
Put up a notice that the bathroom tap is backwards, so you don’t spend ages wondering why the water is not running hot.
Fix the bathroom doorknob, which falls off.
Bathroom light takes ages to come on.
Best not to comment
Lovely and quiet, but some road noise which was to be expected.

A really lovely caravan park. Super friendly locals that call the place home. Clean amenities and easy to book in. Located centrally to everything in town and easy to get to out of town places too. Would happily return. Thanks for having us.

A very pleasant surprise! What a picturesque park, along the canal, full of green areas and magnificent trees and close to the main town.  The accommodation was quaint clean and comfortable. Thank you for welcoming my pets. Staff were all warm welcoming and friendly.

I’ve just returned from a road trip to Sale, Victoria, where I’d the pleasure of staying at Sale Caravan Park. This park was located a short 3-4 minute drive to the main town which has plenty of great cafes, pubs, restaurants, shops as well as a great public pool/gym facility, beautiful Botanical Gardens and a picturesque lake with a lovely walking track all of which I utilised during my stay.

I personally chose to stay in one of the very new Tiny Houses that are now available on-site. I stayed in Tiny Houses #2, which was called “Tiny Bilby”. This small home was beautifully positioned on the outskirts of the caravan park with views of the surrounding countryside, ie. plenty of large native trees & lush green grass bordered by a small river.

Tiny Bilby was very clean, comfortable and well appointed. It contained;
• A comfortable loft style bed, with all linen, pillows & doona provided
• A private en-suite bathroom which included a shower, toilet and small hand basin with fresh bath towels, hand towels, face washers and soap provided.
• A smart TV with remote opposite a comfortable two seater couch
• A split system heater/air-conditioning unit which worked very well to maintain a comfortable temperature in the cabin 24/7.
• A small kitchenette which contained a fridge/freezer, a microwave, an induction cooktop, a toaster and a kettle and was definitely sufficient to allow one to self-cater meals.
• There was also a lovely deck with two outdoor chairs and a small table which was the perfect place to sit and enjoy a morning cuppa tea/coffee ☕️ or perhaps even evening drinks 🍷 and nibbles under the stars ✨

The service of all of staff that I made contact with on-site was very friendly and professional and the surrounding public ammenities were also very clean and well maintained. I noticed there was also a small laundrette on site which I found very handy, and reasonably priced - at the cost of $5 per washload or $5 per tumbledry load. PS: Best to BYO $1 coins, but coins can also be supplied at the main office which was located nearby at the entrance to the park.

Steve and Liz the Park Managers were very friendly and always available via mobile phone should you find yourself in need of assistance.

All I can say is that I’d definitely return and I can highly recommend ‘Tiny Bilby’ as a very comfortable, well positioned and self contained Tiny House in which to enjoy a quiet & relaxing getaway surrounded by nature.

Came down for a visit this weekend,
Went to The Wedge to see The Rocky Horror Theatre show.
What an amazing Caravan park.
Very friendly environment.
Staff are very welcoming.
Everyone in the Caravan park are friendly.
Will definitely be back to visit this amazing park.
Thank you to Steve and Liz for all the work you do to provide a relaxing and tranquil escape for everyone. 🥰
Stayed in Tiny Home Unit. Pleasantly surprised ! Yes it’s tiny but unit had everything we needed. Very clean & very comfortable. Decking & outlook excellent.  Staff were awesome.

Tidy quiet park
Clean amenities. Easy access from the highway. Not a lot of road noise.
Shared kitchen and bathroom facilities exceptionally clean. Good place to camp on large open field of grass. Would definitely return.

I stayed at this caravan park for 2 nights on the weekend, the staff were friendly and helpful and l had a wonderful time, if l had occasion to visit sale again, l would definitely stay at this caravan park

We enjoyed our stay on a powered caravan site. Beautiful lush green grass, tall gums, large caravan sites and close to town. The park was relaxing and Liz, the park manager, was very friendly and accommodating.

Our stay at Sale Caravan Park was lovely and very pleasant. Liz and Steven were so helpful and friendly throughout our stay. Great location walkable to town and great facilities, very clean and lots available. Will definitely be visiting again.
Stayed in one of the Quoll Cabins. Clean. Modern. Cosy. Bed was very comfortable. Perfect.

Great place neat and tidy. Lovely grass to park on and nice clean and new amenities block. I would’ve given it 5 stars but the miserable woman on the desk was very unwelcoming. When we asked if we could have a late checkout we were told it’s and extra $20. Really!  She needs training in Customer service.
Great grounds to stop for a night with our van. It was quite and easy to park our van
We booked last minute, but they were incredibly accommodating and even waited for us to arrive despite it being quite late. The facilities were spotless. Stunning plot backed onto the showground

Stayed here over Christmas and through the New Year. We have been to many parks over many years  and this is up there with the best .Liz the manager is very accommodating, friendly and a pleasure to deal with . Well maintained grounds , very clean facilities. We would recommend this park to anyone looking for a great stay . We will definitely be returning in the near future. Thanks Liz for making our stay enjoyable. Regards Rhonda & Michael Vickery.🌻🌻

An excellent option when visiting Sale is to stay at Sale Showgrounds Caravan Park.
The grounds are beautifully maintained with grassy and shady sites.
The amenities are very modern and meticulously cleaned. The LG washing machine and drier are possibly the best I’ve used, with no coins required.
Situated a short drive or bike ride into town to visit the Botanical Gardens, Port of Sale with a must do river cruise on the historical Rubeena (1912) to the swing bridge and a cold beer in the Criterion Hotel (1865).
An added bonus is a short walk through the park to the Greyhound Club. A first class experience with a fabulous bar and bistro, with excellent service offered by the staff.
Highly recommend Sale Showgrounds Caravan Park.

EXCELLENT facilities. Lovely grassy sites.

We stayed here for a week and had a beautiful time on a premium tenting site. The site was very spacious, and the kids absolutely loved being outdoors and exploring. A massive shout-out to Steve, the manager — these are the kind of park managers you want running your business. His kindness and sense of humour genuinely made our days better.

Now, some feedback for the owner. This place has so much potential and is set in a beautiful area. The boom gates really need fixing, and the old information centre is unsafe — smashed glass everywhere, which is especially concerning given how close it is to the kids’ playground. This area needs urgent attention.
With some investment, this park could be amazing. An oven, an additional BBQ area, and a better-equipped kitchen would make a big difference. A safer designated play area for children, and even a pool, would easily attract more families. It would also help if the cleaners had their own laundry facilities — it would make their job much easier and more efficient.
Despite these issues, this place is truly beautiful and peaceful.

With a tired and unsettled baby and a sick wife, I still managed to wash the dishes and clean the kitchen area as best as I could.|When my wife’s condition worsened, I went to the office to explain the situation. I apologised for not being able to complete the cleaning. The staff member told me, “No worries, we understand your family situation.” I thanked them and left.|However, two hours later, $200 was charged to my friend’s credit card who booked this place for us.|I wrote to management again explaining our circumstances, but I was told that “personal circumstances” are not considered in their decisions. The lack of empathy in that response was extremely disappointing. I made it clear that I did not object to paying a fair cleaning fee if necessary, but charging $200 after verbally acknowledging and understanding our situation felt unfair and insensitive.|This review is not about the money. It is about the lack of humanity and compassion shown toward a family with a sick mother and a one-year-old baby. We did everything we could with the limited supplies provided. There were no proper cleaning products or mop in the cabin, only a small number of tissues. Under the circumstances, I genuinely tried my best.|There were also safety concerns in Cabin 40. The rail of the top kitchen drawer (with cutlery) was faulty and collapsed onto my finger, causing injury. The gas cooktop ignitor did not work, and instead, a lighter was left in the cabin, which is unsafe in a small space, especially with a baby present.|We will be following up with Consumer Affairs Victoria regarding both the charge and the safety issues.|I truly hope management reflects on this experience. Families travel to holiday parks expecting a safe and supportive environment. Illness is not something people can control, but empathy and understanding are choices.|If the full $200 cleaning fee genuinely went to the cleaner, I can accept that. Otherwise, it feels like taking advantage of a family during a very difficult situation.|I sincerely hope no other family has to experience this.

We recently stayed at this holiday park and unfortunately had a very distressing experience.
During our stay, my wife suffered severe food poisoning. It was a very difficult night, especially as we have a one-year-old baby. We chose not to leave the park in the middle of the night (at night the drive could be dangerous especially with a baby), and my wife struggled with her illness until morning. As a result, our baby did not sleep at all.
In the morning, despite my wife being extremely unwell, I began feeding our baby and cleaning the cabin as much as I could. I called reception because there were no cleaning products provided in the cabin and I needed something to clean the floor. I received no response, likely because it was before 9am. With a tired and unsettled baby and a sick wife, I still managed to wash the dishes and clean the kitchen area as best as I could.
When my wife’s condition worsened, I went to the office to explain the situation. I apologised for not being able to complete the cleaning. The staff member told me, “No worries, we understand your family situation.” I thanked them and left.
However, two hours later, $200 was charged to my friend’s credit card who booked this place for us.
I wrote to management again explaining our circumstances, but I was told that “personal circumstances” are not considered in their decisions. The lack of empathy in that response was extremely disappointing. I made it clear that I did not object to paying a fair cleaning fee if necessary, but charging $200 after verbally acknowledging and understanding our situation felt unfair and insensitive.
This review is not about the money. It is about the lack of humanity and compassion shown toward a family with a sick mother and a one-year-old baby. We did everything we could with the limited supplies provided. There were no proper cleaning products or mop in the cabin, only a small number of tissues. Under the circumstances, I genuinely tried my best.
There were also safety concerns in Cabin 40. The rail of the top kitchen drawer (with cutlery) was faulty and collapsed onto my finger, causing injury. The gas cooktop ignitor did not work, and instead, a lighter was left in the cabin, which is unsafe in a small space, especially with a baby present.
We will be following up with Consumer Affairs Victoria regarding both the charge and the safety issues.
I truly hope management reflects on this experience. Families travel to holiday parks expecting a safe and supportive environment. Illness is not something people can control, but empathy and understanding are choices.
If the full $200 cleaning fee genuinely went to the cleaner, I can accept that. Otherwise, it feels like taking advantage of a family during a very difficult situation.
I sincerely hope no other family has to experience this.

**Review of My Stay at the Cabin**

I recently had the pleasure of staying at a charming cabin for four weeks, and my experience was nothing short of wonderful. From the moment I arrived, I felt welcomed and taken care of. The cabin itself was cozy and well-equipped with all the amenities I needed for a comfortable stay.

One of the standout aspects of my visit was the exceptional hospitality I received from the hosts. They went above and beyond to accommodate my schedule, allowing me to extend my stay for a few extra days to better suit my early starts and late nights. This flexibility made my trip much more enjoyable and stress-free.

The location of the cabin was also perfect, offering a peaceful retreat surrounded by nature, I found myself enjoying the serene environment, which was ideal for unwinding after a busy day.

Overall, my experience at the cabin was fantastic. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for a comfortable and accommodating stay, whether for a short getaway or an extended visit like mine. I can't thank the hosts enough for their kindness and understanding, and I look forward to returning in the future!

Also, as a family friendly place it might be a good idea to include kids plastic plates/bowls in the cabins?Or maybe other families' 3yos aren't as chaotic as ours and they don't worry about breakages as much as we do... 😅

Loved staying at Sale Caravan Park. We booked a powered site. Easy walk to CBD, always felt safe and most of all the amenities were spotlessly clean. Highly recommend staying here. Staff were friendly and helpful.

Really good location easy walking into town and lots of different walks facilities very clean sites neat and tidy and grounds well maintained and friendly managers really enjoyed our stay. Definitely be back.

This was our first time staying here with our Caravan. We have stayed at the Showgrounds in Sale a couple of times previously but we wanted to give this caravan park a go. We certainly were not disappointed. The manager was extremely obliging and let us choose our own site as it was a quiet weekend. We really enjoyed the quiet peaceful location yet still very close to town and will definitely be returning to stay here next time we are in town.

Great location and the staff are so friendly and thoughtful! The three families of our group really enjoyed our stay! The facilities were great and it was a lovely walk down to the jetty each morning. Thank you to the staff for being amazing! We will be back!

Long Jetty Caravan Park has a lot of potential, but unfortunately one major issue impacted our stay.

The biggest drawback is the camp kitchen. It is completely wide open with no doors at all. In bad or even mildly inclement weather, the kitchen becomes extremely difficult to use — it’s freezing, uncomfortable, and honestly feels unsafe. This is the first caravan park I’ve stayed at with a kitchen designed this way. Management really should address this, as a camp kitchen should be usable year-round and provide basic shelter from wind and rain.

On the positive side, the sites themselves are clean, well maintained, and generously sized. The grounds are kept nicely, and the reception staff are friendly, approachable, and open to feedback, which is appreciated.

Overall, this park has a lot of potential, but the poorly designed camp kitchen — by far the biggest issue — really lets it down. With improvements in this area, it could be a much better experience.

Very happy with the whole environment, staff,eliza and great service .they help me in booking at busy times by shuffling the rooms to accommodate me ..eliza is very helpful lady with big smile .
Cheers
Manish
Great spot to stay. Wonderful sites for our caravan. Would loved to have stayed longer.
A beautiful place.
Lovely caravan park, sand flies are the issue :(

Thank you for a superb few days.. The caravan sites were beautiful.. Lawns mowed like a golf course. The amenities were so clean you could nearly eat off them.. Finally the staff were extremely helpful and friendly.. Could not ask for better hosts.. Thank you 👍👍
The cabin had all that we needed & staff were friendly.  It was in a lovely location.  The cabin was nice and clean. We had a great stay.

Fantastic caravan park, very clean and tidy, it has everything you need, friendly staff and beautiful location! Highly recommended. Great price for value and very peaceful, we will definitely be back.
Thank you!
We only stayed one night in our campervan, would have loved to be staying longer, great park, great service, clean amenities and dog friendly
had lovley stay at the Long jetty carvan park 4 days in all ,grounds well kept and the amenitisas well staff amazing to happy to help .
Great park freindley staff well spread out with own spot would recomend powerd site

It was a good holiday.
Even though it was raining 🌧 we still enjoyed every minute of the holiday.

Beautiful grassy site. VERY large sites. Not crammed in here.
Staff very accommodating and helpful.
Peaceful
Quiet
Well equipped
Definitely recommend this park and the local restaurant “Rusty Gurnard”.

First impressions weren’t too fabulous. The site allocated to us was on the low part of the park and would have been lovely if the weather was more favourable. We were moved to a more suitable but not as picturesque area of the park. Upon talking with the park maintenance person who was so very welcoming and helpful, we learnt that the new owners have a lot of great plans for the park, as long as the council allows and works with them. Here’s hoping this happens as the park position is second to none.

Liz was very welcoming and helpful.
Lovely night stay, powered site in front of immaculate facilities......

Great place to stay.
There is plenty of room and bathrooms are new and clean.

We stayed for a couple of days here two weeks ago.

Elizabeth, the caretaker, was I'll do payment etc was done through the office window by phone. I thought this was very considerate and well done.

The grounds were clean and tidy and the ablution block was well maintained.

The Sale greyhounds are right next door if you're into that.

All in all we thoroughly enjoyed our time here.

Stayed Australia long wkend 2024. Disappointed the old caretaker shouted at and frightened my kids for playing lego in dirt. He went up to them stood over them and shouted stop digging my grass up.  My wife had to hav a word with him that if you hav an issue with chidren speak to the parents.
Showgrounds is 1.5klm from town, new and clean shower/toilet block. No camp kitchen or BBQ

A mate and I rode up to sale from moe with the  wind In the back it was a great ride the swap  meet was good plenty  of car stuff I have a broken leg from a year so we didn't  stay long and then we took the back roads home so all in all great day cheers n .crane

Great show ground to stay at. Stayed one night then came back for another.  Peaceful. Good amenities. Super obliging caretaker who looked after us despite arriving a bit after 5pm. Thanks for looking after us in this rain!

Great place. Very friendly and helpful staff. About my only tiny little grip is the noisy little truck they drive round all day. Perhaps get it serviced to make it quieter or maybe even an electric version. Not a complaint, just an observation. The blonde lady that greeted me and showed me my location was absolutely so courteous. A credit to this business. 10/10 from me.
Great place
Great place to stay, great staff & super clean amenities. Thanks for having us 😀
Not bad.  A touch pricey but new toilets.
Powered site was big enough for caravan and car to be side by side with room to spare. Amenities were plentiful, very clean and the touch of greener gave it a welcoming feel.
Easy access lovely grassed area Facilities very clean Staff friendly and helpful. Close to Greyhound Club lovely friendly  Club and Graze Restaurant beautiful meal
good location. Very clean facilities. Road noise.
Friendly lady staff member. Dinky Di Aussie. Nice spot.
Very bad experience as all the staff members were really inappropriate swearing at 10 year old children
Great venue and friendly staff. unfortunately was raining so couldn't experience everything. We will be back.
Loved our stay. Service was AMAZING.
Wonderful people who were very helpful & kind! Wonderful environment too. Loved everything about it... just go!

We have just spent 4 nights at this lovely peaceful camp-site.
The amenities on the site are clean and tidy.
Great setting with wonderful views. We had a very relaxing holiday.
I would highly recommend this beautiful site and look forward to visiting again sometime in the future.
Specially I want to thank you Justine for your amazing help.

Padma
29/4/2022

Nicely maintained holiday park located very close to Cathedral Range hiking tracks, lots of facilities if you have little kids.
We stayed a couple nights in the riverside cabin. The cabin was just sparkling clean and all the staff I dealt with there were very lovely and helpful. If you can, I highly recommend staying next to the river in either a cabin, a glamping tent or just camp there, it is just very peaceful.

We booked accommodation at Big Taggerty for our wedding guests for the weekend and couldn’t be happier with how everything turned out. Our guests absolutely loved it, we had so many compliments on the comfort, setup, and beautiful location. It was the perfect place for everyone to relax and settle in for the weekend.

Sally was wonderful to deal with, so helpful, responsive, and organised throughout all the back-and-forth in the lead-up. She made coordinating the bookings a breeze, and we’re so grateful to her and the team for making it all run so smoothly.

Highly recommend Big Taggerty for anyone looking for great group accommodation with warm, professional service!

We have been coming here for 10 years for Easter. The park facilities need an upgrade. The amenities block needs to be cleaned and checked a bit more often. It's a great location. The cabins are very tired looking and need an over haul.

Riverside. A decent size caravan park.  Pedal cart for hire for kids or families for a nominal amount to wander around the site.  The site hugs the river for a decent distance.  Keep the kids away from the office/lolly shop. European wasps are everywhere so keep s0me food covers handy and don't leave cans about.
Great for the kids, bit of R&M on guest amenities and cleaning facilities more than once a day would top it off.

Was meant to be a great catch up with family’s and friends over multiple unpowered sites but due to the long dry summer wasps have taken over nearly everywhere along the river camping spots and worst of all the unpowered areas, kids and adults have been bitten multiple times, including one person who has an EpiPen due to an allergic reaction to be has to be extremely careful around them. Cooking time or any time eating has to be done inside a car or inside, enclosed to tent due to being swarmed by hundreds of wasps setting up camp was extremely hard with wasps swarming all the time and getting into the tents, but luckily having plenty of bug spray on hand helped a little bit. Big 4 should have a warning up due to the dangerous amount of wasps right now, which is a safety hazard to people, especially if they don’t notify anybody of this. It’s rich paying over $80 a day in an unpowered site being surrounded by hundreds of wasps all day making a very unpleasant.

Fun little getaway with amazing staff!
We will definitely be back by the river👏
Fantastic

Amazing place ! Beautiful staff , Grace and Darby were so helpful and lovely .
Our cabin was spacious and in a perfect spot overlooking the river .
Will definitely be back !

Love camping here by the river, amenities are cleaned regularly and staff are lovely. We love the pool with the shade sail and the jumping pillow. We also love the go karts. Nice and close to Melbourne for an easy long weekend getaway.
Great place if you have dogs.  Clean and warm amenities
Grassed areas to park on. Ablutions modern. Friendly caretakers. Not far from main shops. Service station  over the road. Greyhound Race Club in same park and very close for meals. Would return!
We had a drive through caravan site with a slab. The park is easy walking into town. We would stay again when going to a show at the Wedge. Had a great weekend.

We have stayed several times at Sale caravan park love the convenience of the position on our way from NSW to Melbourne
The park is friendly clean  and basic but well maintained and has friendly staff would recommend it as a overnight stay
A good spot, close to shops and the Port of Sale and a great walk to the Historic Swing Bridge.
Manager Steve was most obliging and helpful.  There is room to improve the amenities but the camp kitchen was okay and the grounds themselves nice.  Large sites.
Beautiful park with multiple spotless amenities. Stayed in a room with my 2 dogs and was perfect. Thank you.

My Husband and I are travelling from Victoria to Queensland and stayed at Sale caravan park for an overnight stay. Liz and Steve keep the park and amenities lovely and clean. If we are ever in the area again we would stay there longer. Many thanks to you both. Brad & Trina

I can highly recommend this beautiful caravan park . The grounds , facilities & amenities were excellent. Staff very friendly and helpful
